block-level machine translation prototype · page 001

• The author and publisher cannot be held responsible for any results or consequences arising from the use of the contents of this book and attached disk.
• The software included in the appendix disk is generally freeware that can be used and distributed without cost, with each author retaining copyright. For details,
refer to the documentation of each software.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 002

block-level machine translation prototype · page 003

Preface

Welcome to the world of NH!

NetHack—NetHack—is this game familiar to you? One of the free software programs. To be honest, it's not a particularly mainstream game.

If playing computer games is one of your hobbies, then even if you do not know the details, you have probably heard of Rogue at least once or twice. NetHack is
a game that evolved from Rogue.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 004

NetHack the RPG—with FMP & MAG loader—

Table of Contents

Disk Attachment Method 10

Requirements Items 10
Install 11
Start Up! Startup Method 16
More Info. Reference 18

Characters 23

• Parameter Ability 23
• Class Class 26
• Be Invoked NetHack Start 39

Commands Commands 43

block-level machine translation prototype · page 005

NetHack began offering Rogue as a service during its trial period, but many lines were filled with Rogue players, and there were even users who incurred phone
bills exceeding ten thousand yen, leading to the service being discontinued, according to a well-known story.

Why are they so interesting? Unlike the numerous RPGs in the world, the characters in "Rogue" or "NetHack" lack flavor and personality. There are no cute girl
characters, and it is far from a world with animations or conversations via PCM.

In most RPGs, players are often represented as 2-head-height characters. However, in this world, players are simply denoted by @. Various monsters and
dungeon contents are displayed using characters that can be shown on a computer——such as "o" and "r".

The game itself is quite illogical. No matter how well you progress, there can be small triggers that easily lead to game over. It might be a game that makes
players want to give up, especially for players accustomed to balanced RPGs.

However, I still find myself playing "one more time..." It's a game with a mysterious charm. Before you know it, you will see yourself in @ and goblins in o.

Once you are hooked on this game, you will end up playing NetHack all day long on a sunny holiday... just like the authors!

Good luck, and happy hacking!

block-level machine translation prototype · page 006

block-level machine translation prototype · page 007

NETHACK GENERAL PUBLIC LICENSE
(Copyright 1989 M. Stephenson)
(Based on the BISON general public license,
copyright 1988 Richard M. Stallman)
Everyone is permitted to copy and distribute verbatim copies of this license,
but changing it is not allowed. You can also use this wording to make the terms
for other programs.

The license agreements of most software companies keep you at the mercy of
those companies. By contrast, our general public license is intended to give
everyone the right to share NetHack. To make sure that you get the rights we
want you to have, we need to make restrictions that forbid anyone to deny you
these rights or to ask you to surrender the rights. Hence this license
agreement.

Specifically, we want to make sure that you have the right to give away copies
of NetHack, that you receive source code or else can get it if you want it, that
you can change NetHack or use pieces of it in new free programs, and that you
know you can do these things.

To make sure that everyone has such rights, we have to forbid you to deprive
anyone else of these rights. For example, if you distribute copies of NetHack,
you must give the recipients all the rights that you have. You must make sure
that they, too, receive or can get the source code. And you must tell them their
rights.

Also, for our own protection, we must make certain that everyone finds out that
there is no warranty for NetHack. If NetHack is modified by someone else and
passed on, we want its recipients to know that what they have is not what we
distributed.

Therefore we (Mike Stephenson and other holders of NetHack copyrights) make
the following terms which say what you must do to be allowed to distribute or
change NetHack.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 008

COPYING POLICIES

1. You may copy and distribute verbatim copies of NetHack source code as you
receive it, in any medium, provided that you keep intact the notices on all files
that refer to copyrights, to this License Agreement, and to the absence of any
warranty; and give any other recipients of the NetHack program a copy of this
License Agreement along with the program.

2. You may modify your copy or copies of NetHack or any portion of it, and copy
and distribute such modifications under the terms of Paragraph 1 above
(including distributing this License Agreement), provided that you also do the
following:

a) cause the modified files to carry prominent notices stating that you changed
the files and the date of any change; and
b) cause the whole of any work that you distribute or publish, that in whole or in
part contains or is a derivative of NetHack or any part thereof, to be licensed at
no charge to all third parties on terms identical to those contained in this
License Agreement (except that you may choose to grant more extensive
warranty protection to some or all third parties, at your option)
c) You may charge a distribution fee for the physical act of transferring a copy,
and you may at your option offer warranty protection in exchange for a fee.

3. You may copy and distribute NetHack (or a portion or derivative of it, under
Paragraph 2) in object code or executable form under the terms of Paragraphs 1
and 2 above provided that you also do one of the following:

a) accompany it with the complete machine-readable source code, which must
be distributed under the terms of Paragraphs 1 and 2 above; or,
b) accompany it with full information as to how to obtain the complete
machine-readable source code from an appropriate archive site. (This
alternative is allowed only for noncommercial distribution.)

For these purposes, complete source code means either the full source

block-level machine translation prototype · page 009

distribution as originally released over Usenet or updated copies of the files in
this distribution used to create the object code or executable.

4. You may not copy, sublicense, distribute or transfer NetHack except as
expressly provided under this License Agreement. Any attempt otherwise to
copy, sublicense, distribute or transfer NetHack is void and your rights to use
the program under this License agreement shall be automatically terminated.
However, parties who have received computer software programs from you
with this License Agreement will not have their licenses terminated so long as
such parties remain in full compliance.

Stated plainly: You are prohibited by the terms of this License Agreement from
using NetHack for gainful purposes. You are permitted to modify NetHack, or
otherwise use parts of NetHack, provided that you comply with the conditions
specified above; in particular, your modified NetHack or program containing
parts of NetHack must remain freely available as provided in this License
Agreement. In other words, go ahead and share NetHack, but don't try to stop
anyone else from sharing it farther.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 010

NetHack General Public License (Translation)
(Copyright 1989, 1990 M. Stephenson)

(Based on the Bison General Public License:
Copyright 1988 Richard M Stallman)
This license can be copied and distributed by anyone. However, it cannot be
modified. Additionally, the expressions in this license can be used to create
conditions for other programs.

Most software company license agreements are unilaterally decided by those
companies. In contrast, our General Public License aims to give everyone the
right to share NetHack. To ensure you can indeed obtain these rights, it is
necessary to prohibit anyone from denying your rights or demanding waiver of
rights.

We particularly want to ensure that you are aware of your rights regarding
copies of NetHack, obtaining the source code, modifying NetHack or part of it
for use in new free software, and the fact that you can do these things.

To ensure that everyone can truly possess these rights, we must prohibit you
from taking these rights away from others. For example, if you distribute
copies of NetHack, you must give all your rights to the recipient. They must be
able to obtain the source code just like you. And you must teach them their
rights.

We also need to make it clear to everyone that there are no guarantees
regarding protecting ourselves in NetHack. We want those who obtain a
corrected and distributed version to be able to recognize that it is not our
distributed version.

Therefore, we (Mike Stephenson and other NetHack copyright holders) require
you to obtain permission for distributing or modifying NetHack.
Omitted, see English reference

block-level machine translation prototype · page 011

Usage of Attached Disk

Bisk

Disk Preparation: The NetHack included in this book has various
tricks implemented, so while it's not impossible to play using a
floppy disk, it's practically impossible. A hard disk or EMS would
be preferable. Let's explain the preparation for playing NetHack
first.

Requirements Necessary Items
Items to prepare for playing the NetHack included on the attached disk.
First, let's list the items that are absolutely necessary.

First, a PC-9801 VF or later with at least 640 KB of main memory is absolutely
necessary. In other words, any series of PC-9801 with a CPU of V30 or higher,
and PC-286/386/486 series would suffice. Machines released within the last 2
to 3 years should be adequate.

And, MS-DOS version 3.1 or later is required.
Next, let's list the items we'd like to have. First, a hard disk. The disks attached
to this book contain background music and image CGs for NetHack, so using
floppy disks would require two disks.

As long as you have additional memory for EMS and the corresponding EMS
driver, you'll be happy. However, even without them, NetHack will still run,
though performance will be poor, making it essentially unplayable in practice.
By the way, it's also a good idea to prepare an English-Japanese dictionary and an ample supply of

10

Disk—Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 012

Time. These two might be essential for completing NetHack.

Install

Installation to Floppy Disk
As I have mentioned before, playing NetHack on a computer with only a floppy
disk is quite difficult. While it is difficult, it is not impossible, so let me explain
the installation procedure for the floppy disk.

First, please prepare two floppy disks with 2 HD each.
Drive A insert MS-DOS system disk, Drive B insert empty disk and boot the
computer. When MS-DOS system boots up...

A>

from this state,
FORMAT B: /M /S
Type and press the key.
Then, the disk drive starts formatting the disk in B drive. Once this process is
finished, remove the disk from B drive. We'll call this disk the "boot disk," so
it's a good idea to put a label with that written on it. Now, put another disk into
B drive and...

A>

... state from,
FORMAT B: /M

Install—Install

11

block-level machine translation prototype · page 013

Type that in and press the key. The disk drive will move again and format the
disk in drive B. When the process finishes, remove the disk from drive B and
put a label on it saying "Data Disk."

Next, remove the MS-DOS system disk from drive A and set the 'boot disk'
instead, then reset and reboot the computer.

And once MS-DOS is booted, set the attached disk to drive B. Then, proceed
again...
A>
... state from,
B: INSTFD B A
Type and press key ①.

Disk—Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 014

Then, various messages will be displayed on the screen, essentially indicating
that the NetHack program is being expanded onto the "boot disk." If instructed
to change disks midway through, please put the "data disk" into drive A. The
CG and other data will be expanded onto the "data disk."

If EMS is usable, it's better to copy the EMS driver you usually use to the "boot
disk" and set it up in CONFIG.SYS. While the specifics would require individual
explanation, we will omit them here.

Installation on Notebook Computer (RAM Drive)
On notebook computers, you can play NetHack somewhat comfortably if you
use the RAM drive properly (it's better to install it on the hard disk if it's
built-in). The basic installation method is the same as for floppy disks, but
there are still several things to keep in mind.

Before starting the installation, you need to save the contents of the RAM
drive. If you don't mind losing the contents of the RAM drive, you can skip this
step. However, it's better to boot while pressing the HELP key to display the 98
Note menu, then select "RAM Drive → Floppy Disk Copy" and transfer the
contents of the RAM drive to a single floppy disk.

Next, set the MS-DOS system disk in the floppy drive and select "Auto Mode"
to boot. After booting, set a new floppy disk in the floppy drive and...

A>

... state from,
FORMAT B: /M /S
Type and press the key.
Install——Install

13

block-level machine translation prototype · page 015

Then the floppy disk is formatted. So, after processing ends, remove the disk
and put a label saying "boot disk" on it. Now, put another disk into the floppy
drive....

A>

from this state,
FORMAT B: /M
Type and enter, then press the ④ key. This will format the disk again, so
remove the disk when the process finishes and label it as "data disk".

Next, copy the contents of the attached disk to the RAM drive. Insert this
book's floppy disk into the floppy drive and press the (HELP) key while
resetting. In the 98 Note Menu, select "Floppy Disk → RAM Drive."

14

Disk——Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 016

Select "Copy." When the copy is finished, choose "Mode Settings" in the 98
Note Menu, and set "System Boot Device I" to "Standard," "System Boot Device
II" to "FD," and "First Drive" to "FD." After setting, insert the boot disk into the
floppy drive and press the (ESC) key twice to exit the 98 Note Menu. This
should allow the floppy drive to become drive A and boot up. Then proceed
again ....

A>

from this state,
B: INSTFD B A

Type and enter (press key ④).

Then, various messages will be displayed on the screen, essentially indicating
that the NetHack program is being expanded onto the "boot disk". If instructed
to change disks halfway through, please insert the "data disk" into the floppy
drive. Data such as CG will be expanded onto the "data disk".

If EMS is usable, it's better to copy the EMS driver you usually use to the "boot
disk" and set it up in CONFIG.SYS. While the specifics would require individual
explanation, we will omit them in this book.

■ Installation on Hard Disk
If you want to install NetHack on a hard disk, ensure there is at least 3MB of
free space.
Install first by booting MS-DOS, setting the attached disk to the floppy disk
drive, and moving to that drive. Specifically, if the drive with the floppy disk
inserted is B drive, ...

A>

from this state...
Install—Installation

15

block-level machine translation prototype · page 017

B:

Type and press the key. And...
B>

if you end up in this state...
INSTHD B A

Type and press enter. By the way, A refers to the installed hard disk. It might
vary by user, with A and B being hard disks and C and D being floppy disks. If
that's the case, please use appropriate drive names instead.

After that, follow the instructions displayed on the screen until installation is complete.

Start Up! Startup Method
If installed on a floppy disk, insert the "boot disk" into drive A and the "data
disk" into drive B, then reset the computer to boot from them. After a while,
the menu screen will appear along with the message "Welcome to NetHack."
Note that if you do not set SW 3-4 to OFF (LCD reverse display) under "System
Setup" → "Dip Switch Settings," the character CG may be unviewable. In any
case, be prepared for the screen to be difficult to see on monochrome LCD
displays. Here, enter your player name, your cat's name, your favorite fruit,
etc., use the cursor keys to move the cursor and select your class, then press
the key to start the game.

After ending the game, use the following command to pickup when restarting.

NTHK

16

Disk—Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 018

If you installed the RAM drive using a notebook computer, set the boot disk on
the floppy drive and start the computer while pressing the (HELP) key. Choose
Auto mode from the 98 Notebook menu. When the message "Welcome to
NetHack" appears along with the menu screen, reinsert the data disk on the
floppy drive, enter your name and other information, choose your class, and
press the (J) key to start the game.

To restart after quitting, use the following command to stand up.
NTHK (J)

If you have installed it on a hard disk, first boot up the computer and then
move to the installation destination. If you installed NetHack on drive B...

A>

from this state...
B: (J)
Enter ... (if you installed it on the A drive, this is not needed). Next,

CD games & nethack (J)

Enter ... to move to the directory.
NTHKPATH (J)
afterwards, finally...
NTHK (J)

Start Up! — How to Start

17

block-level machine translation prototype · page 019

When you input ..., the message "Welcome to NetHack" appears along with the
menu screen. Input the player's and pet's names, choose a profession, and
press N to start the game.
restart is still done with the following command.

NTHK N

Note that NetHack requires a free memory area of at least 450 KB to run. If you
are using it on a hard disk, it is recommended to check using tools like
CHKDSK.EXE or VMAP.COM. When using CHKDSK.EXE, the available memory
displayed last is the free area. While NetHack can be played if this number is
450000 or more, it is preferable to have around 520 KB of free area. If there is
not enough, please remove the Japanese FEP or take other measures to
address the issue. The specific steps vary depending on the reader's
environment and cannot be explained generally, so they will be omitted.

More Info. For reference
This subsequent explanation is merely for reference, so you can skip it without
any problem. However, it might be useful information for those who
understand it.

■ About NetHack. CNF
NetHack.cnf is a file that records the environment for running NetHack. The
attached disk for this book includes an "NetHack Startup Menu Program," so
there is no need to modify NETHACK.cnf. However, you can set NETHACK.cnf
appropriately so that you can start NetHack directly without going through the
startup menu.

18

Disk——Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 020

For example, my NETHACK.CNF is as follows.
OPTIONS=!pickup,number_pad,time OPTIONS=98_BIOS,rawio,98graphics
OPTIONS=name:Avelna-R,female,
dogname:Suezow,catname:Patty,fruit:satuma
HACKDIR=A:¥GAMES¥NETHACK LEVELS=A:¥GAMES¥NETHACK¥BONES
SAVE=A:¥GAMES¥NETHACK¥BONES;n

In this case, the character's name is Avelna, the class is Rouge, and the
character is female. The dog's name is Suezow, the cat's name is Patty, and
the favorite fruit is satuma (orange). Additionally, it states that PC-9800
series-specific functions are utilized, and NetHack is installed in the A drive
directory "¥GAMES¥NETHACK".

Typically, the settings for characters or pets would be corrected on the third
line. The 'R' after the name represents the class, which corresponds to the
following:
AArcheologistBBarbarianCCavemanEElf
HHealerKKnightPPriestRRogue
SSamuraiTTouristVValkyrieWWizard

Regarding fruits, note that melons, oranges, bananas, apples, and pears
already exist in the game, so you need to specify other types of fruits.

For note laptops and other models without a numeric keypad, removing the
text "98_BIOS" on the second line and "number_pad" on the first line can be
helpful. This allows you to use b, j, n, h, l, y, k, u instead of the numeric keypad
for character movement and direction specification. If you don't do this, you'll
need to press (NUM) lock each time.

Be sure to note the directory specification in the last three lines. This should be
the drive where NetHack is actually installed.

More Info.—For Reference

19

block-level machine translation prototype · page 021

NetHack will not start if the cartridges are inserted incorrectly.

NetHack. Even after properly setting up CNF, you need to move to the
directory first...
NETHACK
If you input that, it should start the game immediately.

About Boot Options
Direct start method. If you want to play with friends by starting directly, or if you want to
change the name occasionally to switch up the mood, do so at startup time....

NETHACK -uYositune

Enter it like this. The letters after -u become the character's name. Similarly, if
you want to try a different class……
NetHack - English

Type that and it will work. Enter capital letters for your class initials in the
"English" part.

■ Starting in Immortal Mode
NetHack is a very difficult game. Beginners will likely raise dozens of
tombstones before long. That's where the invincibility mode (discovery-mode)
comes in. To play in invincibility mode...

NETHACK -X

Start it this way. When your hit points reach 0 and you die, the game will ask
whether you really died; enter 'n' there and you can revive.

Note: renaming, choosing a class, and specifying invincibility mode should be done simultaneously.

20

Disk——Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 022

You can do that. If you want to play as an immortal mode priest named Tike...

NETHACK -uTike -P -X

In this way, if you can launch it, that would be good.

About FMP and MAG
This book's attached disk includes CG images in addition to the original
NetHack, and we have used SAM's "MAG Loader" free software to realize the
display of background CG images. MAG Loader is a separate program from
NetHack, so you can view the CG images individually, but for that, you need to
run...

File Name (□)
Enter (make sure the path to MAG.EXE exists and pay attention to the file
name path).
File names of each CG are as follows.
ArchelogistNTHK_APriestNTHK_P
BarbarianNTHK_BRougeNTHK_R
Cave-manNTHK_CSamuraiNTHK_S
ElfNTHK_ETouristNTHK_T
HealerNTHK_HVarklieNTHK_V
KnightNTHK_KWizardNTHK_W

Background music (BGM) is also specially prepared for this book, and we have
used Mr. Guu's "FMP" free software to perform it. As is natural, an FM sound
source board is required for performance. You just need a machine that has an
internal sound source board or one with an external sound source board
installed.
Title back and background music (BGM) prepared in 3 pieces are played along
with the CG display. To listen to these songs individually, you need to follow
these steps.

More Info.—For Reference

21

block-level machine translation prototype · page 023

First, have FMP run constantly. From the MS-DOS command line, input the
following:

FMP's theme music plays, and this means the background music is now ready
to be played. To play the music here, do the following:

File names for each tune are as follows.
Title Back for Chaotic Characters
Neutral Character Title Back
Title Back for Low-Level Characters
Background Music 1
Background Music 2

Background Music 3 (for Speakboard)

Background Music 3 is only fully supported by a somewhat special FM sound
board called Speakboard, so it becomes an incomplete song on regular FM
sound boards. I understand it might be a bit difficult to listen to, but please
bear with it.
Note that some programs allow you to specify a level via the menu. Here is...

Type and press Enter to launch. After launching, specify the file name using
cursor movement and ① key.

22

Disk——Usage of Attached Disk

block-level machine translation prototype · page 024

In the world of NetHack, the characters you send into the game
are set with various parameters and occupations, allowing for
distinct personalities to be discerned among them.

Parameter Ability Values

Information on parameters necessary to judge a character's
abilities will be shown here. These are mainly displayed in the
two lines at the bottom of the NetHack status screen.

St : Strength

Strength Constitution

This parameter becomes greater the more rugged and strong one is. It
determines the damage dealt with weapons and how many items one can
carry in the Yendorian dungeon.
Warrior characters need this parameter to be outstanding above all else.
Additionally, magic-user characters also need some strength to cast spells.

Dx : Dexterity

Dexterity Agility, nimbleness

Dexterity is necessary for hitting rates in combat and for dexterous tasks
such as picking locks and disarming traps.
Thieves need to be dexterous. The fate of a thief who is not dexterous... is
tragic.

Parameter——Ability Values

23

block-level machine translation prototype · page 025

Co : Constitution

Constitution Vitality

Constitution is highly beneficial for enduring physical exhaustion and
avoiding damage from poison. It also has a significant impact on the increase
in hit points when leveling up.
As a warrior, one should expect a high Constitution. Even if you excel in
strength, a weak warrior would be nothing but a laughing matter.

In : Intelligence

Intelligence

Intelligence affects the ability to cast spells. When reading a magic book to
learn a spell, an Intelligence value is required. Why can an illiterate human
read a magic book?
Of course, a great deal of intelligence is required for those who practice
magic.

Wi : Wisdom

Wisdom Intelligence

Many are related to negotiations with the gods. The gods prefer wiser beings.
Additionally, to store the energy required for magic, one must be much wiser
than a usual human.
Priests (or Priestesses) are favored by the gods due to their wisdom.

Ch : Charisma

Charisma Appeal

The presence or absence of charisma will directly affect negotiations with
storekeepers who run shops in the Yendorian dungeon. They do not have
fixed prices for their goods; instead, they tend to sell items at lower prices if
the buyer is attractive to them.
By the way, charisma refers not only to physical appearance but also to the
overall appeal as a human (or an elf, or possibly other monsters). There are
no elements of appearance that are completely zero.

24

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 026

The six parameters are usually represented within the range of 3 to 18.
However, Strength can exceed 18, in which case it is written as "18/xx".
Additionally, Strength can increase to 25 or higher by obtaining a certain item.

Let's consider a rogue with Strength 14, Dexterity 18, Constitution 13,
Intelligence 10, Wisdom 8, and Charisma 7. Let's think about this rogue's
personality based on these six parameters.

First, it has a moderate strength and endurance. The dexterity of the hands
and the speed of the feet are first-rate. However, regarding intelligence, it can
read at least some text. The judgment of the situation is not necessarily
accurate, but it never takes extremely unconventional actions. If you imagine a
person who is slightly unpleasant to be around and has made some enemies,
you might consider yourself a seasoned NetHack player.

Attributes are also present for characters in NetHack. Similar to
GOOD/NEUTRAL/EVIL in Wizardry, they refer to the personality of the character,
consisting of LAWFUL/NEUTRAL/CHAOTIC.
LAWFUL means a character who follows social order, while CHAOTIC refers to a
character who disregards social order and follows their own path (of course, if
their ideas align with societal ideals, they will follow those instead. They are not
simply misfits). NEUTRAL indicates a character who is between these two
extremes. Of course, this attribute is not fixed from birth until death; it can
change based on the character's actions.

Attributes Lawful Neutral Chaotic Social Adaptation Type Middle
Type Anti-Social, My-Pace Type

Parameter—Ability Values

25

block-level machine translation prototype · page 027

However, there are things that more eloquently tell of a character's nature
beyond these parameters and attributes. That thing is the class (occupation).
NetHack offers 12 different professions. Some may boast of their strength,
while others excel in magic. Perhaps some are captivated by ancient treasures.
In this way, personalize your character. They are never mere game pieces but
rather characters born into the world of NetHack.

Class Profession

NetHack offers 12 playable classes. Each has advantages and
disadvantages, and it's difficult to say which is best. However,
you can clear the game with any class, so please play patiently.

Of course, there are classes suited for beginners and those that are not. The
author recommends the Barbarian or Valkyrie for beginners. Both of these
classes meet the necessary condition of having many hit points to survive in
NetHack. Additionally, they both dislike spells, which means they can avoid
relying on troublesome magic.

Reverse recommendations would be archaeologists and tourists. While the
former might be an interesting character for veteran players, they could be a
bit tough for beginners who are not yet familiar with the NetHack world.
Tourists are not necessarily for advanced players. In terms of just enjoying the
game, they might be the most fun class, but they are likely too challenging for
beginners.

Additionally, once you have gotten somewhat accustomed to NetHack, I would
like you to try playing as a Rogue, Priest, or Mage. By the way, the class that
the recent author enjoys the most is the Rogue.

26

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 028

Archeologist

Archaeologist: Archaeologist

This archaeologist is dressed somewhat like a scholar and somewhat like an
adventurer. An archaeologist who skillfully wields a long whip and wears a
leather hat is likely based on someone you know from a famous movie!

Archaeologists venture into the dungeon of
Yendor in search of ancient treasures rumored to
be hidden there. Of course, they can move
stealthily through the dungeon to seek out
treasures—despite traps that might trigger,
causing rolling boulders (whether such traps
exist in Yendor's dungeon is another matter).

Archaeologists are knowledgeable about gems
due to their profession. They can identify any
gem at a glance—however, this does not apply
to those enchanted by magic.

They also excel in survival skills and possess a
special item that allows them to can monsters for
food (a rare treasure rarely found even in
Yendor's dungeon). Thanks to this tool,
archaeologists can stockpile food and are thus
immune to starvation. However, this tool is very
heavy, so it is reckless for those with low
strength to carry it.

Attributes: Rowlful Direct Combat: △ Magic: △ God: Quetzalcotl

Class -- profession

27

block-level machine translation prototype · page 029

Barbarian

Barbarian: Savage

Barbarian is a typical warrior as we imagine.
Armed with sturdy armor that provides safety
commensurate with its weight, and wielding a
longsword to strike down everything.

They tend to fight more directly than they think.
Generally, their muscle reactions are much
faster than their brain's thinking. They boast of
abnormally strong bodies, but they are amateurs
when it comes to magic. If you find a spellbook
in Yendor's dungeon, it would be foolish to touch
it. There is nothing to lose by not reading it, and
you could sell it for a few coins at a shop.

They venture into Yendor's dungeon in search of
honor, which they cannot obtain by any other
means. Their great strength is surely sufficient
to break through the traps and monsters lurking
in the dungeon.

Due to their excessive stamina, no poison can
affect their body.

Attributes: Chaotic

28

Direct combat:◎

Magic: ×

Deity: Morrigan

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 030

Caveman / Cave-Woman

Cave Man: Primitive

From afar, they seek something—perhaps a value system unknown to us—and
challenge the dungeon of Yendor. These are the Cave Men (or Cave Women).

Their agility in running through fields and climbing hills clearly suits warriors,
but their equipment is somewhat lacking. The cudgels they used in the fields
would be considered inadequate weapons in Yendor's dungeon. Additionally,
their armor consists of the skins of animals they hunted, which is quite poor
quality as armor. Therefore, after entering the dungeon, Keubman will need to
search for new weapons and armor.

They carry a bow for hunting prey in
one hand. They are skilled warriors and
also hunters with arms equal to or
better than those of elves.

However, it may be hard to believe, but
the god they worship belongs to the
side of order. At first glance, they might
appear savage. But that is simply
because the cave men are crude.

Attributes: Lawful

Direct combat:○

Magic: ×

God: Anu

Class -- profession

29

block-level machine translation prototype · page 031

Elf

Elf: Elf

They are called elves in human legends, but in
reality, they are closer to subhumans. There are
several races, and there are various differences
depending on the race. Their bodies are
generally about one size smaller than humans
and not particularly sturdy. And because they
have pointed ears and almond-shaped
characteristic eyes, those who do not know of
elves would likely see them as small, strange
humans.

This, one could say, is the notable ability of the
elf. The eyes of the elves are special. Human
eyes can see transparent objects that are
invisible to them.

Elves are also generally skilled archers.
Furthermore, this strong eyesight makes it easy
for them to spot hidden doors, passages, and
traps. The value of these elf-specific abilities in
Yendor's dungeon can be understood only by
actually going to Yendor's dungeon.

Elves are said to head into Yendor's dungeon out
of their rare curiosity. When exploring, they
often carry beautiful elven-made short swords
and wear light but sturdy chain mail. Of course,
as natural-born hunters, they would never forget
bows and arrows.

Attributes: Lawful

30

Direct Combat: △

Magic: △ God: Solonor Thelandira

Characters——Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 032

Healer

Healer: Healer

Healers can be considered quite a special
existence as adventurers. In terms of
personality, they are close to priests, possessing
a large number of healing potions and books of
healing spells. However, their healing power is
not reliant on divine intervention but is mostly
achieved through their own means such as
medicine and magic. They venture into the
depths of the dungeon in Yendor to obtain spirit
herbs.

However, when it comes to combat, they can be
considered complete beginners like the priests.
When venturing into the dungeon, the weapons
they carry are merely a few daggers. And the
armor they wear consists of nothing but gloves
to prevent their skin from being affected by
drugs.

However, all Healers have a special stethoscope
that allows them to discern monsters' abilities at
a glance. This will provide them with much
wisdom in the Yendorian dungeon. It is said that
if used correctly, this stethoscope can help them
fight monsters by using potions wisely to turn
the battle in their favor.

And due to handling too many chemicals, they
have developed resistance to all toxins.

Attributes: Lawful

Direct Combat: ×

Magic:○

Deity: Athena

Class -- profession

31

block-level machine translation prototype · page 033

Knight

Knight: Knight

Knight is a proud warrior clad in various armor.
Their steed cannot enter the dungeons of
Yendor, but their compassionate noble spirit can
impress even the merchants operating stores in
the dungeons of Yendor, and they may receive
some discounts.

While they may not be as wise as priests,
knights also possess enough wisdom to receive
divine favor. In other words, one could say that
knights are warriors-priests. Although they
cannot distinguish blessings from curses, only
knights and priests can use their god's power to
annihilate living beings that have been born into
the realm of death.

Knights wear many pieces of armor, such as
chain mail, dragonmail, and helms, so their
starting defense at entering the dungeon is
among the highest of the 12 professions.

However, unfortunately, the strength and
dexterity of the knight, which are necessary for
combat, are not particularly noteworthy. One
could say they are somewhat lacking.

In case of need, pray to the gods for their aid.

Attributes: Rowlf Direct Combat: ○ Magic: × God: Lugh

32

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 034

Priest / Priestess

Priest: Priest

Priests undertake the challenge of entering
Yendor's dungeon to elevate their faith through
trials. Due to their nature, their prayers are
more likely to be heard by the gods compared to
any other human or elf. They also possess
considerable skill in magic—particularly in
healing. Among other things, priests alone are
allowed to carry two magical books when they
enter the dungeon.

They possess the ability to discern blessings and
curses as a result of their training. This ability is
very effective for surviving in the dungeons of
Yendor. There is nothing more pitiful than the
sight of adventurers clinging to cursed weapons
and armor. Priests will not wear cursed items.
This can be said to be the greatest blessing
bestowed upon priests. Additionally, like knights,
priests have the power to annihilate cursed
creatures that possess the origin of life in the
realm of death through divine power.

However, they do not possess any notable
abilities in combat. But their kicks have enough
power to easily break treasure chests, and at
times, a single strike can slay a monster. This
might also be due to the favor of the gods.

Attributes: Neutral

Direct Combat: △

Magic:○

Class -- profession

God: Crom

33

block-level machine translation prototype · page 035

Rouge

Rogue: Thief

Original "Rouge" means something like "rascal."
However, due to their profession, they are often
called thieves. They carry tools for picking locks
and can easily open treasure chests and coffins.
Of course, door keys are practically non-existent
in front of rogues. They go to the dungeon to
hone their thieving skills and to obtain the
legendary treasures said to be hidden in
Yendor's dungeon.

They also have the skill to walk without making
any noise as a result of their training. Even in a
room of Yendor's dungeon where sleeping
monsters are present, it is easy for them to pass
by without awakening the monsters.

Thieves prefer light daggers as their main
weapon and tend to wear leather armor to avoid
noisy metal armor. They are also skilled in
handling poison. Many of the darts they throw
are coated with poison.

Attributes: Chaotic

34

Direct combat: △

Magic: △

God: Rat God

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 036

Samurai

Samurai: Samurai

Serve only their lord——a samurai is one who
stands by their lord's side——and they believe
the spirit of their sword has possessed their
soul… In the remote island nation of the far east,
these solitary warriors are samurai. They are
knights of the far east, proud and noble. They
carry two katana, which are the large and small
swords of a samurai, and shuriken, star-shaped
throwing knives.

They challenge Yendor's dungeon for what
reason? No one but themselves will ever know
the truth. The samurai carries a firm resolve
within their heart and heads towards the
dungeon to uphold that resolve.

They carry several fortune cookies, which are
their favorite food. Also, like monks, samurai
have powerful kicking power. If the monsters are
on lower levels, they can be easily defeated with
their kicks rather than using a katana.

Attributes: Lawful

Direct combat:○

Magic: △ God: Amaterasu Omikami

Class -- Occupation

35

block-level machine translation prototype · page 037

Tourist

Tourist: Traveler

Tourists are just ordinary humans. They have no
notable abilities. So why did they come to this
terrifying dungeon? There is no room for debate.
They have come to the famous Yendor dungeon
for tourism!

Their main weapon is darts, but their cameras
can be considered their true weapons. The flash
from these expensive cameras blinds monsters
that haven't adapted to flashes in the dark
dungeon.

Also, when you see the Hawaiian shirts they are
wearing—this is the only armor of the
tourists—you must be careful, as the
shopkeepers intend to trick them into buying
things. Furthermore, they have credit cards, but
cash payment is the only accepted method in
Yendor's dungeon. However, there is no need to
worry about the tourists' money. They have a lot
of cash on them. Probably, they plan to buy
souvenirs in the dungeon as well.

They have magic mapping scrolls up to the map
of the dungeon. If for some reason they were to
conquer the dungeon, it would undoubtedly be a
defeat for Yendor.

Attributes: Neutral

36

Direct Combat: ×

Magic: × God: Chih Sung-tzu

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 038

Varkyrie

Valkyrie: Warrior

Valkyrie is a female warrior—originally referring
to 12 goddesses serving the supreme god Odin
in the north, but now generally means a female
warrior. In some places, she is also called
Valkyrie.

They possess combat power on par with or
exceeding that of barbarians. Unlike the crude
strength of barbarians, they would slice
monsters with supple muscles. And like the
barbarians, they also boast sturdy bodies that
are not easily felled by monsters' claws and
fangs.

Warcylere is famous for bringing very few items
into Yendor's dungeon. First, a longsword and a
shortsword each. A small shield boasting
excellent defensive power through magic. And
there is only one piece of food. She is not even
wearing any armor.

They seem able to withstand cold even without
clothing, perhaps because they come from cold
regions. Whether by magic or nature, it doesn't
matter to them.

Attributes: Chaotic

Direct Combat:○

Magic: △

God: Loki

Class -- profession

37

block-level machine translation prototype · page 039

Wizard

Wizard: Mage

A human skilled in magic. They are barely
competent in close combat, and their preferred
weapons are mere daggers. While they have
enough stamina to cast spells, their physique is
far from robust due to a life of continuous
magical research.

Adventurers carry several magic items to the
dungeon. A spellbook, a magic wand, and two
magic rings.
If they cannot skillfully use these items, it will be
impossible for them to survive the dungeon of
Yendor.
However, their diverse magical techniques more
than make up for the weakness in close combat.
You should buy them if you find magical books in
the dungeon shops, even if it means selling all
your possessions. They need to survive by
acquiring as much magic as possible.

Attributes: Neutral

38

Direct Combat: ×

Magic: ◎

God: Thoth

Characters—Sentinel Characters Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 040

NetHack Start
When you start the game, you'll notice that several parameters other than
strength are displayed on the two lines at the bottom of the screen. These
show the character's status.
Character name is written at the far left of the first line. Next to the name, a
title such as "XXXX the Plunderer" is written, which changes along with the
character's level. After the title, the parameters mentioned earlier are written.
These parameters also change during play for various reasons. The character's
attributes are written at the far right of the first line.

The second line shows the character's current status. The leftmost DIvI
indicates which floor of the dungeon the character is on. To its right, G:
represents the character's gold.
The hit points are written next to the gold you are carrying. These points will
decrease due to enemy attacks and other factors, and if they reach 0, the character
will die and the game will end.

You hear the footsteps of a guard on patrol.

Yoshitune the Bushi St:18/03 Dx:10 Co:18 In:10 Wi:10 Ch:8 Lawful DIv1:3 G:101
HP:11(49) Pw:25(25) AC:4 Xp:5/247 T:434 C1 CU CA S1 SU VOID NWL INS REP *Z

Be Invoked——NetHack Start

39

block-level machine translation prototype · page 041

When it exceeds. The number in parentheses represents the maximum hit
points. Even if you rest or take potions, your hit points will not recover beyond
this value.
That to its right, Pw: refers to mana. It decreases every time a spell is cast. As
expected, you cannot use a spell if this number is below the mana required to
cast the spell, even if you have memorized it.
AC: Armor Class is a abbreviation for armor class. This armor class represents
the strength of the armor the character is wearing. The fewer the numbers, the
better. If it's negative, that's even better.
Experience points are shown on the left. The number in parentheses on the
right indicates experience points. When your experience points exceed a
certain value, your level increases. As your level increases, your hit points
increase and your resistance to poison and magic improves. T: indicates the
elapsed time.
In addition, next to the elapsed time, your hunger level is shown. Normally,
nothing is displayed. When you are full, it shows Satiated, hungry shows
Hungry, extremely hungry and weak shows Weak, and when you are so hungry
that you pass out, it shows Fainting. When you are Fainting, you can't even
walk. Eventually, you will starve to death. Ensuring food supply should be your
top priority.
Health status on the side often shows how you feel. It will display Conf when
confused, Sick when sick, Blind when blinded, Stun when knocked unconscious,
and Hallu if affected by certain drugs or eaten by certain monsters.

At the top of the screen, messages depicting the surroundings and the
outcome of battles with monsters are displayed. For example, sounds heard or
special events occurring around the character (such as a pet suddenly
disappearing from sight) are described in English. Additionally, the names of
items dropped on the floor, thrones, fountains, and other objects present there
may also be shown.
or, choices for your character's actions will also be displayed on this line. For
example, when you want to drop an item, you might see a choice like "What do
you want to drop? [$ad-fi-kw or? *]."

40

Characters——Send Your Avatar to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 042

And the items found by the character walking around in the dungeon are
displayed in the central part of the screen. Below are the meanings of the
displayed text.

Table
a~z} Monsters nesting in the dungeon
A~Z Door on the vertical wall or horizontal wall of the room
;& Door on the horizontal wall or vertical wall of the room
|| Floor of the room, or entrance without a door
—— Passage, or sink, or movable bridge
.. Stairs up
## Stairs down
<> Closed door, or spellbook
++ Human (player), or monster in human form
@@ Gold piece
$$ Trap (not displayed until discovered or triggered)
^^ Weapon (weapon)
)) Armor
[[ Food (includes monster corpses)
%% Scroll with spells or various effects
?? Wand
// Ring
== Potion of water
!! Various tools
(( Amulet or spider's web
”” Jewel or pebble
** Rock or statue
00 Iron ball (used as leg irons for some crimes)
—— Altar, or iron chain to attach an iron ball
}} Puddle or moat (falling in results in drowning)
{{ Spring
¥¥ Luxurious throne (something may happen when sitting)

NetHack Start

41

block-level machine translation prototype · page 043

Now, these symbols construct the Yendor dungeon, but you will need to move
your character using the numeric keypad to navigate through it. However, if
you are using a notebook computer or other device without a numeric keypad,
you can use the cursor keys or lock the (NUM) key and use it instead.

Character

Note that if there is a monster in the direction you want to move, you will
attack with your weapon. However, if the target is a pet, it will automatically
be avoided. When you try to attack a friendly monster or the shopkeeper, you
will be asked if you really want to attack, and you need to answer with [y/n].
This is reassuring. If you want to attack, just input y. However, it is generally
best to avoid attacking friendly monsters or the shopkeeper.

Please refer to the "Items" chapter for the effects and power of weapons, and
to the "Monsters" chapter for the abilities of monsters. Other items such as
altars, fountains, and sinks and their meanings can be understood to some
extent by reading the descriptions provided by Sandras Fone, a leading
researcher on the dungeons of Yendor.

42

Characters—Your Doppelganger Sent to the Dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 044

Walking through the dungeon...

Commands

To travel the world of NetHack, you must master many
commands. However, most of them are single-key inputs, and at
most two characters. You will naturally memorize them as you
progress.

The guild master took a seat at his usual desk in the guild's office and, as usual,
drank the tea that was prepared for him. After taking a sip, he let out a small sigh.
Today was the day to go to the dungeon... How many of us?

He asks the woman by his side. It must be a commonplace conversation for
them. It seemed that everything was conveyed through his single word.
She pushed back her long hair and scanned the parchment she was holding.

Today four people arrived. A samurai, a priestess, a valkyrie, and a thief each.
So far, no one has left. Only one person returned from the dungeon this
morning. Of course, they didn't have an amulet... Four people, is that a bit
many?

She read the parchment with a report written on it in a businesslike manner,
and then, after a brief pause, called out in a long voice.
"Shouldn't there be six? When I entered, there were six of us——but the
amulet is only one."
He answered without taking his eyes off the desk. The feather pen in his hand
moved smoothly across the documents. His work is as busy as ever today.
"why are you here"
The woman asked further.

They will be puzzled too. Just like when we entered the dungeon.

Commands Introduction

43

block-level machine translation prototype · page 045

Though he still held the feather pen, he looked up and answered.

"But if they are sufficiently clever, they should be able to escape the dungeon.
With a bit of luck, they might even come back with the amulet. Well, all we can
do is wait for them to return. Since we know everything, we have entrusted it
all to them."

"……I see"
After a moment's pause, she replied. She had taught them all the survival skills
she knew in the dungeon. There was nothing left for her or the Long to do but
wait for their safe return.
She bowed briefly and turned her back, heading towards the door. She must
teach her technique to those who are aspiring to become adventurers, just like
the four who entered the dungeon today.
[OCR? today she was busy]
Similarly to the guildmaster.

Entering the dungeon of Yendor, just like their beloved disciples.

In this chapter, we have explained the commands in a story-like manner based
on actual situations where they would be used. You should be able to
understand the necessary actions for various situations.

Note: In the text, "t" followed by an English letter means to press the Ctrl key
and the specified letter key simultaneously. Similarly, "#m" followed by an
English letter means to input "#" and then the specified letter. Please make
sure to remember this distinction clearly.

Commands have different meanings for uppercase and lowercase letters, so
please be mindful of this as you play and become more familiar with them.

44

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 046

Pick up Check and pickup items dropped by monsters
Nasrula swings a sword.
"got it"
Great sword falls through the air with a thunderous sound.

Elves tried to block Nasrula's slash with her sword above her head, but her
movement was too slow. Nasrula's swipe pierced through Elves' defense and
embedded itself in her left shoulder.
Blue blood soaks Nasrula's cheek and armor.

"O, thou"
Elves swung their counterattack sword, but they no longer had the strength.
Nasrula's sword would be sufficient to parry it. A few moments ago, just
parrying it would have sent them flying all the way to the dungeon wall.
Nasrula wielded the elf sword and continued the counterattack. She thrust
fiercely and then swept her sword sideways.
"ggg"
Elves cry out. While the blow was parried, the sword tip grazed near the
wound. It was not the elf's body handling of just a moment ago. The sword
technique that flowed like swimming through the wind has been lost.
The outcome was already decided.

"Let's stop now"
Nasrula shouted.
"_you have no chance against me. It's pointless to fight_"

"What is useless? The battle is not over yet. Even wounded, I would not be
defeated by someone like you."
Elves readied their swords. The greenish-silver sword faintly glimmered.

"I am a Dark Elf warrior and the leader of the Hyrpanion Knights, Neveryth. Can
I surrender to mere humans?"
While drenched in blue blood, Neverya attacked. The sword, swung from
above, aimed for Naslura's head. It was a precise strike. But

Checking and Pickup of Dropped Items

45

block-level machine translation prototype · page 047

Throughla dived out of the way before the elf's sword could reach him, slipping
into her pocket. A white-bladed sword sliced off her right arm and pierced her
heart, staining her chest with even more blood than before.
"mu, munen da"

Elves gasped and knelt.
"Even to a human, a human. As for a dark elf warrior like me..."
Nasrula swung the sword. Blood attached to the sword splattered and wetted the floor.

"An't too much... lick the humans"
Elves moved their lips but eventually no rebuttal came out. Bending over, they
fell without using their arms. With a dull thud, the Elf fell.
Nasrula approaches slowly. The elf's body rapidly loses heat, and the light of
life is fading. A creature that was breathing, thinking, and conversing just a few
hours ago has become nothing more than armor and sword.
"Please forgive me"

Naslura did not say anything. She had to achieve her goal no matter what. The
dark elf appeared to hinder her and fought her. If he hadn't interfered with
Naslura, she would have survived.
Nasrula picked up the knife that had been aimed at her throat just moments ago.

(No, maybe it was me who was killed)
Dark Elf was the strongest among the enemies she fought in the dungeon. She
excelled in skill, stamina, and experience. Nasrula could have won if he hadn't
underestimated the humans. If he hadn't thought of playing around, Nasrula
would have had no chance at all.

She suddenly finds herself staring at an elf's corpse. Along with the body, there
were swords, shields, helms, and shortswords scattered around. All of them
were items she had never seen before. Nasrula bends down and picks up one
of the shortswords.
"This is for pickup. Elf's lord."
She sheathed her dagger at her waist.

I feel no sympathy for elves. A warrior should use full strength even to defeat a
rat. A warrior who loses while using not even half his strength is a failure.

Commands—Walking Through the Dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 048

He lost for good reason.
However, he took pride in being a warrior. The pride of a warrior was his life.
"Where should I send your keepsake? Give me the contact information."

He drew his sword and said so. It was a warrior's courtesy for the victor to
deliver the loser's loot. Nevverby didn't think he would lose, so he asked
Nasrula where to deliver the loot. Nasrula had no confidence, so he said
nothing.

The result was reversed. However, he is a warrior.

If not, I should at least deliver a keepsake.
When you kill a monster, there may be a sword or scroll dropped with the
corpse. To check for dropped items, use a command. Stand on top of the
target item and press the (;) key. You can see the list of dropped items.

Checking and Pickup of Dropped Items

47

block-level machine translation prototype · page 049

To pick up an item on the floor, use the pickup command. Stand on the item
and press key ①. If there is only one item, it will be picked up. If there are
multiple items, press key ② or ③ to select the item you want to pick up.

Pickup is an essential task for progressing through the game. Valuable items
often drop unexpectedly. Pick up what you desire and try using them.

r

Read

On the cold floor of the dungeon, Tike lay bare-chested.
Tike's body, which can no longer be called that of a child, is covered by a
single thin gown.
It is not Tike's either. All the items Tike was wearing were piled up in a heap at
the bottom.
A smooth silk gown with Tike's body outline faintly visible through it.
"uh... huh..."

Tike gasped weakly and regained consciousness.
In the time it takes to blink three times, Tike understood his situation and sat
up.
"Yikes..."
The upper body rose up, and the gown slipped down from the chest area. Pink
freckles have appeared from the chest to the neck.
Tike gripped the gown tightly and pulled it firmly to his chest.
"Me..."
Tike's body was completely cold. It seemed like a dream compared to how
warm it had been just a little while ago. But Tike's body remembered that it
was not a dream.

48

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 050

Tike first experienced a thrilling sensation that made him shiver. A fierce joy
erupted from deep within his body and overwhelmed Tike's consciousness,
causing him to shout incoherent words without restraint.
Tike suddenly threw away the gown.
As I reached for the pile of clothes at my feet, my gaze stopped on the
spellbook lying nearby.
So.
When he came over, I was trying to memorize this spell book.
Incubus, a human-shaped monster.
While concentrating on memorizing a new spell, Tike did not notice the incubus
approaching from behind. Although all the doors in the room were closed, they
had not been locked with Wizard Lock. Against most monsters, even after
taking the first blow, Tike would have noticed at once and been able to
counterattack.

However, the incubus did not attack Tike, but instead enchanted Tike.
Tike did as he was told, dropping his weapon, removing his armor, and then...

Bit my lip, and Tike stood up.
Putting on a Hawaiian shirt and picking up chain mail from the floor. For some
reason, it felt lighter on me. Thinking about it, Tike was putting on the armor
while wondering if the note in the fortune cookie he had eaten earlier, which
said "Incubus kills those it likes and gives power to those it likes even more,"
was true.

Wear shield on left arm and wear mace on waist. Wanted to seek a spring and
purify body, but there are more important things to do first.
The room has two doors, both of which Tike locked with a wand. Then he sat
cross-legged on the floor, placing the spellbook he had been reading on his knees.

Magic book cover is written in gold letters.
“finger of death”
That incubus is probably still somewhere on this level.
Absolutely find it and repay it with this spell.

Read

49

block-level machine translation prototype · page 051

The r command is used to read scrolls and spellbooks. If you enter ? after the
command, a list of the scrolls and spellbooks you have will appear, so make
good use of it. By the middle of the adventure, you will always be carrying
several scrolls.

When you read a scroll, its effects take place immediately. Unlike wands or
rings, the effects of scrolls are often clearly visible, so even with an unknown
scroll, it will usually be registered in the identification list with a [?] label before
being identified. Unidentified scrolls have meaningless labels, but once
identified, they change to meaningful names.

When attempting to read a scroll, it takes some time until completion. During
this time, monsters will move around and attack, so it's better to read scrolls in
a closed space. If for any reason you decide to stop reading halfway, the scroll
will not disappear. There is no need to worry.

All spellbooks have a difficulty level set, which serves as a guideline for the
level required to learn them (the spellbook's level refers to the "spell level").

50

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 052

To convert experience levels, multiply by 2 and subtract 1. For the highest
level 7 spell, it can be learned at experience level 13. Additionally, the
intelligence value is important; characters with low intelligence will struggle. If
you fail to learn high-level spells, various (unpleasant) effects such as
teleportation or being cursed may randomly occur.

Once memorized, spells consume mana equivalent to Pw to cast. After a few
uses, they become unusable until you read the spell book again, which
restores their ability to be used. Think of a single spell book as being able to
charge multiple uses.

iI

Inventory

(There's no time to linger, you know...)
Nasrula descended the stairs and began checking her equipment. She had
picked up quite a few items so far, but she hadn't had the time to sort them
properly. Fortunately, the room she came down to has only one door, which is
locked. Here, she can examine the items before encountering any enemies
and prepare herself thoroughly.
First, the wand...
Her true identity is unknown, but she was holding three wands. One made of
aluminum, one of iron, and one of oak. She could use them if she wished, but
there seems to be no effect. In the worst case, the power of the wand might be
directed against herself. Gripping one of the wands, she saw many charred
corpses. She is prepared to die, but she wants to die beautifully. She did not
want her face torn by rats.

"Next are four scrolls"

She arranges the scroll tied around her waist. Two of them were seen before.
One is Remove Curse, a necessity to remove curses. It was useful when I
accidentally put on a cursed armor previously. I don't plan to wear a cursed
armor again, but armor or weapons can be cursed by monster attacks.
Anyway, having it will surely help in the future.

Equipment Check

51

block-level machine translation prototype · page 053

Another is a scroll of charm. I haven't used it yet, but it reduces monsters'
combat will. It should be effective in rooms with lots of monsters.
The other two are scrolls whose effects are unknown. It's unclear if they are
cursed or blessed. Nasrula had no idea what their effects would be. Like the
wand, they were too dangerous to use directly.
It's so disappointing.

are rings and potions.
The true nature of the rings has been revealed. They are fire-resistant rings.
With these, one can walk through fire. This is a valuable item for Naslura, who
is weak to heat. It's clear that having a defensive ring would be advantageous
during combat. The two rings are quite beneficial for advancing the adventure.

In contrast, the potions were hardly useful. The true nature of most potions remained unknown.

52

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 054

It was a disaster. I could only confirm the floating potion, but the rest remained
unidentified. The liquids in the red, blue, and green bottles were all vivid, but
the color did not necessarily correlate with their effects. There's a possibility
that they could be deadly poisons or paralyzing potions.
Nasrula thought that carrying such useless items was pointless, but she
couldn't bring herself to throw them away. If she could use a confirming spell,
the true nature would become clear. Maybe there's a usable potion.

(Sad to see you go.)

Breathing out, Nasrula checked the remaining rations and weapons and armor
one last time. The armor was a new plate mail bought on the 6th floor of the
dungeon, and it was in perfect condition with no scratches. The shield was the
small shield he had brought down to the dungeon, which had high defense due
to the enclosed magic power, but it seemed to have some scratches from
overuse.
(It's about time to replace it)
And the sword. The sword is the legendary blade Stormbringer. It was given to
her by the god Loki who watches over her. The edge is indeed sharp; it doesn't
even drip blood. For Nasrula, who has low strength and constitution,
Stormbringer was an indispensable weapon even deep in the dungeon.

After checking everything, Nasrula slowly stood up. The next enemy is waiting.

Check your equipment and items using the i or I command when you have
many. Both commands are usable at any time.
Press ① to display a list of your items. The order is amulet, weapon, armor,
food, scroll, spellbook, ring, potion, wand, tool, and jewel. If there are many
items, they may not fit on one screen, so try pressing any key to switch
screens and see if the next set of items is displayed.

Listed in the inventory are the item names and quantities. For example, "2 uncursed"

Equipment Check

53

block-level machine translation prototype · page 055

if it shows scrolls of enchant weapon, it means you have two unenCHANTed
enchant weapon scrolls.
The I command allows you to display only the specified items from your
inventory. Pressing ① and a key will bring up a selection screen. At this time,
entering a symbol will show a list of specific equipment or items. For example,
entering ② will display a scroll catalog. If ①, it refers to wands, and if ②, it
refers to scrolls. Additionally, entering "!!", "/", etc., when inputting the I
command will display the catalog without bringing up the selection screen.

Special uses of I commands include 'lu', 'lx', and 'l$'. 'lu' is for items with
unpaid charges, 'lx' for items with unpaid charges that have been used, and 'l$'
to count money. It seems useful when you get confused about your purchases
at a large shop.

dD

Drop drop

Nasrula felt that her footsteps were becoming heavier. It takes three to four
steps of effort just to take one step. After walking ten steps, she runs out of
breath and needs to rest after every thirty steps.
(Probably picked up too many items, huh?)

When I come across something that seems usable, I can't help but pick it up.
As a result, I have unidentified potions and scrolls stuck all over my body.
Although I've organized them many times, I haven't been able to bring myself
to throw any of them away.
However, if luggage continues to increase, it may become a hindrance to
movement. In some cases, it could even be life-threatening.
"No help. Should I drop some?"
First, the gems. Naslura picked up quite a number of gems, including blue, red, green,
and white. Growing up in a world far from gems, she doesn't know the names of the
stones.

(If only Rapshy were here...)
Nasrula was thinking of a friend from the training facility.
Lapsi is also the closest friend among the trainees since their dorm rooms are the same.

54

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 056

He was intelligent, always topping written exams. He was well-informed, and
particularly regarding gems and potions, few teachers could match her knowledge.

"Ah, that's a ruby. Don't handle it roughly."
"Wh-What about this one then?"

"That is just a red glass sphere."
Lapsi would recite the name of the stone just by glancing at it, never missing
once.
"Why do you know this?"

And, Nasrula had heard it many times, but Rapsody would only laugh and not
answer.
For gem identification, both fakes and genuine gems must be known. You need to be
able to discern genuine gems to spot fakes; it's impossible the other way around.

Nastrana knew that Rapsody was of upper-class origin, though Rapsody herself
never revealed her past and showed no inclination to do so. Therefore, she did
not inquire about Rapsody's past.
Friendship and learned much about gems and potions… that was supposed to
be.
However, when actually entering the dungeon and trying to identify them, I
was completely at a loss. Even now, there are several stones with a yellowish
brown glow in my hand, but I have no idea if they are just glass balls, topazes,
or amber. If they were topazes or amber, I could get quite a good price for
them at a specialty store. But if they are just glass balls, they have no value at
all and would only get in the way.

Nasrula hesitated.
But, the decision was quick.

For items whose saleability is uncertain, there's no need to risk losing one's
life.
She threw all the gems away. Naslura silently watched as the gems rolled
away.
Next are the potions. I couldn't distinguish them at all. Like with Lapsii, I tried to
smell them, but I couldn't tell them apart. They don't particularly make you sleepy.

drop

55

block-level machine translation prototype · page 057

did not see any hallucinations.

In the end, the only identifiable items among her potions were levitation and
healing potions. The rest were mysterious items whose nature was
unclear—whether they were potions or poisons. There's nothing more terrifying
than unidentified potions. Although I thought they might be useful for
equipment checks, it seems they just took up space in my inventory.
(che, are you going to drop this too)

Nasrula dropped all except those whose identities were known. If luck favors,
he might come back for them later.
If only I had asked Lapsy more about this.
Examining the heap of gems and potions piled high, Nasrula sighed. It's
shameful. I shouldn't have such thoughts as an adventurer.
However, it seemed like such a waste.

56

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 058

It might be better to drop items with the d or D command when you have too
many items and equipment to carry.
If you know which item you want to discard, type "dh" to throw away one item
registered in h. Typing "dh 2" will throw away 2 items. If you're unsure, just
press (d). When asked what item to discard, press (?) to see the item catalog
and examine the items carefully.

Using the D command allows you to place several items at once. When you
press the (D) key, it asks what you want to place. Here, if you input a symbol, it
will ask which of the targeted symbol items you want to discard. By entering
(/), it refers to a wand, and by entering (?), it refers to a scroll. Then, choose
the item you want to discard by looking at the screen. This command is handy
when you want to place a specific item all at once.
Special uses of commands include "Da" and "Du". "Da" drops all items without
confirmation, while "Du" drops items unpaid for. "Du" can be used as "D ? u" to
discard only unpaid scrolls. These commands are handy in shops.

OC

Door Opening and Closing

Nasrula closed the door.
(Saw something unbelievable)
The room behind the door was filled with monsters. Not just one or two, but so
many that they crowded the space. There were 15... no, depending on the
situation, there might be up to 20.
There are rooms where monsters gather in the dungeon.

And Yendor taught this during lectures.
"Be careful. You can fight each monster for more than five turns, but against
a group, it's impossible. The strength of monsters doubles or triples."
"..."
"If we fought properly, we would be turned into meat in less than a minute"

Door Opening

57

block-level machine translation prototype · page 059

How do you deal with such a room?
Nasrula asked, and Master Nealco smiled faintly.
"Decided"
"..."
"Run away"
Nasrula turned away from the door. He intended to follow his master's words faithfully.

However, the foot did not move.
Certainly cannot win in a fair fight.
(But, a surprise attack might work)
The monster is unaware that Nasrula opened the door. Fortunately, she has
one more use of the flame wand. She also has a ring of protection. By taking
down one-third of the monster before it gets into position and engaging from
the entrance… It may not be possible to fight without taking damage, but they
should be able to fight even-handedly.
Surprise training has been repeatedly conducted at the training facility. Nasrula
received a "Good" in the surprise subject. She would approach her target
unnoticed and attack. The opponent would be caught off guard and defeated
without even realizing they were spotted. She once killed nearly twenty
trainees by herself in a simulation. She had confidence in her abilities.
(yaruka)
Nasrula had bought quite a bit of food on the 8th floor, so she wasn't
financially strained. The food was fine, but she no longer had the funds to
obtain armor or scrolls.
Treasure rooms where monsters gather have a lot of money taken from
adventurers. To make future adventures easier, I wanted to save up some
funds. The best way to get money is to find a chest, but it's difficult in the
current environment without teleport scrolls or magic maps.

A room where monsters gather, not the best for making money, but a second-best option.

However...
Nasrula hesitated to place her left hand on the hilt.
"Peril should be avoided whenever possible."

It is a cardinal rule for adventurers. Even Neardok emphasizes this repeatedly. You
should not risk your life for immediate gain. It may seem like taking a longer route, but it
is more reliable.

58

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 060

To travel on safer paths is closer. It's too late once you die.

Nasrula's funding is tight, but it's not something that involves life and death.
It's silly to risk one's life for a scroll.
This is straightforwardly

And her reason was saying so.

She hesitated. Her thoughts raced back and forth, oscillating between
conflicting choices. She couldn't seem to settle down.
To open or not to open. That was the question.
However, Nasrula's scale tends to favor emotion over reason.
Further, her blood was that of an adventurer. The danger is known. However,
adventure cannot progress by avoiding danger alone. Most importantly, she
fears danger.

Door Opening

59

block-level machine translation prototype · page 061

You don't enter the dungeon from the beginning.

This is a trial
Nasrula thinks.
If you cannot get through this, you will not be able to survive later.
She touched the door.
Take a deep breath and open the door.

When Naslura leaped into the room, the monsters' gaze converged on her. A
strange scream arose, and the monsters sitting on chairs stood up. An orc
grasped a sword and intimidated them.
Nasrula held up the flame wand. The wand glowed red.
"Fine!"
Flames writhing like serpents scorched the monster. Shouts echoed through the room,
and an awful stench assailed the nose. Corpses, like wood, tumbled and disintegrated.

Sometimes you cannot enter or advance even though there is a room in front
of you. This is because you haven't opened the door of the room. To open the
door, press key ① and specify the direction of the door. When the door opens,
a message will appear, and you can come and go freely.

Usually opens with one attempt, but if the door is poorly fitted, it may not open
easily. In such cases, try opening it several times. By the third or fourth
attempt, it should open if the key is not stuck.
A door locked with a key must be unlocked first. Without a key, your efforts to
open it are likely to fail. It's better to stop or find another way if possible.

To close an opened door, press key ③ and specify the direction of the door.
This will close the door, preventing entry or exit until it is opened again. While
this action is less frequent than opening a door, it can be useful, for example,
when fleeing from a monster without hands. I recommend trying it when you
feel it's necessary.

60

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 062

Z

spell casting

"Ah?"
Yoshitsune couldn't help but look at his hands. The hand that was supposed to
be holding the katana was empty.
However, upon a full body check, it was found that the katana was not the only thing missing.

Helmets, dragon hands, and even the plate mail made of crystal, which I had
struggled to obtain, were all gone. The only armor left was the boots reaching
to the knees. Even the backpack on my back seemed lighter.
"Ugh, so you met a nymph..."
Yoshitane slumped onto the floor, clearly exhausted. A look of regret that he
couldn't express fully was on his face.
Nymph——Countless carvings, paintings, and poems have continued to praise
their beauty. Even the most beautiful women, who are aware of their own
beauty and have polished and refined it to its utmost limit, would be unable to
match the nymph's demonic beauty. Her beauty is beyond description, and
any sculpture, painting, or poem would pale in comparison to the nymph's
beauty.

That nymph was beautiful to such an extent.

Yoshitsune was captivated by its appearance alone. Not a single eyebrow
moved, and he allowed a nymph to take his katana, remove his helmet, and
pull the dragon's hand from his arm. No, he had no choice but to allow it.

"Ah!"
Yoshitsune sighed heavily.
"Must confirm what has been stolen..."
muttering my usual soliloquy since entering this dungeon, I went through the
contents of my backpack.

Spellcasting 61

block-level machine translation prototype · page 063

And then, my eyes doubted. There was not even one weapon. My trusty
lightning wand and not even a single shuriken remained.
"Nante kotta"
Yoshitane couldn't help but look to the sky. Although he needs to defeat a
nymph to retrieve what was stolen, he now finds himself fighting unarmed.

At that time——a nymph appeared in the room where Yoshitsune was.

The nymph, possessing the power to transcend space, appears and disappears
suddenly. With a beautiful and seductive smile on her lips, she can be seen
approaching again to grab Yoshitsune.
"Ugh, there's nothing wrong..."
Yoshitsune was not enchanted, but after his bare body was rubbed against it,
Yoshitsune's body remembered the sensation of his mind going blank for an
instant.
"Chihh, let's see if you've got what it
takes."

He knew he had only one hand left. However, it was a hand lacking in
certainty. But, that was the only thing he had left.

62

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 064

Not being there is also a fact.
Yoshitane, having made up his mind, closed his eyes and placed his right index
finger on his forehead. Strange words escaped from his lips. As he continued
the incantation, he soon felt a hot mass rising from within his body.
Open eyes. I could see the nymph was approaching closely. Yoshitane's actions
seemed suspicious, and he was slightly lagging. However, Yoshitane's
incantation did not stop.
Hand reversed, held high. The incantation stopped, and a roar, neither a shout
nor a cry of effort, emerged from Yoshitane's mouth. At the moment his
fingertips grasped the nymph, multiple beams of light were emitted from his
fingertips.
