A torrent of light tears through the sky and is absorbed by the nymph's body.

Nymph let out an inaudible scream and fell down.
Bits of armor and dragon hands appeared from somewhere on its body.
"Ah. Has it... been created..."

YoshiTune slumps, letting his knees give way. The incantation of unfamiliar magic
and the concentration required drained more of his stamina than he expected.

"Rather not do it again if I can help it."
Yoshitane muttered the spell he was casting over and over in his head while
breathing heavily.
"Magic missile. Might be useful depending on the situation."

By reading a spellbook, the magic memorized is usable with the Z command.
Additionally, the list of memorized spells can be displayed with (x) or (+).
Magic fades with use (casting a spell consumes the memory of the spell), and
eventually, spells can be forgotten entirely.

Sometimes when casting a spell, you will be asked for the direction to cast the
magic. In this case, for attack spells and the like, use the movement key in the
direction of the enemy. If you want to cast magic on yourself, simply input (○).
Also, it's a good idea to remember that light-based spells will reflect off walls.
However, intelligence was insufficient or not enough stamina to cast spells
Spellcasting 63

block-level machine translation prototype · page 065

If it doesn't exist or there isn't sufficient magic power (Pw), nothing happens
and the magic power is wasted. It's safer to test-fire it beforehand.

Use Tool

a

Nasrula felt the world turning upside down. She lacked the strength in her legs
and knees buckled. Trying to put her hands out for balance, she could not
move them and lost her balance. Nasrula fell on her back, and her vision
distorted strangely as her sense of balance went haywire.
I was careless. I thought I had checked everything, but it seems that some
strange food had gotten mixed in.
Muscles spasm and movement is restricted. Vision is distorted. There is also ringing in the ears.

However, fortunately, my thinking is clear. The degree of corruption was low. It
doesn't seem like I took fatal damage.
(Lean slightly to the side……)
And Nasrula thought. More accurately, there was nothing else he could do.
If monsters come, there's no place to hide.
He meant to smirk, but he could not even move his lips.

I felt sick. It was the feeling I had after drinking too much alcohol during my
training days, when I got a two-day hangover for the first time after drinking
alcohol. At that time, I didn't know where the limit was. I got drunk by the
atmosphere, and even Rify and Lapsy couldn't stop me, so I opened two
bottles of wine. The next day, I couldn't move and just lay on my back without
moving.
Recovery will likely take quite some time. Perception was screaming like a
deranged gear.
The flickering vision steadies, and the outlines of objects become clear. The
loud snore gradually subsides, and silence should have fallen… except that
Naslura's surroundings are not empty as they should be.
However, Nasrula's ears caught something... sounds like footsteps.
Something is here

64

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 066

In a large dungeon, you won't usually encounter other adventurers except
Nasrula. If you hear footsteps, they are definitely those of a monster, and quite
close. They could be in the same room, or only separated by a single wall.
Failed to utter mocking words; no sound came out.
(This won't do, I can't use a unicorn's horn like this)

Unicorn horn is a magical item. It can remove diseases, hallucinations,
blindness, and all other conditions. With a unicorn horn, you can recover from
any condition to full health.
However, the freedom of his limbs has not yet returned. He struggles to
concentrate strength in his arms, but only his fingertips move slightly. Though
the unicorn's horn is just a short distance away at his waist, his fingers seem to
be moving through a diamond wall.

The roar of the monster is getting closer. There can no longer be any doubt
that it is in the same room.
(Oh, hurry... Unicorn Horn)
Using Items

65

block-level machine translation prototype · page 067

Guess and Naslura moves her fingers.

I felt the bottle of potion in my fingertips. The unicorn's horn is below it.

However, that was the limit. Nasrula could no longer move her fingers. The
monster was looking down at her.
The true form of the monster is unknown. However, no matter how weak the
monster is, it is still a formidable enemy for her, beyond that of a dragon.
Previous section
She also made her decision.

However, surprisingly, there was no attack. Most monsters do not hesitate to
attack adventurers the moment they spot them, whether with swords or fangs,
there was always an attack.
(maybe...)
The monster might be hesitating. It might think the motionless adventurer is
dead. Except for creatures that feed on corpses like jackals, monsters don't
engage with corpses. Especially not intelligent creatures like elves....

Nasrula did not move a muscle. Not only was her breathing stopped, but all the
muscles in her body were halted. Taking advantage of the fact that she
couldn't move, she pretended to be dead.
Nasrula's bet paid off. The monster had been watching her for a while but
eventually went away.
Again, Nasrula moves his fingers. Something hard touches his fingertips.

This is it
She gripped it reflexively and pulled it out all at once. She forcibly raised the
arm that tried to resist and held it against her forehead. The sensation in her
hand was incomplete, and the tip of the horn had pierced her finger. She
realized how severe the paralysis was, to the point where she felt no pain.
However, what she had pulled out was undoubtedly a unicorn's horn.
Nasrula felt the numbness being sucked out of the unicorn's horn. At the same
time, sensation returned to his fingers.
(can)
She grasped the sword and stood up. The monster turned around and stared at her.

66

Commands——to walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 068

It was as expected, an elf.
Nasrula readies her sword and faces the monster.

A command is a command to use an item.
tools are items classified as tools among carryables. There are many types,
such as unicorn horn, blindfold, pickaxe, lamp, master key, etc. All of them are
used by hitting the a key. When opening a lock, hit the a key to select the key
and specify the direction of the door you want to open. As long as you have
the key, you can unlock it.

When using a pickaxe, press the a key and specify the direction of the wall or
door you want to break; it will usually create a hole in most walls. To blind
yourself, simply press the a key and select blindfold, which will obscure your
vision and put you in a blind state. This is essential when fighting monsters
that can turn you to stone with just a glance.
These tools are almost essential for continuing your adventure. I hope you will
choose them wisely and overcome crises.

Z

Zap Swing wand

"Ah"
YoshiTune let out a small cry.
Unknown room attack SOURCE attacked me the moment I tried to enter the unknown room by opening the door.

However, I managed to parry before being attacked…… That was a
swordsmanship worthy of praising myself. ……If the opponent wasn't an Acid
Blob, that's the story.
Acid Blob is a magical creature armed with strong acid for self-defense. When
slashed with swords or axes, the blade can be corroded by the acid deep
within its body. Yoshitsune always tried to kick or fight with bare hands against
Acid Blobs, as he once had a club endorsed by the shopkeeper for its
resistance to acid specifically for use against Acid Blobs.

However, there is no time to switch to barehanded strikes or to reach the range for kicks.

Swing staff

67

block-level machine translation prototype · page 069

There was absolutely nothing. The body reacted before such thoughts. It's
something a warrior would be praised for.
"…That also depends on the situation."

Yoshitsune muttered as he looked at the katana he had long cherished.

An acrid odor and smoke, characteristic of metal dissolving in acid, drifted
from the katana. At the very least, several chips must have formed in the
blade. But if Yoshitsune carelessly reached out to wipe it off, he would burn
himself. He could only watch in silence.

Several minutes later, the smoke finally cleared. Swinging the katana back and
forth to check the balance, it was clear that the center of gravity had changed
significantly. It would be difficult to hit an enemy at all, let alone worry about
blade spray.
"……Cannot be enchanted without a scroll……"
Yoshitsune sighed softly and sheathed the katana, then drew the wakizashi.
While a chipped katana is bad, a proper short sword is somewhat better.

At that time, Yoshitane noticed a rumbling sound coming from the door on the
other side of the room.
(Oak tree)
Moreover, it was as bad as encountering a Urk-Hai. Yoshitsune had just met
several Urk-Hai on the floor above, and had seen them in a half-dead state.
OCR? ...
Yoshitsune glanced at the Thunderbrand wand in his belt——it had revealed its
true nature due to a mistake he made. A wand that releases powerful lightning
by unleashing trapped magical power, but it might have lost all of its magical
power already.
"...how many more times--no, can it still be used?"

At that moment, the door opened, and five Urks-Hai appeared in the darkness.
Yoshitane reflexively drew a wand from his waist holster and swung it.
The wand is burning hot in Yoshitune's palm.

Two bright flashes erupted.
When the flash of light subsided, the room contained four corpses and a single
Urk-Hai reduced to half-burnt charcoal.

Commands——Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 070

Joshua threw away the wand and readied his wrist sword. He will stab the
wounded Urk-Hai through the side. After that, he can eat the moderately
roasted oak meat. It won't be a particularly refined taste, though.

Adventured-found wands can be used with the z command. If you have
multiple wands, enter 1 to see what kind of wand you have. However, like other
items, the nature of a wand obtained immediately after picking it up is
unknown. After all, "a wand with no power" does exist.

It's preferable to identify scrolls and spells before using them, but you can also
identify them by waving them at monsters. If fire appears, it's a fire wand; if
electricity appears, it's a lightning wand.
However, many attack-type wands emit rays that reflect off walls. Of course,
using them towards walls in narrow rooms would be foolish.

Swing staff

69

block-level machine translation prototype · page 071

It's also inevitable that the number of sacrifices will increase by one (and who
that sacrifice will be... that's also inevitable, I suppose). It's best not to use
attack-type wands unless there is a spacious area.
Other useful wands will prove valuable aids in your adventure. By the way, if
you want to use a wand on yourself, just input nothing when asked for a
direction.
However, all wands have a limited number of charges. The number of charges
is shown in parentheses when listed with the i command. When this number
reaches zero, the wand runs out of power and can no longer be used. Even if
you know the wand's power, unidentified wands may not react at all when you
try to use them because their number of charges is unknown.

cursed wands have the usual effectiveness but have the weakness of
potentially exploding.
This way, a wand is convenient, but it comes with dangers when used.
However, if you properly identify it and use it in the right place and situation, it
will be very helpful.

Search Dungeon exploration

S
The path ended here.

Nasrula examines a hand-drawn map. From the steps, it's certain that there's
a room beyond the wall.
However, before her was simply a stone wall. No door could be seen.
"There must be some secret passage..."
She touched the wall. However, there was no reaction. She prodded it with the tip of
her sword, but there was no change. She checked again, but the result was the same.

Nasrula bit her lip.
"False lead...?"
If there is no door here, you can only go back the way you came. However, if you walk a
little, you will encounter an ogre. At her current level, she was unable to defeat that
monster just now.

70

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 072

It was barely avoided. In my current state with unhealed wounds, it was
evident that I would have become an ogre's meal.
She faced the wall.
"Are you going to give up?"

Nasrula was remembering the words of Neurko.
As an adventurer, Master Nealco explored many uncharted places, including
the Sea of Chinarock, and after retiring he achieved great success in training
adventurers. Countless famous adventurers came from among his pupils,
including Nealktic, who explored Eclipse Mountain, and Royalchi Ranger, who
revealed the full extent of the Falamond Caves.

His mentor at Nasrula's Adventurer Training School was also Nealco. He taught
the skills necessary for an adventurer. The techniques he imparted are
perfectly useful in the dungeon. In fact, without his teachings, it would have
been impossible to survive in the dungeon.
At his graduation from the training school, he gathered all the graduates and gave them his final lecture.

"Don't give up"
Master Neurko peered into his students' eyes.
"Those who give up in the dungeon are the losers. Even if your hit points are low or
you are surrounded by monsters in a desperate situation, you must not give up."

"Teacher!"
Nasrula raised his hand. His companions frowned and stared at Nasrula. It was
the greatest disrespect to interrupt a master's lecture at the training school.
Some teachers would even hit the student who interrupted. However, Master
Nealko merely smiled slightly.

"something"
"Question?"
Say it
Certainly. Here is the English translation of the provided block:
"Indeed, I think it is important not to give up as Mr. said. But there are times
when you really can't do anything. For example, when surrounded by monsters
on all sides and there is no way to escape. There are times when fighting is
futile. What should you do in such cases? Truly giving up..."
Dungeon Exploration

71

block-level machine translation prototype · page 073

Is it better if there isn't one? Perhaps it would be better to die cleanly and not
make such a spectacle of oneself.
Master Nealco gazed at Nasrula for a while. There was no anger in his eyes.
Rather, they were filled with a fatherly kindness.
"Indeed, that might be the case"

Master Nealco said in a whisper.
However, I cannot recommend that to you. What I have taught you and your
companions is the skill of survival. I have trained you all to endure any
environment and return to your families and friends here. I cannot advise on
death.

When we find ourselves in a desperate situation, what should we do? What if
there's no escape, and our food runs out—how should we proceed?
"Action"
Still looking into Nasrula's eyes, Master Nealco declared firmly.

72

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 074

"Even if it seems outrageous. Even if it's a desperate action. There's no need to think
calmly. Just move forward. Keep moving forward. Then an escape will open up."

Nasrula listened silently. He wanted to argue but couldn't find the words.
"You don't need to know now. You'll understand after you've gone on an adventure."

Nasrula only nodded in response to Neardok's words.
At that time, I couldn't understand why death wasn't scary. But I didn't want to
die in a pathetic manner.
However, after descending into the real dungeon, her way of thinking changed.
She no longer wanted to die. She didn't know why. Just from seeing corpses of
people who had died unseen in the dark dungeon, she became afraid. She
didn't want to become like that. She desperately wanted to survive and get
back to the surface.
Desire arose in Nasrula's heart.
"Are you going to give up?"

Nasrula mutters and examines the wall. He breaks apart bricks and digs
through mud, carefully checking for anything missed.
She found the hidden door handle only a few minutes later.
when stairs are not found in Yendor's dungeon or a message appears but the
shop is not found, you may have overlooked hidden doors or secret passages.
Use the s command in such situations. Press the (s) key in places where doors
or passages might be hidden. Since it often doesn't find anything just once, try
the s command multiple times at places you think might have something. You
can input "n 10 s" to explore 10 times consecutively.

Elves automatically investigate walls and passages without needing to enter
the s command. They possess a supernatural ability to discern slight
differences in walls and passages. However, do not overtrust this ability; use
the s command if something seems amiss. Only elves are born with the innate
ability to explore, but archaeologists, thieves, and (for some reason) tourists
can develop equivalent skills through experience.

Dungeon Exploration

73

block-level machine translation prototype · page 075

Hidden doors and passages may be cunningly disguised in some locations.
Don't give up; search thoroughly and patiently multiple times. Unexpected luck
might await you.

#o#p

Offer, Pray

"heavy oh"
Tike was struggling to carry a corpse that was twice his size. It was one of the
Frost Giants he had killed in the nearby room. There was no way he could lift
and carry it, so he was dragging it by one arm over his shoulder. Step by step,
he walked slowly, pressing each foot down as he moved.

Tike is not something to eat. Take it to the room you found on this level called
"Altar Room."
There is an altar dedicated to the god Tike where Crom is worshipped.

If Tike was not a priest but had taken up another profession, we might have
been able to pretend not to see and pass by. Alternatively, if he were a
lycanthrope or a chaotic deity, or a neutral deity other than Crom...
From the style, its altar was made of chrome, Tike knew immediately. No, he
knew it. He had seen it every day for years. There was no way he could have
been mistaken.
Tike wandered through the ruined altar, cleaning it and seeking offerings for
Chrome.
Fortunately, there were giant dwellings on the same level. Tikes' power has
grown to easily handle giants. Upon returning to the surface, the title of Canon
will be bestowed. The armor I'm wearing is made from scales of the gray
dragon I defeated before, and the sword is none other than Excalibur.

By offering this giant's corpse, the service to Tike will likely be over. If it's a
giant of such size, the god will surely be satisfied.
Nevertheless...
74

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 076

"Wow, it's heavy! "
What would happen if Tike were to abandon this giant and run away, he
thought as he stopped. Croam would surely get angry and never lend Tike a
hand again.
"Um... sir, uh... about 6 times?"
Tike remembered how much Crom had helped him until now: when he was in
the shallow levels of the dungeon and was about to starve after running out of
food; when cockatrice petrification poison had spread through his body and,
just before losing consciousness, he called out to Crom; and when Crom
restored his strength after he was surrounded by minotaurs. He had also
prayed for trivial reasons, such as removing a cursed ring.

"Y, this much is necessary. ... But it's heavy!"
Tike took a deep breath and began moving again.

Offer, pray

block-level machine translation prototype · page 077

After several minutes, Tike was between the altars. Struggling to place the
giant's body on the altar, Tike tumbled onto the floor, breathing heavily like a
horse that had run itself nearly to death. After resting for a few minutes, Tike
finally managed to sit up and began muttering prayer words reluctantly.

"O Crom, lord of worldly battles and swords. Accept this offering as a token of
my gratitude... I mean, please take it as a pickup."
Say this and raise both hands above your head at the same time.

"……What's that?"

After waiting for a while, nothing happened. Normally, the sacrifice should
vanish instantly.
"Um, ah. ...Chrome?"
When Tike started to wonder whether it was the one who had misspoken the
prayer or the one who had lain down in front of the altar—
"Old!!"
A loud "voice" echoed in my head.
"Sorry! Sorry!"
Tike pressed his head and crouched, desperately shouting.

Still, the giant's corpse seemed to have decayed by the time it was brought
here.
I noticed a strange smell and wondered if it was also a Tike.
#p and #o commands are both related to gods. However, you won't need to
use the #o command unless you find an altar. The #p command is used more
frequently.
Praying with a command can sometimes resolve various problems, such as
hunger, a critical loss of hit points, blindness, paralysis, or even cursed
equipment. If you find yourself in a difficult situation during the game, try
praying first. The effectiveness of your prayer depends on your wisdom value.
However, if the deity is angry or enough time has not passed since your last
prayer, you may not receive any help. Additionally, if you are on what is called
the "Hell" or hel level, unfortunately, divine power cannot reach you.

76

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 078

Praying without need will anger the gods and bring down appropriate divine
punishment.
If you stand on the altar and use #0, you can offer monster corpses to it. As
long as it is the altar of the god you believe in, it doesn't matter where it is
(you can find out the name of the god by using the command on the altar). The
more valuable the offered monster, the greater the reward. A raging god may
calm down, and if you have a friendly relationship with it, it might grant you a
powerful item. However, since altars appearing in the dungeon are random,
there might not be an altar corresponding to your attributes.

q

Drink Quaff

"Kya!"
The door opened and the room entered, Tike was screaming the moment he stepped in.

However, there weren't any monsters with terrifying appearances that I had
grown accustomed to after exploring the dungeon, nor were there any traps
with nasty intentions that revealed the personality of the setter.
That room had a "spring." In this case, the scream is a "joyful scream."

"kyakkyak"
Jumping joyfully towards the spring, Tike has forgotten to keep watch around
him.
A pool of water has gathered in the depression on the floor, probably seeping
from the ceiling. It's about knee-deep. TiKe knelt on the floor and peered into
the water. The clear water reflected TiKe's face like a mirror.
Oh no! It's so dirty... This looks like a beggar's clothes!
Tike has been in this "Yendor's dungeon" for 5 days since entering. After
fighting monsters, getting splashed by their attacks, dealing with dust, oozes,
spider webs, and other filth, Tike's entire body is so dirty—like a teenage
girl's—that it can't be shown in public. The robe given by the Pope before
departure also has an unpleasant odor in some places.

drink

77

block-level machine translation prototype · page 079

Stains have appeared.

Tike stood up and removed each item he was wearing one by one.
I have found a spring where you can take a bath. There were springs on three floors
above, but those were polluted with the bodies of orcs and smelled terrible.

Dropped chain mail clatters to the floor at your feet.
While hiding her half-grown chest with one hand, Tike tried to step into the
spring with her feet.
"Ah...and!"
At the point where his feet touched the water, Tike exclaimed and
reconsidered entering the spring. His desire to take a bath was so strong that
he had forgotten another important thing.
"His throat was dry too."
Which to do first, wash the body or drink water, is self-evident. Tike scooped
cold water with his hand and brought it to his mouth.
At that time, the water in the spring started moving.

Water swirls and gathers in the center of the spring, gradually building up.

"Water" became humanoid and eventually took the form of a demon with two
arms and a pair of horns.
"Ah, ah..."
Tike could not stand up due to the sudden event and retreated while sitting.

[OCR? ...]
78

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 080

"Ah?"
Tike blinked.
Thank you for freeing me. I should express my gratitude.
Steam-like vapor poured from its body, and the demon shrunk instantly.
Eventually the vision cleared, and there was nothing there. Not even the
fountain that had been splashing water...
Tike remained dazed for a while and then muttered something.
Showered, should not have...

Command is used to drink something. It can be a potion or a spring
represented by {. When drinking water from a spring, stand on it and input the
command. However, left parenthesis, }, should be noted as it represents
digging. Entering here will surely drown you. If it's confusing, it's better to
check in advance using the / command.

If you drink a potion, its effects will become apparent immediately, regardless
of what it is. The quickest way to identify an unknown potion is to drink it.
However, some potions may cause blindness or paralysis—effects you might
not welcome. You should be prepared for such possibilities.

drink

79

block-level machine translation prototype · page 081

Once the effect of a potion is determined, it is added to the identification list
shown by the identify command, and subsequently, when you find potions of
the same color, their names will be automatically displayed.
When drinking from a spring, various effects will randomly occur. While it often
just quenches your thirst, it can also provide a small nutritional boost, or
conversely, drain your strength due to poison in the water. Additionally,
extremely rarely, it may summon water-based monsters such as Water
Demons or Water Nymphs. If a Water Demon appears and you are blessed
with a handful of good fortune, it might grant your wish.

But seeking accidental luck by drinking from fountains at random is foolish. In
the effects of fountains, the scales of good fortune and bad fortune are
balanced. Before you encounter good fortune, you may end up shouldering
fatal misfortune.

Up, Down Staircase traversal

<>

Nasrula held several unidentified staffs at her side as she stepped onto the
stairs. Since they were stone, they wouldn't break easily, but the steep part
near the bottom was terrifying. Despite trembling, she gripped the pillar tightly
with her free hand.
She carefully lowered her feet slowly and cautiously. Step by step, the floor of
the lower level became visible. If she had nothing else, she could jump down.
However, she was worried about the three potions in her pocket. If the bottles
broke and she inhaled the contents, she wouldn't know what would happen.
There's even a possibility of going blind instantly.

descending stairs your feet naturally slow down.

" It's not that easy. "

Ryfro's face changed. He twisted his lips and stuck out his tongue.

You won
And. As expected, Rifyu disappeared above her head.
This isn't a joke. Training with a guy like that...

descended the stairs much faster than half her time. No one could match her
in lightness.
Ryfro was an extremely skilled trainee, but was avoided by the other students.

Reason is simple. He was a thief.
Training halls had students from all levels of the dungeon. The most common
were children of adventurers like Nasrula, but there were also farmers,
craftsmen, merchants who had switched careers, children of knights or
samurai, and priests or mages.

Among them, Rify was special. A student from a thief background, Rify

Stair Climbing

81

block-level machine translation prototype · page 083

In addition to them, there were a few others, but all of them were lying about
their origins. They were trying to conceal the fact that they were thieves so
that no one would sniff it out. But Riffor was different. He clearly announced
during self-introduction that he was a thief. Moreover, he said that he was

Nasrula knows how thieves are referred to outside the training facility. She said
that she has no pride in being a thief, and there were hardly any students who
associated with Riffo, who made such a claim. Nasrula was one of the few
exceptions.
Nasrula and Rhyfor felt uneasy for some reason. The techniques they learned
were countless. How to open boxes, how to disable traps, and her dexterity
skills were almost all taught by him. It was thanks to Rhyfor that the clumsy
girl passed the test.

Why Nasrula couldn't understand why he was being avoided, Reiffo is a honest
man who doesn't lie. He's not some worthless scoundrel who tries to fool
people with words. A trustworthy man.

82

Commands——to walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 084

(Shall we continue training?)
Nasrula began descending the stairs. He had also taught her the trick of going
up and down stairs, and she was simply doing as he had shown her.

Finally Nasrula reached the ground.
When he sensed the texture of the ground, he felt as though he were
ascending to heaven despite having come down. Thinking that he would not
fall any further, Nasrula took a deep breath and sat down on the ground.
"Good job!"
After training, the man would always approach her and give her advice.

Nasrula felt like he heard a man's voice from somewhere.

descending or ascending stairs is a necessary action for controlling the
dungeon.
To use a staircase, you first need to find it. Usually, ascending stairs are
indicated by a symbol within the dungeon, while descending stairs are shown
by the ">" symbol. When the symbols are not visible, try walking around
statues. There may be stairs hidden beneath them.

To ascend or descend, move the character to the desired symbol and press the
corresponding key. The screen should then switch, and you should be able to
confirm that you are on the upper or lower level.
Just carrying too many items can result in being unable to climb stairs and
having to reluctantly drop items. When using stairs, you also need to pay
attention to your items and carrying capacity. Additionally, if you use stairs to
go up from level I without the Yendor amulet, the game will end, so be careful
not to make this mistake.

As the adventure progresses, other means of moving between levels besides
stairs will appear. However, the basic method is to use stairs, and it is
impossible to achieve the objective without using them. When you arrive at a
new level, first secure the stairs down.
Stair Climbing

83

block-level machine translation prototype · page 085

Put on, Weild, Wear — finger ring, weapon, armor

Her plate mail removed, in front of her was a new suit of armor. The color was
a light gray. It did not have a good impression. It was made entirely of leather,
with no metal used. Even if it were in a specialty store, there would not be a
single person who would want to buy it. A drab-looking suit of armor.

However, she knew. It was a dragon's armor. One of the strongest and most
defensive armors compared to all others.

"Do you know about dragonmail?"
Talking to my uncle happened three days before entering the dungeon.

At that time, Nasrula had returned to his aunt and uncle's home. He had
informed them in advance by letter that he was about to embark on an
adventure. However, he wanted to tell them himself about going to the
dungeon and express his gratitude for raising him.
However, I could not say anything in front of my weeping aunt. She was a
strong woman. Nasrula had never seen his aunt cry since being taken in, even
when her brother died, when she arranged the funeral on the battlefield. She
was often spoken of behind her back as "the iron woman."

Nastrana could hardly believe the sight of her aunt crying like a child. She
didn't know what to do and just stood there in a daze. If her uncle didn't put
her aunt to bed, she might have stood there all night.
"Even though it's an adventure, you can't help but get emotional when your daughter goes. It's
different from going to get married. The possibility of never seeing her again is higher."

Nasrula silently listened to her uncle's words.
"..."
"Still, you want to go, I see."
Nasrula nodded.
"Then, listen to me for just one thing. Listen with the intention of appeasing
me," he said, and then asked Nasrula if he knew about the dragon's armor.

84

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 086

Nasrula shook his head. This was something not taught at the training facility.

Dragon scale mail is armor made from dragon scales. Much harder than
ordinary armor. You can't even scratch it with the best weapons. It's the
strongest armor.
"Did not know"
Nasrula muttered.
"Training center didn't tell me that"
"That's true. Few have seen a dragon's armor. Only a handful, such as the
prophetic king Baliair, ring king Gudorfin, Sir Eclipse, Sir Saint Simon, and Sir
Hyperion, have ever laid eyes on it."
"Why does Uncle know this?"
Nasrula stared at her uncle.
"Unbelievable, uncle...)"
Old story
He waved his hand.

"More important than that is dragonmail. Listen carefully. Dragonmail is made
from a dragon's corpse. If you happen to slay a dragon...""

Nasrula heard his uncle's story. The uncle carefully explained all the details. By
the time he finished listening, Nasrula had no questions left.
"Got it. With this, we should be able to make a dragon's armor. If you think it's
a good chance, don't hesitate to try it. Sounds good."
Nasrula nodded. He understood the dragon's armor.
(But...)
One thing Nasrula was concerned about.
"Hey, uncle"
"Uh, what's up?"
"Why did you teach me how to make dragonmail? I don't really understand,
but if dragonmail is a valuable item, its crafting method shouldn't be
known—no, it shouldn't be known!"
"Seems like it..."

Rings, weapons, armor

85

block-level machine translation prototype · page 087

"Then why"
" That's about all I can do for you. "
Uncle took a pipe and exhaled deeply. Rising smoke hid his face.

"Really, I'd be the same as him. I don't want you to go adventuring. But you're
not the kind of kid who would back down once you make up your mind. Go
where you said you would, no matter what anyone else says."
"..."
