Uncle...
Return home. You don't have to succeed with your adventure. Just make sure to come back alive.

86

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 088

That's all. This is my request.
Nasrula hugs without saying anything. She knew she would cry if she spoke.

Uncle
Nasrula thought while looking at the armor.

Thanks to my uncle. I got the best armor.
She picks up the armor. It was lighter than plate mail. Earlier, she tried cutting the
breastplate with a sword, but no damage was done. Its high defense is undeniable.
She looked away from her previous armor. She switched her sword to Stormbringer
and stepped onto the downward stairs. Floor 25 of the dungeon awaited her.

To wear armor, use the W command. Press [W] and it will ask you which armor
to wear. If you're unsure which is the desired armor, input [?] to see a list of
available armors. Once you've confirmed, select the armor you want to wear.

When putting on new armor, you need to remove any previously equipped
armor first. You must take off the old armor before putting on new armor. The
same applies to shields, gloves, helmets, and boots.

Some armor requires you to remove other armor before putting it on. You
cannot wear a Hawaiian shirt over armor. Remove your armor first. Also, cloaks
are equipped over armor. Therefore, once you put on a cloak, you cannot
change your armor until you take off the cloak.

Commands to equip weapons are w. Press w, then choose the weapon you
want; unless it is cursed, the selection will go smoothly. If you're unsure which
weapon to choose, press ? to display a list of weapons, then select an alphabet.
To carry nothing, input -.

Rings, weapons, armor

87

block-level machine translation prototype · page 089

There is a command to equip a ring. It's the P command. Enter P and select the
ring to equip it. However, only one ring can be equipped on each hand, left or
right. You cannot equip more than three rings. If you want to put on a new ring
even though you already have one on each hand, you will have to remove one
of the existing rings.

Remove, Take off Remove armor, remove ring

RTA

Nasrula took off the plate mail.
Covered in wounds from various places. Several grooves as if scratched by
breastplate armor, and part of the shoulder is missing. If compared to a
human, it would be a warrior who has fought continuously and is covered in
battle scars. The scars shone like medals.
(But, you've done well so far...)

Nasrula gazed at the armor. Since purchasing it on the 6th floor underground,
he had used it non-stop all the way down to the 24th floor without changing it.
It had been slashed by a zombie's sword, bitten by a jackal's fangs, and clawed
by a dragon. It was a rough usage. He had fought through battles where the
armor could easily have been reduced to powder.

Defense did not drop until the very end; it could be called a miracle.

"Really a fine item, wasn't it?"
Nasrula ran his hand over the armor and muttered.

"Miss, that's a fine item."
The shopkeeper called out to her as she examined the armor.

This is a special suit of armor crafted by Sir Hyperion, the world-renowned
master craftsman. While it lacks magical power, it's truly remarkable as his
final work. You won't find anything like this very often.
"Is that so? It doesn't seem all that different to me..."
Nasrula showed little interest. Indeed, the black-painted armor seems sturdy. The
breastplate is thick, and the overall balance is good. It's clearly not cheap junk.

88

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 090

was different.
However, it was also a fact that there were no outstanding features at all.
"Don't be silly"
The shopkeeper became angry at her indifferent attitude and raised his voice.

Don't be foolish. This contains a soul.
"Soul?"
"Indeed. A magic-infused armor might be nice, but this chain mail will surely
protect the lady. That's clear to me."
"Can't see it for me"
"Sure, there's a big difference in age between you and me. If Lady could see
what I see, I'd hand over the store and retire quickly. I can see souls rising from
armor, which is why I'm doing business. Others can't see what I see."

"Is it not resentment, rather than a mistake?"

Cursed armor is useless. It actually reduces your defense. I don't want to buy
armor that serves no purpose.
Such things never happen
The shopkeeper tapped the chain mail. A dry sound filled the store.

"This armor is really good. You'll see when you try it. Buy it just to be sure."

The clerk stared at Nasrula. A serious gaze.
Nasrula smiled wryly and decided to buy the armor he recommended. Of
course, he didn't believe the shopkeeper's claim that "the soul is embedded."
Armor had to be purchased anyway. It was just a coincidence that the armor he
liked matched the one he was recommended.

However, I was slightly swayed by the shopkeeper's overly earnest persuasion.

Please drop by on your way out.
A shopkeeper called out to Naslura, who was clad in new armor.

"What's going on?"

Remove armor, remove ring

89

block-level machine translation prototype · page 091

"No, I really want to hear about its usability. Since I recommended this armor
with confidence, it would affect my reputation if it broke right away or wasn't
useful."
"What do you do if it really breaks?"
"Then I'll return it with double the payment."
"Because I believe."
"Okay. So, please come back alive."
The shopkeeper's words were correct.

The armor served her well beyond expectations. It continued to protect her
until she finally changed it for a new one. Even if she had paid double, it might
not have lasted.
It might be true that souls are contained within them.

Thank you
She whispered to something invisible.

90

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 092

Did not want to discard it. Wanted to bring it along until returning to the surface.

However, the dungeon has reached level 24. Although we defeated the
medusa, there are surely more monsters stronger than the medusa waiting
ahead. We had no choice but to fight them and survive.
Nasrula held the armor tightly before slowly placing it on the ground.

T and A commands are commands to remove armor.

The command is used to remove one piece of armor at a time. If you are
wearing only one piece of armor, that armor will be removed. If you are
wearing multiple pieces of armor, it will ask which piece you want to remove.
Enter the alphabet corresponding to the armor you want to take off, such as a
cuirass, shield, gauntlets, or helm, and it should remove the armor unless it is
cursed. If you don't know the alphabet for the armor you want to remove, try
entering ①. You can check the armor list and enter the alphabet for the armor
you want to remove to take it off.

Command is used to remove all armor. Press ② to remove all armor unless it is
cursed. Input ③ to remove weapons and become unarmed.

To remove a ring, try using the R command. If only one hand has a ring on it,
just press 4. If both hands have rings, make sure to check which one you want
to remove. Unless cursed, the ring should come off easily with the R
command.

t

Throw

Blood from wounds is scattered on the dungeon floor.

Clain pulled his shortsword out of the goblin's body with a rough breath. The
dead goblin collapsed onto the floor, losing its support.
"Ah well, I messed up this time"
Claymore placed his hand on his right shoulder and noticed that the leather armor was torn through.

Throw

block-level machine translation prototype · page 093

Warm blood was flowing out.

"I probably fell into a pit trap and Mike is worried about me, so let's head back
quickly to heal the wound..."
As soon as Klein thought this, a loud cry that resembled that of a strange bird
echoed through the dark dungeon.
With narrowed eyes, he discerned the enemy's true form, and couldn't help
but curse to himself.
A dark green scaled body, amber eyes that reflect no emotion, and a
menacing jaw lined with sharp teeth—there is no doubt, it is a lizardman.
Hastily switching to a bow, Clain shot arrows at the approaching lizardman.
Two of the four arrows grazed the lizardman's body, but no fatal wound was
inflicted. In that moment, the lizardman used his sword to defend his head
while gradually closing the distance with Clain.

Clain was overcome by a sensation as if his footing were giving way.

"Damnit!"
When the arrow was thrown, Klein threw a shortsword at the lizardman.
The sword flew in a straight line towards the lizardman's head, splitting the air,
but when the lizardman thrust his sword forward again, it was easily parried.

If you don't run, fighting is a fool's choice... What do you do!?

In a moment of hesitation, the lizardman was able to approach. With a cry of
joy at his victory, he swung his sword and charged forward. It seemed the
lizardman was salivating, as foam continuously dripped from his mouth,
anticipating the taste of Klein's flesh.
There must be something in my hand. Something...

Clain kept his gaze on the lizardman and searched the sack with one hand.
Various items' textures ran over his sweaty palm.
When the lizardman was just a few steps away, a cold glass bottle touched Klein's hand.
Before his thoughts could catch up, his right hand moved. The potion was in the bottle.

92

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 094

The man's head shatters, and white liquid pours over his face.

"Grghh!--!"
The lizardman roared and struck with his sword in a direction different from
where Klein was located.
Dodge the lizardman swinging his sword wildly and hurry to the corridor on the
upper floor.
Behind there is a growl suggesting the monster wants to pursue its prey.

"Hey, sir I'm afraid of you"

Groaning, Clain took a step into the corridor.

In NetHack, use the t command when throwing thrown items, when throwing
held items, and when feeding food to pets.
When using ranged weapons such as bows, slings, or crossbows, you must first
equip the weapon with the w command, then specify the arrows with the t
command and indicate the direction. Of course, you can also throw arrows
without equipping the bow, but the power of throwing arrows by hand is less
than when shot with a bow.
When throwing items, most items are not particularly useful. However, for potions,
if you throw them and they hit a monster, the potion will affect the monster.

Throw

93

block-level machine translation prototype · page 095

You can give the effect of the potion to monsters. That is, if it is a potion that
causes blindness, the monster will be unable to see the player for a while.

Therefore, it's not a good idea to throw potions at monsters indiscriminately.
After all, if the potion restores vitality, the monsters' wounds will also heal.

When giving food to your pet, it's not necessary to do it frequently, so if you
want to tame your pet, you can give food at appropriate intervals.

k ^D

Kick — kick

Ping!
With a clear sound of breaking steel, the needle of the dart that was inserted
into the keyhole of the chest broke.
"An, mooh!"
Tike threw the darts that ran out at the corner of the room and sat down on the
floor. He tried to pickpocket but it's not going well. He's using the darts he
broke just now; it's the fifth one.

Tike looked resentfully at the pentagonal keyhole. If there was a key that fit
this keyhole, one could obtain the contents without such trouble, but Tike only
has triangular and square keys.
"Why even lock the door!!"
Michael, who had been wandering the room, turned to Tike and meowed
"nyaa." He must have thought he was being called.
Tike can now make two choices.
Decide whether to leave without opening the chest or pretend to be a thief
until the key opens it. Another option is to carry the chest around until you find
a pentagonal key, but this is unlikely to be worthwhile.

94

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 096

"Good!"
Tike decided to engage in a thorough battle.

Remove the hair ornament from atop her forehead and hold it in her right
hand. This golden hair ornament was given to her the day before entering the
dungeon by a boy. She knows that even if she breaks the sixth pin, she cannot
pick the lock with darts. Something more like soft wire will be needed.

"Phillian, sorry"
muttering, Tike inserted a soft wire into the lock.
After about an hour, Tike gave up. The pure gold pin of the hair ornament in
his hand is miserably bent and can no longer perform its original function.
"Ah! This! This!"
Tike shook the chest forcefully. The sound of something rattling inside was
irritating. Michael, who had been sleeping beside Tike, woke up and meowed,
"nyaa."
"…let's go"
Tike, exhausted, stood up unsteadily. He picked up his backpack and walked
toward the door at the back of the room. The hair ornament was left next to
the treasure chest.
The handle of the door didn't budge when I touched it.
(There's a key here too!?) >>>

Tike's heart filled with anger. He took a step back and took a deep breath.

"Ha!!"
deliver a front kick with spirit.
With a loud noise, the door flies apart as if torn from the hinges.
Tike is not good at memorizing hymns but has never been outdone in body
training.
Slightly cheered up, Tike was about to leave the room. But something was
bothering him.
"Ah!"
Tike couldn't help but shout. There was no need to open the chest's lock.
kick

95

block-level machine translation prototype · page 097

Kick it apart and it would be done, wouldn't it?
Tike turned around and ran back, kicking the chest with his momentum.
At that moment.

"Kyaah!"
With a loud noise, a flame pillar rose from the chest and enveloped Tike's feet.

There was a trap set in the chest.
"ach, ach, chichi-chi……"
Tike, whose feet were burned, picked up ten or more scrolls.
However, it goes without saying that everything was completely blackened by the fire.

Commands can be used in several ways: kicking monsters, kicking structures
like doors or walls, and kicking items. The procedure is the same for all cases:
input (k) and then indicate the direction using the number keys. However, if
you kick something other than a monster, you are likely to injure your feet,
especially early in the game. When injured, you lose hit points and your
dexterity decreases. While natural healing occurs over time, during this period
your carrying capacity decreases, making it more difficult to manage your
inventory.

96

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 098

Kicking monsters deals damage. In particular, the "kick" of samurai and priests is
powerful, and they can knock out monsters in shallow layers with a single hit.

Normal doors can be opened with the o command, but for doors locked with a
key, you can either use a spare key or a credit card, or kick them in. However,
once a kicked-in door is closed again, it cannot be kicked open again.
Therefore, if you need a secret room and plan to use this door later, it's wiser
not to kick it in. Also, kicking a wall will only hurt your feet, but if there's a
hidden door there, you can kick it in just like a visible door.

When you kick an item, it usually moves in the direction you kicked it.
However, be cautious about kicking potions, as they may break due to the
impact. The distance an item moves depends on its weight and your strength
(very heavy items won't move). Also, keep in mind that you can break a
chest's lock by kicking it, but be sure to consider the risk of triggering any
traps.

#f #1

Force, Loot Open, check contents

"Never ends! Oh!"
Tike grumbled but swung his sword at the ogre in front of him. Excalibur easily
split the ogre's torso in two, and the ogre's upper body fell back with a clean
cut. Another monster appeared, leaping over the ogre's corpse. This time it
was a demon enveloped in flames.

Tike was surrounded by piles of monster corpses. It's unclear how many more
there are, even to Tike itself.
"!"
Tike was hit by a dragon breath from a distance, which was blocked by his shield. The Blue
Dragon's "Thunder Breath" reflected off the mirror-like surface of the shield, hitting several
monsters.

open, to see what's inside

97

block-level machine translation prototype · page 099

Vanish into the corner of the room, dragging the monster with them.

Electricity, fire, and any magic had no effect on Tike. The qualities or armor
acquired while wandering the dungeon rendered light beams and magical
attacks ineffective. And the scale mail made from the scales of a gray dragon
provided almost complete protection against all attacks.

Dragon endured several strikes but eventually fell. And it didn't take much
time for the other monsters to meet the same fate....

"Phew... This might be the last time?"
Tike threw a Tike on the floor, creating a chest. Chests and coffins kept appearing,
and though he was half-exhausted, Tike gathered them all in one place.

There are about 8 of them. Finding this many treasure chests in one room is a
first for Tike. There was a castle on the upper floors, and I saw armories and
treasuries, but there were no treasure chests arranged inside the rooms there.

Tike was happy in front of the mountain of treasure chests. From experience,
he knew that what's inside the treasure chests are good items.
Tike inserted Excalibur between the hinges and pressed down the lid to open
it. This is unusual for Tike; he usually kicks open treasure chests. While it's
certainly easier to pry something open with a sharp weapon like a sword, using
a holy sword in such a manner feels wrong.

But I'm tired...
Yendor's legendary sword Excalibur shouldn't break or have its blade fall off,
but Tike moved on to the next long chest without much thought. It took no
time at all for all the lids to open.
"This is... not needed. Pass, pass, pass, pass, pass, pass!"
Tike began to test items by picking them up one after another. There were
many powerful items such as Gauntlets of Power, Reflect Shield, and
Regeneration Ring, but in the end, Tike chose only a few scrolls.

All the others, I had them all.
98

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 100

Using #f and #l commands, you can open chests and coffers and take their
contents. You can also open a chest by kicking it with the k command. Another
method is to use the pickup command to take the contents out first, then use
the a command to "use" the chest. However, this method becomes inefficient
when dealing with heavy chests, as you may need to temporarily drop some of
your items on the floor to pick up the heavy ones.

When using commands, you must equip a weapon with a sharp tip such as a
sword, or a "pounding" weapon like a pickaxe or mace. After entering the
command, you will be asked to confirm, so input y. If the weapon you are
currently equipped with is inappropriate for prying open a chest, a message
will appear indicating this.

To use a command, stand on the chest and input the command. Initially, you
will be asked "Take the contents of the chest out?" and you should answer with
'y'. Then, the name of each item inside will be displayed one by one, and you
will be prompted to enter 'y' or 'n' for each.

When you have displayed all items, the game then asks "Is there anything you
want to put in the treasure box?" Just as when picking up items, you will be
asked about each of your items one by one.

open, to see inside

99

block-level machine translation prototype · page 101

Even though you won't often put something in the chest when you open it, this
procedure is common when using handy tools like sacks or holding bags
(especially if you put items in a holding bag, the total weight will drastically
decrease! ), so it's not a waste to remember it.

Eat take

e
Nasrula was incredibly hungry.

Since losing my last food supply a long time has passed. The grocery store is
far away. First of all, I don't have any money. I thoroughly searched this floor,
but there were no fallen food items. At best, I managed to find some spring
water, but it won't stay in my stomach.
Food, especially meat. Meat was needed.

This will cause me to faint from dizziness.
"Please, anything will do. Just come out, okay?"
While clutching her stomach, she saw a door in front of her as she wandered through the dungeon.

Expectations rise. It's common for monsters to be in the room upon opening
the door. There have even been times when I was attacked by monsters and
suffered serious injuries. If I'm lucky, there might be two or three. No, if they
can satisfy my hunger, however many would be fine. I planned to use all kinds
of magic and items to defeat the monsters.

Nasrula leaned against the door. Took a deep breath and opened the door.

The room was not particularly spacious. She looked around instinctively, but
there was no sign of any monster.
For an instant, it seemed like my expectations were disappointed.

Failed?
feel the strength draining from your body.

However, when she heard the growling sound from the back of the room, her
expression brightened. She looked at the thing located in Nasrula's right front,
the farthest point from her. It was a jackal. With glowing red eyes and bared
sharp teeth, it was intimidating her.

100

Commands—walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 102

Body length is large. It was the largest jackal she had fought.
"Uwaah!"
Nasrula cheered.
"protein"
Before the jackal could attack, Nasrula leaped forward. She swung her sword
with all her might, but it was parried to the right. Due to hunger, her body
lacked strength. Normally, her opponent's head and torso would have been
severed.
Jaguar lunged before Naslura could readjust her sword. Her raised sword barely
blocked the jaguar's teeth. The teeth and sword tangled, and the jaguar's foul
breath touched Naslura's nose.
Nasrula retreated while swinging her sword, but it only grazed his ears.

"damn"
Nasrula continues to attack but it doesn't go well. Instead, she is cornered by
the jackal's claws and fangs. Even against an opponent who usually wouldn't
require much effort, it was futile this time. She couldn't put strength into her
sword-wielding arm, and hitting at all was difficult.

(Otherwise, you will be eaten before you can eat the meat)

Before her strength ran out, Nasrula decided she had no choice but to make one
final counterattack. She thrust out the point of her blade and crouched low.
"Come here"

Nasrula charges as the jackal jumps. The jackal shows no intention of running
away. Nasrula catches the jackal's bite and stops its movement. The jackal
continues to push, but Nasrula does not back down. Without retreating, Nasrula
presses the sword forward.

Her passion for his flesh is intense. She exhausts all her strength and keeps
pushing the much stronger jaguar.
The jackal felt disadvantaged and retreated to increase the distance from Nasrula.

"Got it!"
In a moment of opening, Nasrula swung the sword down. Since it was right
after retreating, the jackal could not dodge it. A fatal blow cracked the skull.
Brain matter splattered, and the eyeball fell out.

Eat food

101

block-level machine translation prototype · page 103

Killed instantly.

"Good job"
Nasrula lowers her waist.
"Meat, meat, meat, meat!"
She looked at the corpse and wiped her saliva.

command is for eating.
If you have food, you can eat at any time except during combat by pressing
the ① key. If you have no food but are standing on a monster's corpse, you can
press the ① key to eat the monster's remains.
Eating restores nutrition and allows continued adventuring. Additionally, some
monsters can inherit their qualities by eating the corpse of another monster.

102

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 104

can.
Character's hunger status is shown in the status line at the lower right of the
screen. When hungry, it becomes "hungry." If food is not consumed further, it
will show "weak," reducing Str by 1 and making it harder to cast spells.
Continued neglect will progress from weak to "fainting," eventually leading to
death.
Starvation reduces your combat ability, so you are significantly disadvantaged.
If you faint, you won't even be able to engage in proper combat. At the very
least, I hope you can eat before becoming weak.
No character, no matter how strong, can win against hunger. It is almost
impossible to continue adventuring without eating monster corpses.
Additionally, by eating monsters, you might gain desirable qualities. There may
be hesitation, but try it first. You might come to find it delicious eventually. Of
course, the freedom to say "Elves never eat goblin carcasses!" still exists. It's
the same as having the freedom to die of starvation.

Pay

p

On the floor, various items were placed on a spread-out cloth. This is a shop in
the dungeon, marked by a signboard that reads "Dee's Curios."

This surprised Tike, as he had thought that the dungeon contained nothing but
monsters, traps, and other things that try to kill him.

A potion of an unknown color. A spellbook with a title in gold letters on a
leather cover that I've never heard of. A coat dyed in strange colors that
seems to be worn over armor.
Tike walked restlessly among the rows of items, stopping in front of a ring. The
intricately worked ring, adorned with flowers, looked like a small garland. It
was probably a magic ring imbued with some magic, but more than that, it
seemed like an engagement ring used in wedding ceremonies.

Pay

103

block-level machine translation prototype · page 105

It looks like a ring.
Tike bent down and picked up the ring quietly.
"Ready!"
"Yes!"
Tike almost drops the ring at the shopkeeper's voice.
" That's 466 Zomarks, Lady. "
At the counter, the shopkeeper, who was polishing a shield, looked up from his
work and stared at Tike through round glasses. The shopkeeper's well-colored
expression silently asked, "Would you like to purchase this?"
"Um... a... hehe..."
Tike blushed. Attracted by the beauty of the ring, he couldn't help reaching
out, feeling embarrassed. He doesn't even know what effects it might have.

"Those… what kind of ring is this?"
"Well, I see. You're not interested in that. The important thing is just how much
it can be sold for."
Tike hurriedly asked the shopkeeper who was about to return to work.

"Why do they have prices if we don't even know what they are?"
"That's a merchant's instinct. I'm sure that ring is a find. I could put my sign up
for it."
Tike sighed. This shop prices things of unknown value. In other words, the only
reliable thing seems to be one's own eyes.
Tike returned the ring to its place and walked around the store, collecting only
items whose effects were known so far, such as food, healing potions, and
wands that can shoot magic missiles. However, for spellbooks, he collected
only those he had never seen before.

"This one is 260, that one is 400. The food rations there are 3 for 250."
Tike arranged the gold chips as the shopkeeper read them out.
"Sure, right on. Nice to meet you."

104

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 106

If you take the money, the shopkeeper won't even look at the Tike anymore.
After finishing packing the items into his backpack and turning towards the
store door, Tike suddenly remembered the ring.
That ring was... just beside Tike's foot.
Tike raised his head and glanced at the shopkeeper. The shopkeeper, with his
back turned to Tike, was diligently polishing the shield.
Tike was comparing the ring with the back of the shopkeeper for a while, but
eventually kicked it with his foot. The circular ring rolled quickly and managed
to exit through an open door.
The shopkeeper turned away and is unaware.

(Ouch!)
In his heart, he cheered, and Tike was about to leave the shop.

"Lady, 466"
[OCR? "Ah, already..."]

Tike reluctantly began to untie the money belt.

Pay

105

block-level machine translation prototype · page 107

In the real world, you also need to pay for items you purchase. However, in
NetHack, the p (pay) command exists independently, which is why NetHack is
NetHack.
Whether to pay the shopkeeper is up to the player's discretion. However, if you
leave the shop without paying, it can lead to trouble (see the command
introduction for "#w" for details).
If you intend to pay, after picking up items, enter 'p' and all item prices will be
automatically deducted from your gold. If you don't have enough gold, you can
sell unwanted items by dropping them with the 'd' command, but you'll only
receive about half the purchase price. There are also shops that specialize in
selling wands and rings, and other items cannot be sold at these shops. Most
shops will buy gems, but many of the gems you pick up in the dungeon are
worthless "glass balls." However, some real gems can be bought for a high
price, and using them can gain the favor of the gods. In this sense, the gem
appraisal skill of an archaeologist is very useful.

Here are some hints on how adventurers have traditionally tried to trick the
shopkeeper and obtain items for free in this world. The methods include using
a pet, kicking, polymorphing (changing form), and teleporting (or causing
someone else to teleport).

Rest
The wound was deeper than expected. Blood keeps gushing out from the
Minotaur's claw marks, and the bandages turn red every time they are changed.
(If only there were a healing potion at times like this)

Healing will work even on deep wounds, but there are no scrolls or potions left.

106

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 108

"Then, it seems we have no choice but to wait."

Nasrula sat down. Her wound was treated with herbs. She could move after
resting for a while. Ideally, she would have rested in a small room with the door
locked, but her wound was too deep. If she met a monster, it would be the end.
She could only wait.

Nasrula sighed.
(You've adventured for quite a long time)

Nasrula no longer knew when she had entered the dungeon. A week ago? A
month? Two months? Hearing her aunt's tearful attempts to stop her felt as if it
had happened only recently, yet ignoring her friend's advice also felt as if it
had happened more than a year ago.

Passing through the dungeon gate was filled with anxiety.

"There are monsters over 5 meters tall"

"There is a dragon at the back who kills all who approach with its breath."
The rumors confused her mind and amplified her anxiety.

However, Nasrula's desire to strike down his enemy was stronger than his fear.

Nasrula's father was killed by a monster dozens of floors beneath the dungeon.
Her mother, shocked by the news, took to her bed and then died as if following
him. Left alone, Nasrula was taken in by her childless aunt and uncle.
[OCR? uncle's husband was a blacksmith who made farming tools. He had a
stubbornness befitting a craftsman, but he was kind to Nasrula. No matter how
bad his mood, he never raised a hand to her. Even when scolding her, he only
raised his voice slightly and never screamed. Whenever she cried, he would
always hug her. Nasrula loved her uncle's chest, which smelled of iron.]

Uncle was relatively gentle, but Aunt was much stricter. She was particularly
harsh about discipline and would loudly scold if there was even a slight lack of
propriety. She hit him more than once or twice. If he skipped household chores
or wanted to go play, he wasn't allowed back into the house upon his return.
Usually, with Uncle's intervention, he managed to get in for dinner, though he
often missed it.
Only once have I spent a night in the storage room. Famous adventurers have also done so.

Rest

107

block-level machine translation prototype · page 109

When Naslura visited the town where Throughla lived, she lied to her aunt and
went to listen to the adventurers' stories. The stories were so enjoyable that
she couldn't bring herself to leave, and by the time she finally returned home,
it was already dark. Naslura asked her aunt to let her in, but her aunt refused
to open the door.
"What were you doing today?"
"..."
"Probably the adventurers' stuff. You know it well enough, after all."

" Aunt, listen to me"
I don't want to hear about a daughter who won't listen to me. Spend a night
outside to cool your head.
Nasrula pounded on the door many times, but there was no response. She went crying to
the storage room. It was spring, but the mornings and evenings were cold. Shivering, she
walked.

108

Commands——to walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 110

Passed the night without resting.
"Be ladylike" was her aunt's catchphrase, and the education to that end was strict.

However, Nasrula did not resent her aunt's policy. Perhaps her aunt sensed
that Nasrula strongly inherited her father's blood. That's why she thoroughly
educated her as a woman. She wanted Nasrula not to follow her father's path
and become an ordinary woman. I understood the kindness of my aunt's
intentions. When I spent the night in the storage room, a blanket and nighttime
food were properly placed there. I knew she never disliked me.

Yensura wished she could have lived with her uncle and aunt. But she couldn't.
The adventurer spirit inherited from her father drove her to seek adventure.

[OCR? 16-year-old Nasrula told her foster parents she wanted to join a guild to
train as an adventurer. Of course, her uncle and aunt opposed this. They
wanted her to take a husband and continue the family business. They
repeatedly urged her to live a life of loving a husband as just a woman and
nurturing children.]
However, Naslura did not give up.
Dangerous. Foolhardy.
She still couldn't stay away.
Nasrula managed to join the guild by pushing through both objections. This
was three years ago.
"!"
The jackal's roar brought her back to reality. Sharp teeth and red eyes were
directed at her. It was a threat. She sensed an attack was imminent.
Nasrula stood up. The time for sentiment was over.

Command is a rest command.
When you use a key, time advances by one turn without performing any
action. However, if you are injured, your hit points will recover.
It may seem like an meaningless command, but the rest command is optimal
for restoring hit points without the power of healing. Combined with the E
command, you can restore a considerable number of hit points.

Rest

109

block-level machine translation prototype · page 111

#t

Turn Purification

"Hey?"
Tike hesitated upon reaching for the door handle, puzzled by a strange
sensation, and retracted his hand from the knob.
Through the door, you sense something in the air.
To put it like this, it's somewhat similar to the feeling that Tike experienced in
the sanctuary of the temple where he was born and raised. Priests generally
have a keen sense of spiritual beings, which is understandable given that the
purpose of their training is to converse with the divine.
"nnn..."
Tike closed his eyes and tried to calm his mind, sensing the
surroundings delicately.
This aura is not that of a divine being of a higher plane. It feels murky. I
thought there might be a "altar room" behind the door, but instead, something
unwelcome seems to be there. Tike held his mace in his right hand and readied
his shield in his left hand.

Push through the door from shoulder height, opening it wide in one motion.

"Uwawa!"
Rooms contained "colorful" undeads. Faded purple, skin peeling pink. Green
wriggling with maggots, and mouse-brown like mummy wrappings.

Undead who noticed the Tike were slowly approaching with unsteady steps.
While one by one might not be so concerning, being approached by such a
group is more than just "creepy"; it starts to make you feel queasy.
Tike was unable to suppress the rapidly fading fighting spirit. Even though she
knew it was hopeless, girls have a natural aversion to this kind of opponent
that they can't control.
It was now at the limit.

In just a few seconds, you'll likely scream in embarrassment and run away.

Commands——Walking Through the Dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 112

"O, guardian god Crom……please help me!"
Without consciously thinking about it, Tike muttered the name of the gods
and, with his extended finger, drew a symbol in mid-air.
A zombie at the front of the row is enveloped in bright light.
A few seconds after the light disappeared, the zombie stopped moving like a
puppet whose strings had been cut.
"Chrom!"
Tike, gaining momentum, cries out again in the name of the gods.

Mummy dragging decayed bandages glows white…….
But the mummy did not fall. As soon as the light disappeared, it resumed its
steps as if nothing had happened.
"Yander why!? ——Chrom!"
Rushed to call out, but the result was the same.
Light is easily deflected, and the mummy's steps do not stop.

Tike held his ground, restraining himself from crying. His will, which had
wavered for a moment, was now holding strong.

111
Purification

block-level machine translation prototype · page 113

For this, you can rely only on your own strength.

"Crude chrome!"
Tike screamed and attacked the mummy.

Tike will eventually realize that his inexperience was causing the undead's turn
to fail, but that's another story.

Turn Undead is a special skill possessed by Priests and Knights. A spell of the
same name exists but has the same effect, allowing the destruction of undead
creatures. However, the spell has a limited number of uses, while the two
aforementioned classes are born with this ability and can use it unlimited
times. Note that there is a wand called Undead Turning, but this merely instills
fear in the undead, causing them to flee, and its effect differs from the skill and
spell.

The effect of turn affects only visible undead. If you are in a room, all undead
are targets. However, on a corridor where you can see only one step ahead,
only adjacent undead are targets. Note that undead whose location you know
through telepathy are not affected, as you are not actually seeing them.
Similarly, you cannot turn if you are also blind.

Whether turning will succeed to destroy the undead depends on the
character's level and the monster's level. Against undead that are several
levels lower than the character, turning is almost certain to succeed. For
higher-level undead, they may resist (regenerate), and the success rate
decreases as the level difference increases.

However, if the turn fails, you won't gain experience points, and you can only
turn one undead per turn. Also, you can only turn undead that are several
levels lower than yourself. Considering these factors, it might be better to
attack with your weapon instead. Note that blessed weapons hit undead more
easily and deal more damage. If you have a blessed weapon, you should attack
without hesitation.

112

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 114

^ #u

Detect Trap, Untrap Trap Detection, Trap Removal

"Man, thieves don't have what it takes!"
Yositsune's scream echoed futilely in the dungeon.
Yoshitane found a long coffin on level 12 of the dungeon.
It's not unusual. Sometimes you can find three or four cabinets or treasure
chests in a single level. The lock on this long cabinet required a square key,
but he didn't have even one key. As usual, he tried to kick it open, but for
some reason, he sensed something this time—hostility, or perhaps a killing
intent, from the cabinet.

Because he was able to have this sense, he was able to survive as an
adventurer until now. Yoshitane well understood that this sense must not be
ignored——but he was not a thief.
Yoshitne had received basic training from the guild, but that was all. He
passed the examination. However, the instructor whispered to Yoshitne, "Don't
think you'll actually try to disarm traps when you find them."
Clumsy, basically.
No, the expression clumsy does not apply to Yoshihane. He was sufficiently
dexterous. However, the task of disarming traps does not suit his nature.

For about two hours, Yoshitane sat in front of the coffin investigating the type
of traps that were likely set.
"……It's no good! I don't understand!"

Yoshtene lay flat on his back on the floor. He understood without a doubt that
a trap was set. In other words, the only thing he had figured out in two turns
was that his senses were correct.

Disregarding the trap and kicking it open is also an option. The trap will
definitely activate, but you can still obtain whatever is inside. However, if the
trap sprays fire, that's one thing, but you should also consider traps that
release sleep gas or paralyzing poison needles.
Traps

113

block-level machine translation prototype · page 115

It has been taken. There is even a possibility of being eaten by goblins or orcs
while asleep.
Yoshitsune's mind contained many possibilities, and all of them were discarded
into the corner trash bin.
I knew the easiest choice was left. Although it frustrated me to have to make
this decision after spending so much time, there were no other options
available, so there was nothing I could do about it.
"Have you given up?"

Yoshitne slumped and sighed, then staggered to his feet. Behind the long
coffin, Urshi, who had been curled up, stood up with a yawn, saying "Well,
well."
It was important for Yoshitane to obtain famous swords and armor, but he also
understood that surviving was the most crucial thing. However, he regretted
wasting two hours indecisively.

After Yoshitsune was teleported by the trap, he stood in front of this long coffin.

114

Commands——to walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 116

It will happen again. However, it is another matter if he discovers that there is
nothing inside when he manages to disarm the trap by chance.

Sometimes, traps are found on doors or the lock parts of chests. Traps on
doors are mostly triggered when attempting to open them, causing explosions.
Those set on chests or treasure boxes may contain poison needles or sleep
gas, among other types.

These commands to discover traps are #u. After inputting this command, you
need to indicate the direction where you want to investigate for traps. If you
find a trap, you will be asked whether you want to disarm it. Entering (y) here
will start disarming the trap, but failing to disarm it will trigger the trap instead.

Disarming traps requires a suitable degree of dexterity, so be careful. If your
Dx value is around 11 or lower, you will learn the importance of knowing your
own limits. Sometimes triggering such a trap can even be fatal. Of course,
once a trap has been triggered, there will be no further trap there to find.

Sometimes you may discover traps set on the floor using s commands and
others. While these traps cannot be disarmed as shown by the h command,
you can still determine the type of trap using the h command.
Sometimes it is necessary to intentionally fall into a trap. For example, if you
can find a teleport trap in a sealed room within the dungeon, you would
probably jump into it anyway. Even if it is a trap, thinking flexibly can
sometimes reveal unexpected uses.

Traps

115

block-level machine translation prototype · page 117

E

Engrave write

"Nothing's here, you know."
Tike stood in the center of the dimly lit room and grumbled.
Descending the stairs to this floor, this is the third room, but it contains
nothing. There are no items dropped here, and no treasure chest.

No light was found in any of the rooms checked so far, so time was wasted just
confirming that there was nothing there. If monsters had appeared, Tike
wouldn't have been angry, as most monsters become food when defeated.

"Sometimes it's nice to have a peaceful one like this."

Tike muttered to himself and took off his backpack from his shoulder. Having
been wandering around for quite some time, he had started to feel hungry a
while ago. From the cluttered contents of his pack, Tike pulled out a package
labeled "K Ration." He had acquired just one of these before and had saved it,
looking forward to seeing what it tasted like.

Tike also thinks that the food situation in the dungeon isn't necessarily bad.
However, this is assuming you don't consider taste.
"Ah, no!"
Tike realized that he had left the room door open. Once before, while absorbed
in eating, he had failed to notice a monster approaching. Still holding the
ration wrapper in his hand, Tike stood up.
"Hey? What's this?"
Tike forced the rusted hinge and closed the door. Looking down at the words
written on the floor, he tilted his head. They were carved with a strong hand,
saying "It's not a good idea to slash at the Acid Blob." The signature
"Yoshitane" can also be read.

"Yoshitsune-san ka... strange name"

116

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 118

Tike chuckled and looked around to see if there were any other graffiti. It
didn't take much time, and he found several similar ones. Tike forgot to eat
and read each one carefully.
Altar: can distinguish curses and blessings
If you fall into a trap, remember its location. Traps are effective against
monsters as well. --Naslura
"If you're hungry, steal from the shop. You'll get more cream pie than you can
eat. —Cline"
Tike gazed intently at the text, reading on. Those who wrote this must have
endured being thrown into a strange dungeon, like Tike, learning one way to
survive at a time. It felt like Tike's lingering sense of loneliness was slightly
eased.

What should I write about now?☆
Holding the magic marker, Tike fell into deep thought. There was so much he
needed to write. Tike's survival until now was never due to mere chance or
good fortune.

Write

117

block-level machine translation prototype · page 119

Tike's stomach growled.
Tike tore open the silver paper that had been held in his hand and bit into the
semi-solid object.
"What is this!"
Bad.
Thin and tasteless. This terrible food Tike had never eaten before. It seems to
have started since coming to the dungeon.
Tike ripped the cap off the marker and began writing vigorously on the floor.
"Do not eat K-rations unless you are on the brink of life and death. -- Tike"

The E command is a somewhat special command. In terms of function, it's
simply "writing text on the floor," and there's nothing problematic about that.
However, whether it actually serves any purpose is something that requires
scratching your head. The written text has no meaning if no one reads it. In
version 98 of NetHack, the game is played solo, and the entire dungeon is
rebuilt every time you create a new character.

However, the floor of the dungeon may contain randomly generated messages.
These could be useful advice or lies that appear truthful. It is up to you to judge
their validity.
The text written on the floor will wear off over time and eventually become
unreadable, and may disappear completely. The time it takes for this to happen
varies depending on how it was written. There are two methods: writing in the
dust accumulated on the floor using fingers or wands, and engraving into the
stone floor using sharp objects like swords. While writing in the dust is easy, the
text can become unreadable after just a few passes over it. Engraving into the
stone lasts several times longer, but the tool used could become damaged and
deteriorate. Regardless of the method used, passing over the text will shorten
its lifespan.

Note: According to unconfirmed information, by engraving a god's name on a
monster and standing on it, you can apparently seal almost all monsters'
attacks.

Commands——Walking Through the Dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 120

C#n

Call, Naming — naming

"chitchitchi"
Yoshitne licked his lips while waving the orc's meat in front of him. The wild
dog in front growled, following the meat with its eyes.

Yoshtene had lost his beloved cat Fommy in a fierce battle with several
Urks-hai on a few levels up. When Fommy was cleaved in half by an Urk-hai
curved sword, it felt like a part of his own body had been taken away.
That time, I thought we would never explore this dungeon with a partner again.
I didn't want to experience such sorrow and pain a second time.
However, now that the sorrow of losing Fomory has faded, Yoshitane came to
realize how great its existence had been to him.
In this harsh and dark dungeon, Formi was the only friend. They fought
together, searched for food, and cautiously approached each other. Sometimes
Yoshitane would distract the shopkeeper so that Formi could steal something
from the front of the shop.

In any case, Fommy was an irreplaceable partner.
"Nobody can take his place"
YoshiTune talked to himself whenever he thought of Fomny. However, it was
certain that a new partner was needed, even if not a replacement. A new
partner to fill the void in his heart.

In such circumstances, Yoshitsune met a wild dog. It likely became feral in the
dungeon, having outlived other adventurers. Despite being a dog, it had
unusually lustrous black fur.
YoshiTune met the dog's eyes and immediately felt that they could be
partners. He couldn't put it into words, but he felt the same sensation he had
when he first met Fommy.

Pulled the oak's flesh out of my backpack and waved it in front of me. Though

"How was it? Good?"
Yoshitsune continued to eat the meat without paying any attention to the
sound of his voice. The black dog seemed to completely ignore his existence.
Yoshitsune shrugged his shoulders and left the room. If he couldn't tame the
black dog, it might become dangerous to keep it around for too long. But he
wasn't willing to take the dog's life either. So, he had no choice but to let it go.

When I touched the handle and opened the door.

Corridors of darkness, several arrows flew at him. Most were deflected by his
crystal plate mail, but one grazed his temple.

"Chizz, what's this. Poison arrows...?"
YoshiTune's mind flashed to the poison arrows used by the orcs, but silhouettes of
monsters were already rising from the darkness. Half-human half-horse forms...

"Centaur! Why in Yendor's dungeon?!"
Kentauros is originally a race that inhabits grasslands and mountains. Even if the
dungeon of Yendor is vast, there is no reason for them to be living in the dungeon.

That moment of hesitation resulted in the centaur's powerful kick hitting
Yoshitane's torso.
The air from within my lungs is completely expelled, leaving me unable to
breathe. My vision blurs, and my consciousness drifts through the void.
In the corner of your vision, you see a centaur's hoof. However, Yoshitsune
cannot even draw his katana.

A black dog was seen biting the shoulder pad of a centaur. Head

Yoshitane swung to clear his mind and drew his katana, slicing it through.
Red droplets of centaur blood flew.

Name

121

block-level machine translation prototype · page 123

The C and #name commands are both used to give unique names to items or
monsters. The C command can be used to give names to monsters.
It might be fun to name your new pet like in Yoshitune, or to give monsters
(that you don't challenge) names for identification during your adventure.

Commands are used to give special names to items such as weapons. This
method can be helpful for easy distinction when you have multiple identical
items. Additionally, some types of "named blades" can be obtained using this
method, though the details are unclear.

#d

Dip

"Head for the spring, Mike"

Clair sighs and speaks to her cat Mike.
Mike showed no particular interest, merely glancing at his master Klein, and
then began grooming enthusiastically with his sharp tongue. Mike's feet were
surrounded by the bones of a dwarf he had just finished eating.
"Ha, when my stomach gets a little full, it's this one."

With a look that said he had had enough, Klein approached the spring bubbling
up at the edge of a small room in the dungeon. The dark blue spring
occasionally made small bubbling sounds.
Look at the sword in Clain's right hand.
The reason why he was usually cheerful was depressed. It was a sword picked
up while walking through the upper floor dungeon, but it was unfortunate to
equip it without examining it carefully. A terrible curse was on the sword. No
matter how much he tried to discard it, the sword wouldn't let go of his hand,
as if it had become part of his right hand.

"I don't have any scrolls to remove curses... What should I do?"
when he muttered those words, suddenly, Alvena's words came to mind.
"Klein, I'll tell you one good thing for you to do when you go down to the dungeon tomorrow."

122

Commands——to walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 124

Albena, who had won a lot of money in a drinking contest with a giant man,
was in a good mood and started talking.
Exploring the dungeon is difficult, but the curses on items are quite
troublesome. The quickest way to remove them is to read a scroll of de-curse,
but there are other methods as well.
"Ah, what a method."

from the tavern, Klein asked.
"Try soaking an item in the fountain in the dungeon. If you're lucky, the curse
will be lifted."
"What happens if I'm unlucky?"
"Sometimes it can turn out like this"
As Albena said and pulled down her garment, three irregular claw marks were
visible from her right shoulder to the valley of her white chest.
" wounded by the water demon's claw."
Not applicable [OCR? chest wound touched with fingers, Albena wore a faint
smile on her lips. Nah—go.]
Clain turned to hear the familiar sound, and Mike was right behind him.

"Alright, wait there. I have no idea what will come out."
Clain slowly immersed the sword in the spring by speaking to the microphone.
Suddenly, someone appeared from the bottom of the spring when the sword
blade touched the water.
The beautiful woman emerged from the water. Her naked body, reminiscent of a pale
maiden, constantly dripped with water droplets, and her hair, reaching to her feet,
emitted blue phosphorescence.

(Oh no! A water nymph!)
Clain tried to flee hastily as he shouted inwardly, but his limbs wouldn't move.
He was mesmerized by the nymph's beauty and couldn't obey his own body.
Even Mike, who dislikes humans, is rubbing his cheek against the nymph's feet.
After some time, when Clain regained his sanity, the nymph's form had
vanished.

soak

123

block-level machine translation prototype · page 125

"Damnit, I got hit."
Checking my inventory, I found several items had disappeared. Even my only
armor, leather armor, had been stolen.
But at that time, Klein noticed that his right hand felt light. Following this,
laughter escaped from Klein's mouth.
"m, this is also a good experience..."
Clain meets the gaze with his right palm.

It seems that the nymphs also stole the cursed sword of sorcery for us.

The #d command to soak items has two uses: soaking items in potions and
soaking items in fountains.
Dipping items in potions is generally not very meaningful. Especially mixing
potions together is usually just vaporizing the potions or turning them into
water. However, there are useful ways to use this, such as holy water blessing
an item simply by dipping it, and poison can create powerful poison arrows by
dipping arrows or other projectiles.

124

Commands—Walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 126

When you dip items in the spring, various effects may occur. Monsters may
appear, or you may receive curses or blessings, indeed very varied. If the
player's character is at a low level, it might be quite a foolish act to dip an item
in the spring just to receive a blessing. It would be wise not to dip unless
absolutely necessary or in a desperate situation. Additionally, it is said that if
you dip a certain sword in the spring, you might obtain the legendary holy
sword Excalibur.

↑

Teleport Teleport

Yoshitsune's consciousness had been dominated by a vague sensation since earlier.

"……I feel awful."
It was so off-kilter that it happened five times within just a dozen steps down
the corridor. Although there was nothing wrong with any part of my body or
nothing painful, I couldn't deny that something was off with my condition.
Still, that can of food must have been bad, right...

Yoshitne had been failing to kill any monsters that looked edible, so he ate a
can of food from the coffin without even checking what was inside.
Then it began. The sensation of having a slight fever started to appear. My
limbs felt as if they were floating, and my head was hazy, unclear.

"Seemed like it had poison——"
Unexpected. When Yoshitsune muttered to himself, he was seized by a
sensation of floating. It was a feeling distinct from dizziness. Something
seemed to be rising from within his body, and his body was being pulled
towards it...
The next moment, Yoshitane noticed that the surroundings had completely
changed. Instead of the corridor he had been walking in just now, he had
arrived at some large hall. Moreover, he could see several killer bees in front of
him.
"Ah, teleport trap!?"

Teleport

125

block-level machine translation prototype · page 127

Although Yoshitsune was startled for an instant, he was still a hard-trained
warrior. In the next moment he drew his katana and split the killer bee in two
with a quick-draw slash.
"Next!"
The moment I raised my katana to slash the bee beside the one I had cut
down, the sensation struck me again.
"?"
The next moment, Yoshitane was surrounded by thousands of gold coins.

"Is this... the treasury of Croesus that I've heard about?"
There were rumors that several safes were hidden in Yendor's dungeon. It was
said that Coloresas, a city magnate, had built a safe there to keep most of his
wealth out of reach of thieves. Yoshitsune was indeed among them. Although
he managed to put some of them in his backpack, it didn't seem like they
would all fit.

"Ah, there's always something around. Though what evil deed would warrant,"

126

Commands—dungeon walking for...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 128

What do you accumulate gold for?

Yoshitsune joked, but he realized immediately that such a situation was
impossible. There was no door in the narrow vault room, only heaped gold
coins that seemed to groan with their presence.

Yositsune searched diligently for the secret door, but all the walls appeared to
be just walls. The door did not seem to appear even after half an hour.
"Stop joking! How am I supposed to survive being locked up in here!"

Shouting, Yoshitane pounded the wall with his fist. Pain shot through his hand,
but he paid no mind and continued pounding the wall. If he were to be defeated
by a monster, well and good, but dying locked in this small room was far from
his desire.

And then, the wall opened, and a man appeared.
"Hm? A face I don't know. Who the hell are you!"
Yositsune raised his head just then, and the man shouted.

"My name is Yoshitane the Samurai..."
"Yostinet? Never heard of it. Who gave you permission to enter this vault?
Throw all the gold coins on the floor and follow me. I'll take you out from here."

Yoshitne had no objections. While he did have some gold coins he had been
carrying around, they would certainly be worth more than rotting in such a
place.
However, just as Yoshitsune tried to take out his wallet from his backpack, he
noticed a familiar sensation bubbling up from the depths of his body.
YoshiTune's eyes fell on the Killerbee's head. And from somewhere, shouts
echoed.
"Thief! You have teleportation ability!"
YoshiTune slashed with his sword at Killerbee while listening to its voice.
It seems that the can contained meat from a monster with teleportation
abilities. That ability has transferred to Yoshitane's body.

Teleport

127

block-level machine translation prototype · page 129

"Such a weak self cannot control such abilities!"
Though I wanted to shout, I restrained myself. This was because I remembered
that the guardman Coloresas was looking for is a powerful warrior.

Teleportation can be obtained by eating creatures with teleportation ability or
by picking up a certain ring. To teleport, press Ctrl and then t. However, having
teleportation ability alone is not enough to teleport to the desired location. The
ability to control teleportation can be obtained by another method.
Additionally, skilled magic users can learn to control teleportation.

However, while teleports cannot always be performed at will, there are also
instances where teleportation abilities activate suddenly, without being
desired. If spontaneous teleports occur while shopping, it can be tragic (or
comedic).

But if you can teleport instead of fleeing when combat weariness sets in, or
use teleportation as a way to conserve movement points, you will be able to
run wild through the dungeon. This advantage is hard to give up.
This teleportation ability is very convenient, but it has one major weakness.
Teleportation consumes a lot of stamina. Even if you are full, you can become
hungry just by teleporting a few times. Be sure to keep this in mind.

#s

Sit

The dungeon was filled with the stench of rusted iron.

Claymore stands among the piled corpses of goblins and oaks.
Panting roughly, Klein sheathed his short sword. Klein's armor was stained
deep crimson with the blood spattered back on it.
"Strange room, Mike"

128

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 130

Klein called out without looking at Mike. Mike paid no attention to Klein and
simply devoted himself to filling his own stomach.
This room has so many goblins and orcs. There might be some great treasure
here.
Winking, Clain approached the long coffin he had kept an eye on during the
battle.
The long chest has a sturdy lock installed.
"hmph. It should work somehow."

Clain mutters to himself while examining the lock from various angles. He
takes a lock pick from his sack and carefully inserts it into the keyhole.

After some trouble, Klein's dexterous fingers slowly rotated the lock pick to the
right, and a faint click was heard as the lock began to unlock.
Klein softly peeked inside the cabinet and let out a disappointed voice.

After checking to make sure there were no traps, I opened the cabinet, but
found only a familiar vial of poison potion inside.
"Damnit! I've struggled for this one only!"
Clain sat down on the dungeon floor after stuffing potions into his sack.
The coldness of the rock feels pleasant.

Clain suddenly noticed that Mike's figure was nowhere to be seen. Looking
around to see where he went, he sensed something moving at the edge of his
vision. It was in the right-hand direction, and it seemed to be close by.
It was a hobgoblin that was moving.
The hobgoblin's body, collapsed as if leaning against something, was swaying faintly.

(Indeed I had defeated it... ...)
While Klein was readjusting his sword, a friend appeared from among the
hobgoblin shadows in the dungeon.
"Your work, Mike?"
Mike did not even look at Klein, but simply continued eating the hobgoblin. He
drove his newly sharpened fangs into the hobgoblin's arm, and Mike shook his
head from side to side. When the hobgoblin's corpse collapsed to the ground,
he rested against its back. [OCR?]
sit

129

block-level machine translation prototype · page 131

The object that had been concealed revealed itself.

It was an elaborately made throne.

The throne was adorned with delicate craftsmanship, its elegant curves
suggesting they were crafted by a master dwarf. Klein suspected it might be a
new trap he had never encountered before and carefully examined the throne
to see if anything was set.

"Nothing here. Boring."
As Clain spoke the words aloud, he firmly sat on the throne, the muttered
words increasing as he explored the dungeon.
"...!"
Claymore took a breath, and a voice echoed through the dungeon.
"hn. What did you say, something about 'summoning'..."

Clain's mouth closed suddenly as if he were interrupted.
The wide dungeon space is filled with black fog.
The spreading darkness moved as if it had life, condensing into the images of
reviled monsters.
Eventually when the darkness fades, the room is filled with an indescribable number of monsters.

130

Commands—walking through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 132

And, filled with their bestial cries.
"Phew, what a hassle. Even undead trolls now?"
Clair drew her still blood-stained sword as she stood up.
"Alright then... let's go, Mike!"
With a battle cry, Klein ran toward the group of monsters.
The micel was still diligently eating.

To sit in the throne while playing NetHack, use the #s command.
If you sit on the throne, nothing may happen immediately, but over time
various effects will appear. These are not always beneficial for the adventurer;
they can sometimes lead to disaster. For example, monsters may appear at
times, or your gold may be completely gone.

Still, it is worth trying to sit on the throne. This is because it can grant the wish
(wish) effect, which allows adventurers to obtain the most desirable items.

If you find the throne, it's a good idea to prepare various things in case of the
worst and sit on it. Unfortunately, however, the throne disappears after you sit
on it a few times, so you can't keep sitting on it forever.

#j

Jump

Mic looked at his master Klein with a curious expression.
Clain is jumping around joyfully, much like fleas on Mike's body.

"Ma, Mike. This is good. Oh, t-t-t"
Clain nearly bumped into Mike and hastily stopped.
Yekk!
Mic cries out in pain as Clayne steps on his long boastful tail.
"Bad bad"
Jump

131

block-level machine translation prototype · page 133

Mike gives an resentful squeak in response to the repentant Klein.
"Sorry I said that. Anyway, this is interesting, Mike. With these jumping boots,
you might be able to move faster than me."

Shifting my gaze from the satisfied Clain, Mike moved to a corner of the room.
It seemed he had wisely decided to distance himself from this fidgety master
for now.
Mike turned around and found Klein still jumping around in his jumping boots
without getting tired. Mike looked around to see if there was anything nearby
that he could play with, but aside from a decayed jackal's corpse, there was
nothing that piqued his interest.

At that time, Mike sniffed and detected a scent like that of an animal around
him.
Mike turned just in time to see a giant foot swing down in front of him.

Fghyaaaaaaar!
Quickly dodging the enemy was good, but he let out a scream as his recently
stepped-on tail was stepped on again.
"What's up, Mike."
Mike looked towards Mike, seeing Mike frantically running towards Clayne.
Behind it was a huge shadow.
"Oh, it's an ogre!"
As Orgs approach, Klein steps back.
Then, suddenly, the ground beneath Klein's feet collapsed. His body fell into a
deep hole.
Whilegrimacing in pain, Klein looked up to see Mike peering in worriedly from
the edge of the pit.
"Damnit. A trap at a time like this... wait, I can use this now..."
When the sound of Orga's footsteps approached, Klein put all his strength into
his legs and jumped off the ground. Klein's body floated through the pit trap
and rolled in front of Mike. Then, a loud noise echoed through the dungeon.

132

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 134

.
"Phew, that was close"

Clain looks at the pit, panting heavily.
A ogre trapped by a trap was there. The ogre was showing its sharp pickaxe
teeth and letting out a roar of anger.
"Alright then. Mike, I'll make some bait for you now."

Mike responds with a happy squeak.
Clain changed his weapon to a bow and shot an arrow soaked with poison at
the ogre.

If you can jump, you can use the #j command to make your character jump.

By jumping, you will be able to easily escape from monsters and also from
certain traps. However, when wearing Jumping Boots, be sure not to jump if
you have too many items, as their weight may prevent you from jumping.

Jump

133

block-level machine translation prototype · page 135

#m

Monster's ability

Shaking vibrations are causing the dungeon to tremble.

A red dragon, wrapped in dark crimson scales, was walking through the
dungeon of Yendor.
Strange as it was, there was a Mike's form behind the dragon. The grown Mike,
larger than a leopard but still small, walked gracefully following the dragon.
"Mike......"
A voice emerges from the mouth of a dragon with rows of sharp teeth.

The dragon's voice was strangely hoarse due to the difference in vocal organs,
but there was no mistake—it was definitely Klein's voice.
" I heard there was a ring that could change into a monster... And it turns out
it's this one. "
Clair then looks at the gold and silver rings on the tips of her sharp claws.

In the dim light, two rings with strange magical power shine.
Huff!——!
Suddenly, the mic emitted a noise like when a monster roars at an enemy.

Claymore lowered its long neck and looked forward, and a monster was
seen. Monster.
A monstrous creature of humanoid form had a grotesque bull's head growing on its body.
Its deep red eyes, beneath crooked horns that were bent and twisted, stared intently at
Clark.

(Minotaur!)
Clain cries out in his chest.
The minotaur charged straight at Klein. Thick, powerful-looking bundles of
muscle writhed beneath the minotaur's reddish-copper skin.
(Usually I would run away, but... this time it's different!)
Opening its mouth with fangs interlocked, a flame of scarlet fire burst towards
the minotaur from Klein's oral cavity.
In an instant, the surroundings are filled with intense light.

134

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 136

Thin and slit pupils of almond shape reflected a Minotaur approaching without
flinching from the flame, even though half its left side was blackened by burns.

Clain stopped attacking with his fire breath and tried to bite the Minotaur with
sharp fangs. Clain bites the outstretched left arm of the Minotaur, which is
protecting its head. When Clain shakes his head from side to side, the left arm
is torn off at the shoulder socket with a strange sound.

Seeing the Minotaur spray fresh blood, Klein confirmed his victory. However, at
that moment, intense pain erupted in his left chest. Upon inspection, he found
that the Minotaur's right arm had struck him, piercing through his tough scales
and deeply embedding itself in his left chest.
"Guuaaaa—!"
The dragon's painful roar shakes the air.
Minotaurus still buried his right arm in Klein's chest and tried to crush his giant
heart.

135

block-level machine translation prototype · page 137

In his fading consciousness, Klayn's body underwent a sudden change, having
resigned himself to death. As the outline of the dragon's form blurred, his
massive limbs gradually shrunk.
"Hey?"
Noticing, Klein had returned to his familiar body. He checked his left chest, but
there didn't seem to be any injuries.
Looking up, I noticed the minotaur was scanning its surroundings. It seems it
is looking for where the dragon we were fighting has gone.
Minotaur suddenly lowered its gaze and spotted Klein. After a moment of
silence, it roared, revealing sharp teeth from its burned mouth.
"Ma, mic... I'm running away!"
Shouting, Klein fled with the microphone.

If you are playing as a human character in the dungeon, you might also enjoy
transforming into the monsters that live in the dungeon to play.
The easiest method is to wear the Ring of Teleportation and the Ring of
Polymorph on both hands. The effect does not appear immediately, but after a
while, it will ask what monster you want to transform into, so just input the
name of the desired monster.

When the transformed monster has special abilities, you can activate them
using the #m command.
For example, if you polymorph into a Red Dragon, you can use the #m
command to make flame attacks possible, and if you polymorph into a
Werewolf, you can summon your wolf companions.
In any case, you should use #m to check the special abilities of the monster
you have transformed into. This will likely make your NetHack experience
more comfortable.

136

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 138

#w

Wipe Face

"What the hell is going on!"
Clain was running through the dungeon.

His and Mike's bodies are covered with white cream and torn pie crust.

Reach the door of the room and try to open it, but find it locked. Quickly take
out the lockpick and Klein starts picking the lock.
A man in strange clothing ran out from the door behind where Klein came in.
As soon as they spotted Klein, they started throwing cream pies at him. One of
the pies hit Klein's face as he turned around.

"mu"s
Clain wipes the cream stuck to his face with his right hand. Mike vigorously
shakes his head from side to side, knocking pies off.
Is that what was wrong with it?
Clain mutters as he opens the door.
This strange escape began with an incident at a certain store.
"Hello, Klein! You've come to Tom's scroll shop again."
Clein, who was exploring the dungeon, opened the door leading to a room and
was greeted by a cheerful voice.
Surprised, I stood before a tall, clearly elderly man. His body was clad in black
robes, revealing gaunt arms covered in wrinkles and resembling withered
branches.
A elderly man with a calm smile stood behind countless scrolls carelessly laid
on the ground. Clain cautiously entered the store and picked up one of the
scrolls at his feet, asking for the price of the scroll he had taken.

"Expensive. What kind of scroll is this?"
Clain unfolds a scroll and mutters words that are unclear as he traces them.
Wipe face

137

block-level machine translation prototype · page 139

ended.
"O, no. That... "
Simultaneously hearing the elderly man's panicked voice, the scenery around
began to blur. A moment later, Claen had teleported to a different location
from the shop.
"Phew. Glad I got the reins on Mike." Mike is blinking his eyes and
looking around.
--Suddenly an alarm rang out in the dungeon.

"What is this sound?"
Klein muttered while frowning at the noise.
Dark room, the sound of a door opening somewhere.

And... the first cream pie hit Klein's profile as he stood there.
If we don't do something, we'll end up as human cream pie.

Now the cream pie sticks seamlessly to Clain's body, resembling a cream pie sticking.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 140

presented a mummy-like appearance with its wrappings.

Behind Klein and Mike, as before, there are people wearing strange clothes
chasing them. As Klein, cornered, opens door after door, suddenly a small
shadow blocks Klein's way.
"Are you going to pay, then? Or do you want to eat more cream pie?"

The owner of the scroll seller wore a black robe. The smile of goodwill had vanished
from the old man's face, and all that came from his mouth was a dry, cold voice.

It seems to have returned to the scroll shop while running around.
Behind the old man were men armed with cream pie and rubber hose, waiting
for Clayne's reply.
"Understood understood"

Sighing, Klein pays the scroll price to the old man.
When Clayne pays, the men disappear in an instant, leaving the old man
behind.
What was that... I wonder...
In front of weakly whispering Klein, the scroll seller's owner began to calculate the money.

Mike next to Clayne was licking cream off his face enthusiastically.

Wipe your face using the #w command.
Using the #w command usually cleans just your face, but you can use it to exit
a blind state when your vision is blocked by cream pie or similar items.

This #w command may not have many opportunities to be used, but it's not a
bad idea to remember it in case you find yourself in any situation in the
dungeon.

Wipe Face

139

block-level machine translation prototype · page 141

#r

Rub

Thieves' Guild in the powerful evil city of Bals.
In one corner of Zaldos Street, which had become almost a lawless district due
to its poor governance, there was an inn called Gold Horn Tavern.
On the way back from guild training, when Klein opened the door to the tavern
to head home after drinking a few drinks, he found the place unusually
crowded that day. He squeezed into an available seat and was drinking bad
wine when a new customer sat down next to Klein.

"Kekkeke. Another rare guest has come, I suppose."
Bard Tike, who is also the owner of the tavern "Golden Horn Flute Inn," spoke
in a vulgar voice. As Gross's laughter echoed, the fat body of the man shook
up and down.
Claire placed a pitcher of foaming ale on the table, and when she glanced over
to her companion, she saw the traveler removing her dirty leather cloak.
Beneath the hood, her magnificent golden hair cascaded down.
"Long time no see, Gloss."

Woman calls out in gloss.
"Exactly. Anyway, let's go for a full pickup."
He poured the wine into a silver glass that was rarely used. After drinking the
amber-colored wine in one gulp, the woman wiped her wet lips with her left
hand and smiled.
Gloss and female were talking for a while, but when a new customer came in,
Gross went to take the order reluctantly. The female took a heavy wine jug in
her right hand and began pouring the second drink.
Clain was staring at the woman when he saw a loud noise coming from a table
near the entrance of the shop. Turning around, he saw a wooden chair flying
towards a woman next to him. Clain instantly stretched his left arm out and
caught the chair in mid-air.
Clain let out a sigh of relief and looked in the direction the chair had flown, seeing two men and women.

140

Commands—To walk through the dungeon...

block-level machine translation prototype · page 142

They stood facing each other with weapons. One was Regulos, a regular customer known
as the Drunkard Priest. The other was a Walhalla maiden with a refined northern
appearance.

"Even if you are a woman, I shall not forgive those who insult my god!"
Regulos raises his voice, slightly nasal from the wine.
Another session, I see. This always happens when I get drunk.

Clain mutters something under his breath.

"Hey Clain. You've known Rege since childhood, so please help out. The
opponent is Walhalla's warrior Layla."
Clair made a face at Gross as he hurriedly came back.
"Do something already. When he drinks alcohol... "
During the time the two exchanged words, Reglos and Reira's swords clashed
twice or thrice, sending small sparks flying. The spectators gathered around
Reglos and Reira didn't try to stop them; instead, they started betting on
which of the two would win. They shouted encouragement or insults to their
respective opponents who had placed bets on them.
"Glory, use this."
Gross caught the old-fashioned lamp the woman threw at him.

"Ah, a magic lamp!"
"Instead, all your debts up until now are forgiven."

Gloss is smiling and nodding, then starts rubbing the lamp in his hand. Smoke,
a dark gray color, begins to rise from the lamp. The smoke spreads and
gradually forms the shape of a large man.
"……I am your master. Please give me your command."

A voice echoes from deep underground, coming from the giant's mouth.

"Yendor, please eject the two causing trouble outside the shop."

"as you wish"
Jin casually approached Regos and Releth, grabbing both by the collars with his
thick arms. Disregarding their struggles, Jin threw them towards the open
entrance of the door.
"Thanks for the tip earlier"

The woman said this while pouring liquor into Klein's cup.

"What is your name? I am Klein."
hit

141

block-level machine translation prototype · page 143

"Albena... they call her that."
