also creatures that can resist attacks from spores if you defeat and eat these
mycelium. They might not be worth ignoring.
Violet fungus, among the mycorrhizae, seems to have a different flavor. This
organism, despite being a mycorrhiza, can move on its own and even
communicate with us telepathically! It's like a manticore...

Gnome

gnome, ~lord, ~king, gnomish wizard

Gnome (nom) is said to be a humanoid closely related to dwarves. Like
dwarves, they form clans and often dig holes in the earth to build
comfortable dwellings where they live. They are generally smaller than
dwarves and often possess the qualities of brave warriors.
Why they reside in Yendor's dungeon is unknown. Perhaps they are trying to
repel intruders out of gratitude for providing their ideal dwelling place to
Yendor.
They also exist in various forms, just like humans. There are good gnomes as
well as those with evil hearts. In other words, just as there are humans
serving as guardians of Yendor, there are gnomes as well.
Gnomes are found on the surface and aren't particularly formidable foes, but
gnome lords and gnome kings, who lead their clans, and gnomish wizards
who can use magic, are quite strong enemies. However, it seems they don't
possess particularly high magical skills.

Giant Men

giant, stone~, hill~, fire~, frost~,
minotaur, owlbear, ettin, titan

They are called giants, but they have different personalities. Here, we will
explain by dividing them into several groups.

● GIANT
First, let us discuss the so-called giants. Giants are humanoids like humans
and elves; they are simply enormous in body.

210

MONSTERS—monsters in the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 212

is not over yet.
In modern times, they rarely appear in public, so they are treated as
legendary creatures. However, this is a mistaken perception. Giants include
common giants (giant), stone giants (stone giant) that prefer rocky areas, hill
giants (hill giant) that inhabit hilly regions, fire giants (fire giant) that live in
tropical or volcanic areas, and frost giants (frost giant) that inhabit cold
regions. Their forms can also be seen in the dungeons of Yendor. It is
unknown why they choose to reside in the dungeons of Yendor, despite
loving nature.

Eating their flesh is said to transfer their abilities. Many become known for
their abnormal strength.
● MINOTAUR

Minotaur (minotaur) is a half-bull half-human monster. It resides in dungeons
and caves, boasting such tremendous strength that it can chop adventurers
in half with its huge axe, even through armor. Its intelligence is low and it is
violent, making it one of the most troublesome opponents for adventurers.
● OWLBEAR

Owlbear (owlbear) is a monster synthesized by ancient magic from a bear
and an owl. It suffocates its enemies by hugging them with its powerful body.
If you encounter an owlbear in Yendor's dungeon, it's best to forget about
running away.
● OTHER GIANTS

Titan (titan) are said to be descendants of the ultra-ancient gods, but the
details are shrouded in mystery and largely unknown. The same can be said
for ettin (ettin).

Stalker

stalker

Stalker (stalker) is a monster summoned from another dimension by magic
users. They will stand in the adventurer's way obeying Yendor's commands
until the moment they vanish due to Yendor's magic.
Their greatest feature is being invisible. In many cases, their first strike is a
surprise attack on the adventurer. Even after it becomes clear that they have
been attacked, the lack of visibility of the "stalker" makes it difficult to deal with
them.
Gnomes. Giants. Stalker.

211

block-level machine translation prototype · page 213

gnome

minotaur

giant

jabberwock

Guard

YUKI /
HIRO
YUKI /
HIRO

block-level machine translation prototype · page 214

It would be a terrible invasion. It could be said to be a fearsome attacker.

However, being transparent means nothing in front of elves' amazing vision,
and those who gain the "see invisible" ability in some way can see their form.
There are rumors that eating the flesh of a stalker can make you
transparent, but this comes with considerable danger. The effects of an
extradimensional lifeform's flesh on us are completely unknown.

JabbaWoak

jabberwock

A mysterious existence. It seems to be a monster with considerable power, and the fact that
sightings are extremely rare is what makes the Jabberwock a mysterious existence.

Based on limited sightings, it appears to be a creature relatively close to
dragons. It has wings similar to those of a dragon and can almost certainly
fly. Its claws and teeth are extremely sharp, making it likely a carnivore that
would attack humans. Additionally, it is reported to have excellent eyesight,
with eyes glowing like flames.
In rare cases, sightings have been reported on the surface as well, mostly of
figures appearing swayingly from within the woods. Additionally, some
reports mention a sound like bubbles rising and foaming as they approach.
While some sightings have been reported, the actual ecology and personality
of these creatures remain unknown. The illustrations in this book are merely
imagined depictions, and their true form is uncertain. It may not be easy to
take precautions, but if you encounter a dragon-like creature with sharp
fangs and claws, treat it as a formidable opponent.

Keystone Cop, Cop Sergeant, Lieutenant, Captain
Shopkeepers are hired guards patrolling a shop in Yendor's dungeon. They
exist to make customers who attempt to steal valuable items from the shop
pay the price. If an alarm rings in the dungeon, be cautious, as it may mean
the shopkeepers have appeared at the request of the shop owner. As long as
a criminal is present, they will pursue him wherever he goes.

JabbaWock. guard

213

block-level machine translation prototype · page 215

Security guards arrive, each holding a cream pie in one hand and a rubber
hose in the other. They blindfold the culprit by throwing the terrifying cream
pies and subsequently punish them for the great crime of shoplifting using
the fearsome weapon, the rubber hose.
However, if pickpockets genuinely repent and pay compensation to the
shopkeeper, they would obediently disappear (though with a grunt). It goes
without saying that they should vanish just as suddenly when they disappear as
when they appeared.

Richi

lich, demi~, master~

Richchi is a general term for sorcerers or high priests who gained eternal life
through ancient great magical arts or by the power of a wicked deity.
Because their cursed life spans half the realm of death and the other half of
the present world, their souls are immortal in the mortal world.
Their magic power, which has become formidable through the will to
manipulate the forbidden laws of immortality, can be summed up in one
word: spectacular. Because they are immortal, they spend aeons studying
magic. As a result, they can control all magic, summon storms, and create
infernos.
And those who touched its body were drained of all their essence by Ricty's
overwhelming power, and anything touched by just its fingers would be
cursed. In the depths of Yendor's dungeon, Ricty is one of the most feared
beings.
As mentioned earlier, the Richies possess half of the essence of life in the realm of
death. Therefore, it is also argued that their bodies cannot be harmed by ordinary
weapons, and only weapons with magical power can injure them.

Mummy

mummy kobold, ~gnome, ~orc, ~elf,
~human, ~ettin, ~giant

Mummies are those of buried beings who have a grudge against this world,
resurrecting only their bodies. Alternatively, there are traditions in certain
regions where the bodies of the dead will resurrect after a certain period of
time has passed. However, it seems that due to the failure of this
resurrection, they turn into monsters and attack people.
They hate living things for their inability to obtain the souls of the living, so
they attack all living beings. They attack kobolds, gnomes, and orcs.
214

MONSTERS—monsters that wander the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 216

Such races that become mummies retain their power similar to when they
were alive. However, giant mummies are sometimes discovered. They also
possess the same power as when they were alive, namely, enormous bodies
and abnormal strength. In particular, one should be cautious of mummy
ettins and mummy giants.
In Yendor's dungeon, mummies seem to be produced by the magic of that mage.
It is impossible to consider that only those arising from resentment or failures in
body resurrection are wandering around, given the number of mummies far
exceeding what would be expected.

Those who die while wearing bandages obtained by destroying them turn
into mummies.

N Nagga

red naga, black~, golden~, guardian~ red naga
hatchling, black~, golden~, guardian~

If you find a monster with the upper body of a human woman and the lower
body of a snake, it is a Naga (naga). There are some similarities between
dragons and nagas, such as the fact that they can breathe dragon breath
and are distinguished by the color of their scales. It is also confirmed that
they are oviparous, and several Naga hatchlings have been found in the
middle levels of the dungeon, similar to dragon hatchlings. However, Naga
hatchlings cannot use their special abilities and seem to use only their young
fangs as weapons.

Various creatures have been confirmed to exist, such as those that spit venom,
those with fangs containing paralyzing poison, and those that can wield magic. It
seems that the ones that breathe fire have red scales, but the specifics of the
other nagas' ecology remain unclear. Most of the aforementioned characteristics
are based on sightings only, so approach with that in mind.

In Yendor's dungeon, its presence has been confirmed from the middle levels
to the deeper levels. Guardian nagas are known to be powerful entities, but
details are unclear as they are found too deep down.

Ogre

orge, ~lord, ~king

Ogre. That is an ogre (orge). There may not be a more accurate term for it.
Its body far surpasses that of a human, and its arm thickness reaches the
size of an adult's torso. It boasts such strength that it can easily knock down
humans with a single punch.

Richi. Mummy. Naga. Oghma.

215

block-level machine translation prototype · page 217

lich

mummy

orge

YEKI /
YIRO

yeki /
YIRO

black puding

naga

YEKI /
YIRO

block-level machine translation prototype · page 218

They wrap themselves in tattered loincloths and prefer to swing clubs. It's not
that they particularly like clubs; some say this is because their fingers are
clumsy and clubs are the easiest weapons for them to handle. However,
despite successful observations by scholars who studied ogres in detail, the
exact reasons have become unclear as all the details were consumed by the
ogres.
Ogres are carnivorous and particularly enjoy eating human flesh. It's not
uncommon for them to bite off a live person's head. In fact, ogres eat
humans just as vegetables are eaten by other creatures. Their sharp teeth
can regrow when broken, so an ogre never runs out of teeth throughout its
lifetime.
In Yendor's dungeon, its existence is confirmed relatively close to the
surface, unlike oaks, and it often acts alone. Conversation is possible, but it
doesn't have much intelligence. As long as the adventurer has sufficient
strength, it should not be a threat.
However, forming a tribe is rare, and there may be beings who unify the
tribe or a king, etc.

Slime-like Creature

gray ooze, brown pudding, black ~

Gray Ooze, Brown Pudding, and Black Pudding are known as dungeon
cleaners. They ingest and digest corpses, weapons, and armor left by
adventurers. Gray Ooze and Black Pudding can dissolve metal items like
swords and plate mail, while Brown Pudding ingests leather and cloth. There
are rumors of places where these creatures are kept in garbage dumps for
this purpose. However, they occasionally undergo abnormal reproduction and
become uncontrollable, so caution is advised.

Basically, slimes have nothing that can be called intelligence; they merely
exist as living things. However, if they are in the direction an adventurer
needs to travel, you may have to fight them. When you must fight them, do
not use corrosive weapons. Weapons made of materials that rust or rot will
quickly corrode and become dull when they strike these slimes. Also,
because they resist acid, poison, and cold, defeating them should be very
difficult. However, many slime-like creatures are confirmed to be weak
against fire. Magical fireballs or a wand of fire would be useful.

slime-like creature

217

block-level machine translation prototype · page 219

Closing the door is pointless when trying to escape from slime threats. Slimes
are amorphous creatures and it has been confirmed that they can flow
through gaps in the door.

Q

Quantum Physicist

quantum mechanics

They seem to manipulate a system of magic that does not fit any known
magical concept. Those who touch them are said to be transported through
space and end up somewhere unknown. However, this is certainly not the
time-space transcending technique we know. We have no way of knowing
how they do it, but they appear to possess and utilize the logic of this world
that they have constructed with their hands.

Why they exist in Yendor's dungeon is completely unknown. There are also
rumors that they are conducting some kind of experiment in Yendor's
dungeon. It seems that their bodies are constantly contaminated by toxins
from radioactive heavy metals due to long-term experiments. Therefore, it is
very dangerous to eat the corpses of quantum physicists.

R

Acid monster

rust monster

Larger than humans but moves quickly. Has dark brown skin and eyes that
resemble emeralds. This monster's food is metal. It is rumored to prefer
steel, but this has not been confirmed. Also said to swim gracefully in water,
but neither of these claims have been verified.
This monster is reported to be unaffected by metallic weapons or armor. Its
skin has the ability to oxidize all metals. An adventurer witnessed a sword
that had been sharpened just an hour ago rusting instantly upon impact.
There are also reports of adventurers seeing a spear corrode upon touching
the monster's skin.
Defense works similarly. It seems that just touching the tentacles growing
from this creature's head causes metallic armor to degrade. Since it doesn't
have fangs or claws, it won't inflict wounds on the body, but the defense of
armor and helmets will be reduced, making it disadvantageous when fighting
other monsters.
Methods of attacking: using weapons and armor that never degrade.
218

MONSTERS—monsters in the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 220

isn't it?
This monster's reputation among adventurers is rather bad. It apparently
doesn't appreciate indirect bullying. That's reasonable.

Snake

snake, garter~, water moccasin,
pit viper, cobra, python

Snakes have been detested in this world since ancient times. The exact
reason is unknown, but according to legend, they played a significant role in
driving humans out of Eden. It is said that a certain god, enraged by the
destruction of Eden, cursed the snakes. As a result, there are legends that
say snakes lost their limbs and were forced to live in a strange form. The
facts are unclear, and no one knows about the gods and the immortal
"saints" who have survived from that time.
Regardless, snakes and their kind are despised and outcasts in the world.
They might be considered the closest beings to Yendor in some sense.
Snakes exist in various places in the dungeon and often attack from
unexpected locations. Sometimes they hide under items or boxes in the
dungeon, striking when an adventurer touches them. Attacks consist mainly
of bites and the poison they deliver. According to adventurers, bites
themselves do not cause much damage, but the poison is terrible. Without
the ability to counter them, these attacks can be fatal, and many have died
in agony from snake venom. Snakes are formidable creatures that should not
be underestimated.

Troll

troll, ice~, rock~, water~, Olog-hai

A species of giant. In the dungeon, there are common trolls (troll), water
trolls (water troll) that live in water and drag adventurers in, ice trolls (ice
troll) whose bodies are as cold as ice, and rock trolls (rock troll) that appear
by destroying walls.
Olog-hai (ogres enhanced by dark sorcery) are said to be wandering around,
but official reports have not been made. They are cruel and consider eating
raw meat their greatest pleasure. Their only weakness is that they turn to
stone if exposed to direct sunlight, but there is no hope of solar aid in dark
dungeons.
They are immortal beings. Even if you succeed in defeating them, make sure to properly dispose of the body.

Quantum physicist. Oxidation monster. Snake. Troll

219

block-level machine translation prototype · page 221

rust monster
Quantum Physicist

troll
umber hulk

cobra

YUKI
HIRO

block-level machine translation prototype · page 222

Until you have reached a certain level and have sufficient weapons and
armor, trolls are dangerous. If you succeed in defeating a troll, it is better to
eat the corpse quickly or run away before it revives.

Trolls' weapons are claws and teeth. Claws can be defended against if
adequate armor is worn, but teeth can inflict unexpected injuries on
adventurers. If you fail to dispose of a troll's body and are attacked
repeatedly, you may suffer excessive bleeding, so caution is necessary.
SectionHeader An amber Hulk

umber hulk

A large man with brown skin. Details regarding appearance are unknown.

This report on this monster is scarce, but there is one common point. "Be
wary of their gaze." The severity of the symptoms varies among the
reporters, but it seems that when they are stared at, they are overcome by a
strong sense of self-deprecation. They begin to question why they are
fighting this monster, why they are in such a place, why they descended into
the dungeon, and ultimately, why they are still alive. They wonder what they
are striving for.
The theme of why you are alive might be something an adventurer considers
once. However, it is dangerous to have such thoughts in front of monsters.
Adventurers may become confused and unable to take normal actions. By
the time you realize it, you might already be severely injured. Caution is
necessary.
It seems they attack with claws and fangs other than by sight, but it's
unclear what effects they have. Adventurers attacked by monsters are
confused and cannot deliver accurate strikes.

Vampire

vampire, ~lord, Vlad the Impaler

Vampire (vampire) differs from other monsters in that it was not summoned
from Yendor or enhanced by magic. It seems to have appeared from the east
desert and settled in the dungeon when the demonic creatures of Yendor
gathered there. Of course, it pays respect to Yendor and follows its
commands, but it is not a direct subordinate. It is treated more like a guest.
It can be said to be a peculiar existence within the dungeon.
Their height is one or two heads taller than humans. They move agilely, and a crawling
attack can easily be dodged. Their weapons have claws, but their main weapon is [OCR? ...].

Hulk Amber, Vampire

221

block-level machine translation prototype · page 223

It is indeed the fangs. They gain vitality by sucking the lifeblood of adventurers.

Adventurers bitten by the carotid artery not only suffer physical damage but
also mental damage. Their accumulated mental energy is stolen by
continuing to fight. Mental energy encompasses not just spiritual elements
like experience and knowledge, but also the vitality that supports the body.
Having energy drained means that the adventurer's abilities will decline.
To prevent vampire attacks, one must prepare items that they are weak against. These
items are passed down orally and it is strictly forbidden to write them down.

In Vampire society, there is a hierarchy, and one must unconditionally obey
the commands of those above them in rank. Vampire Lords (vampire load)
are among the upper ranks within vampires. They are stronger than other
vampires and have higher magical abilities. It is rumored that there are
vampires even surpassing Vampire Lords, but their true nature has not been
confirmed.

Ghoul

barrow wight, wraith, Nazgul

Ghosts are among the very dangerous entities in Yendor's dungeon.
Although their true nature remains unclear, their intelligence has been
confirmed by several reports. Adventurers have witnessed ghosts attacking
prey with a sinister smile. Some say they were the resentments of
adventurers who died without achieving victory. In Yendor's dungeon, the
resentments of those taken captive can sometimes take over the body of a
buried king, and at other times manifest as new entities blocking the path of
new adventurers.
The power of hatred is immense, and even experienced adventurers who are
fully prepared cannot be guaranteed absolute safety. If some of them touch
you, or if their swords merely graze your arm, they can drain the energy you
should have built up through continued fighting. Strength fades and stamina
decreases. Many adventurers shrink by two sizes, and white hairs begin to
appear in their hair.
Some ghosts may use strange fog to induce sleep and exhaust adventurers,
or cast spells to wound them.
Equipping yourself sufficiently is necessary to fight skeletons. "If your
equipment is insufficient, run away. You should not fight skeletons without
absolute confidence. Otherwise, you may be taken by a skeleton and unable
to achieve reincarnation," the adventurer training manual warns in blood
letters.
222

MONSTERS——monsters that wander the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 224

X Sohn

xorn

Mysterious monster.

While they may not match dragons, the lack of information about them is
unusual compared to ordinary monsters. The only things that are definitely
known are that their weapons are claws and fangs, and they have incredibly
thick skin. Otherwise, reports are little more than rumors.
Reports on wall-penetrating ability are also included among unreliable
reports. According to one adventurer, they have the ability to penetrate
walls. He was resting in a room to heal his wounds. The room had only one
door, which was locked. It was designed to alert him if a monster tried to
open the door, revealing the presence of monsters. Thus, he rested in peace.
However, his rest was interrupted by Soth's appearance. Fortunately, he
managed to escape before making eye contact with Soth, so he did not suffer
fatal injuries. Nevertheless, it remains unknown how Soth entered and
attacked him, as he opened the only door when fleeing and there were no
signs of tunnel digging. Desperate, he wrote in his adventurer's diary, "It
might have come through the wall."

This report from the adventurer has been followed by many similar reports of
attacks by souns. However, because it is such an unbelievable story, it is not
recorded in the official reports. It remains nothing more than rumors.
It will likely take quite some time for brave adventurers to accurately report
its ecology.

Yeti

yeti

Yeti (yeti) ecology has detailed reports, which can be found in the adventurer
training school library. They have a large body, comparable to giants in size
and strength. Except for the face, hands, and feet, their entire body is
covered in white fur. Their skin is thin, and even an adventurer's sword can
penetrate it. Intelligence has been confirmed, but at a low level. They do not
use writing and have a limited vocabulary for conversation. They often travel
in groups of 10 to 20, but do not hunt in groups. When seeking food, they
mostly act alone.

Ghosts. Son. Yeti

223

block-level machine translation prototype · page 225

yeti

barrow wight

yuk /
Hiro

vampire lord

zombie

giant ant

yuk /
Hiro

block-level machine translation prototype · page 226

Yeti is originally a monster that inhabits uninhabited high mountains, but some
have wandered into the dungeon in response to Yendor's request. They have
excellent cold resistance, so attacks of a cooling nature will not affect them.

Yeti's main weapons are claws and teeth. Swing arms wildly with force, so it's
important to gauge the opponent's movements to attack effectively. Due to
its large body, there are many parts that can be eaten. It might become an
unexpected ally when hungry.
Finally, the text concludes that "while a group would be a formidable
opponent, a single one could be fought to at least even terms by an
adventurer who has just graduated from a training facility."

Zombie

kobold zombie, gnome~, orc~,
elf~, human~, ettin~, giant~

A zombie is a corpse given false life by Yendor's magic. There does not seem
to be any particular causal relationship between race and becoming a
zombie. In fact, it has been confirmed that humans, kobolds, orcs, gnomes,
giants, and even elves can become zombies.
They have no thought. They cannot see, hear, or feel. Actions are almost
reflexive, and zombies never attack adventurers on their own initiative or
pick up dropped items. They are living corpses.

Zombies are not undead. Their weakness is the head. They lack thinking ability, but if the
brain marrow is crushed, their obsession is lost and the zombie becomes incapacitated.

Their attacks are primarily with claws. Zombies themselves are not
particularly strong opponents, but you can take unexpected damage if you're
not careful. Especially new adventurers should be cautious.
The body of a zombie is rotting, so it's best not to think about eating the
corpse (?). It can involve risks and be dangerous.

Insect

giant ant, soldier ant, fire ant,
giant beetle, killer bee, queen bee

Insects in Yendor's dungeon are cruel and ferocious. Enhanced by Yendor's
magic, they are far more powerful than their counterparts on the surface.
Ants have large fangs, bees have much sharper stingers, and being bitten by
a golden bug can tear through skin and flesh.

Zombie. Insect.

225

block-level machine translation prototype · page 227

Ants can inflict damage on adventurers with bites that cause heat and fire,
making them more dangerous than generally thought. Their presence is
noted in the adventurer training manuals, which state, "Do not underestimate
insects. In the early stages of adventure, they will be the most formidable
enemies."
They act in groups, which is terrifying. Ant-like monsters often attack
adventurers in groups when seeking food. If an adventurer is struggling to
defeat just one, they may be left with no escape route and completely
surrounded, leading to a gruesome death. Corpses ravaged by ants are
scattered throughout the dungeon.

Insects can be dangerous creatures for adventurers, but they sometimes
bring incredible good fortune. When you encounter a large swarm of bees,
you should not retreat. Several reports confirm that they can bestow
unexpected power upon victory.

Liquid Creatures

acid blob, quivering blob, gelatinous cube

Mysterious creature covered in liquid or foam. Produced by Yendor's magic, it
can only survive in the dungeon. Its shape varies, sometimes becoming
spherical small blocks or cubes. Generally smaller than humans, but
occasionally larger individuals exist, surprising adventurers. Eyes and ears
are mostly black, and it emits a smell like rotting fish.
Their attacks are made using parts of their bodies. Some corrode swords and
armor with acid bubbles and dissolve adventurers' bodies; others split an
opponent's body open merely by touching it. Sometimes they paralyze the
body and render the victim unable to act. The wounds themselves will not be
fatal once you have enough experience, but an indescribable unpleasantness
remains. While fighting, you may be entangled by their sticky bodies, and
even if you manage to defeat them, a foul odor lingers stubbornly. To remove
it completely, you must wash your body with clean spring water for at least
three days.

Adventurers have devised various methods to combat their stench, and it's
detailed enough to fill an entire book. The efforts of predecessors are documented
in "Some Strategies for Maintaining Cleanliness in the Dungeon" at the Adventurer
Training Institute.

226

MONSTERS—monsters in the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 228

Cockatrice

cockatrice

fire snake
A strange monster with the body of a snake and the head, feathers, and legs of a
rooster. Larger than a human but not as large as a dragon. It emits a roar that
even demons tremble at while flying through the air. It is said that its appearance
is even more bizarre than a snake flying through the air.

Originally living far from the dungeon in a desert, it moved its residence in
response to Yendor's summoning. There is little known about its true form. It
is said to be the king of snakes, and all snakes are said to flee upon hearing
the cry of a cockatrice, but no witnesses have been reported. This is because
anyone present would not survive encountering it.
The largest weapon of a cockatrice seems to be its body. Just touching its dull
brown scales can turn adventurers into stone. Additionally, even if an
adventurer manages to strike the cockatrice's body successfully, magic
power flows through any weapon used and turns their body to stone.
Methods to defeat a cockatrice have been studied, but no official battle
tactics have been officially published. It is also said that magic is effective,
but the truth of this claim is unknown.
It is better not to eat the carcass of a cockatrice even if you are starving. It is
said that the cockatrice's magic remains in the corpse. The result can easily
be imagined.

Dog·Dog-like Monsters

dog, little~, large~, jackal, ~were, warg,
wolf, ~were, winter~, hell hound, pup

Dogs are among the animals closest to adventurers. On the surface, they live
closely with humans not only as pets but also as hunting dogs and guard
dogs. The same applies to the dungeon. Dogs are trained at kennels to guide
adventurers into the dungeon. They use sounds to locate monsters and
attack together with their owner. Using subtle scents that adventurers cannot
detect, they judge whether items are cursed and will never touch them if
they are. From the moment dogs enter the dungeon, they fight alongside the
adventurer. They are the most trusted and reliable companions. Please
cherish them.

Canine-type monsters basically attack with fangs and claws. It's just minor
injuries for small animals, but some monsters can deal significant damage.

Liquid Beast. Cockatrice. Dog·Dog-Type Monster

227

block-level machine translation prototype · page 229

cockatrice

gelatinous cube

Yuk /
Hilro

block-level machine translation prototype · page 230

Guard dogs are said to attack not only with their fangs but also with breath
that contains heat capable of melting iron, so you might be defeated quickly
with inadequate equipment.
Some monsters, in addition to wounding you with their fangs, may have the
monster itself possess you. When possessed, adventurers can no longer act
normally and fall into a dangerous state. In the dungeon, there are corpses
that cannot be identified as human or animal; these are almost certainly the
result of possession. Monsters that cause possession often have the bodies of
beastmen, so the adventurer training school textbook says that appropriate
countermeasures should be taken when encountering half-human, half-beast
monsters.
Note: You should not eat the corpse of a troll no matter how hungry you are.
It is said that a troll's magic does not disappear even after death.

Spherical Creature

floating eye, freezing sphere

Among the spherical creatures, only two can be found described in the
adventurer training school library at this time.
A floating eye is one of them. It is a strangely large eyeball. Some places call
it the "stare," due to its enormous size. Additionally, very rarely, specimens
with arms and legs have been found.
In Yendor's dungeon, there are not a few adventurers who have seen a scene
where eyeballs float about. They are much larger than human eyeballs,
about one size bigger than those of a giant, and roughly the same size as a
human head. The pupils are green, and sometimes they emit a strange glow.
There are no eyelids or anything similar; only a few tentacles remain, like the
traces of optic nerves at the back. It's unsettling, making one want to knock
them down.
However, experienced adventurers know that attacking recklessly can be
dangerous. They have seen many adventurers killed by mistakenly attacking
with swords, as they know where the eyeballs are located in Yendor and what
effects they have. Therefore, be cautious with eyeball attacks.
Dead eyeballs can apparently be eaten as well. Most adventurers discard
them out of disgust, but some have thrown them into their mouths out of
hunger. One of them succeeded as an adventurer. He was able to tell where
people were even when his eyes were closed, and correctly guessed what
they were thinking.

Spheres

229

block-level machine translation prototype · page 231

Another spherical creature seems to be just a white ball. There's less
information about it than the eyeball. It appears to be a rather dangerous
existence, and adventurers who fought it are suffering from severe frostbite.
One adventurer said 'explosion' just before dying, but the meaning of this is
unclear.
Cat family animals

kitten, housecat, large cat, jaguar, tiger

Cats, like dog-like animals, can become a strong ally for adventurers. Unlike
dogs, cats cannot be trained at a shelter. However, even on the surface, the
nature of cats is close to monsters, and they have the ability to instinctively
discern whether items are cursed. Cats brought from the surface will not
approach cursed items, so adventurers can judge what kind of item it is
based on the cat's attitude. Additionally, while it is said that cats are selfish
and do not fight alongside their owners, they never do so. Instead, if a cat
feels gratitude and affection towards its owner, it will fight monsters
alongside them.

Cats have their teeth and claws as weapons. More accurately, those are their only
weapons. While not particularly powerful as weapons, they know how to use them
well and can sometimes inflict significant wounds. This is something
inexperienced adventurers cannot afford to underestimate.

However, as experience increases, feline monsters are never formidable
opponents. The lack of descriptions of feline animals at the adventurer
training school indicates the threat level of feline monsters to adventurers.

Gremlin

gremlin

Subspecies of goblin. They are shorter than humans. Generally, it is said they
have the ability to fly, and their flying forms have been witnessed on the
ground. However, those in the dungeon of Yendor do not seem to have the
ability to fly. It is unclear whether they were modified by Yendor's magic or if
they simply do not feel like flying in the narrow dungeon.
Gremlins use teeth and claws as weapons. The teeth aren't much to worry
about, but the claws can leave minor injuries depending on how they scratch.
Sometimes, they can steal special abilities that adventurers have. One
adventurer reported that a power gained by eating another monster
disappeared after fighting a gremlin. There are many reports of other abilities
being stolen. Outside the dungeon, nice dishes and small items often
disappear if you're not careful.

230

MONSTERS—monsters in the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 232

This is a common story. However, I've never heard of a tale where a creature
steals the abilities of other beings. It's probably due to Yendor's magic,
granting new abilities.
Beneficial beasts tend to cooperate with humans on the surface, but they
often attack with hostility in the dungeon. Caution is necessary.
Dungeon Residents

hobbit, bugbear, dwarf, ~lord, ~king

Dwarves (dwarf) are inhabitants of the underground and become
troublesome creatures for adventurers.
Dwarves are a race slightly smaller than humans. They move slowly but have
exceptional strength. Their bodies are robust, and they live long lives,
typically reaching around 200 years. They excel at excavation and are known
for digging countless tunnels to form underground cities. According to
legend, it was their subordinates who created the dungeons of Yendor,
though this is uncertain. Regardless, it is a fact that they settle in dungeons
and attack adventurers through secret passages they did not know existed.

They are excellent warriors, attacking adventurers with swords, axes, and
hammers. Due to the well-developed muscles for combat, they can inflict
significant wounds if they manage to dodge properly a dwarf's attack. They
will be formidable enemies for adventurers who have little experience in the
dungeon. The training manuals at the adventurer's school state, "You are
considered a true adventurer if you can easily defeat them."
Dwarves form clans like gnomes, so there are clear classes and one must
follow the orders of superiors. Dwarven lords (dwarf lord) rank high in
dwarven society and oversee several clans. A dwarven king (dwarf king)
unites the clans and rules over them as their monarch. A dwarven king has
higher abilities than a dwarven lord, and even experienced adventurers may
struggle with him.

hobbit (hobbit) is a race akin to dwarves. They never grow taller than a
human's shoulder, with an average height below the waist. They have weak
strength and low attack capability.

cat family. goblin. underground dweller

231

block-level machine translation prototype · page 233

gremlin

jelly

tiger

tengu

dwarf

block-level machine translation prototype · page 234

i

Mysterious

manes, homunculus, lemure, imp, quasit, tengu

They are summoned by Yendor to make the dungeon more solid, or created
by Yendor itself. They appear from nowhere and settle in the dungeon, like
tenants. In that sense, they resemble vampires, but unlike vampires who live
together as a clan and maintain close contact, they act individually, usually
no more than two or three at a time, and rarely communicate with each
other.
Just note that homunculi (homunculus) are produced from Yendor's magic.
They are pseudo-humans born through magic.
Reports about them, not just homunculi, are scarce. There has never been an
official report on individual qualities, with adventurers' rumors being the sole
source of information. Indeed, adventurers' rumors are a compilation of
several tales and concern their lives, so they have some credibility. However,
rumors are ultimately just rumors, and the accurate details are unclear.
For example, there are monsters with abnormally high noses who attack
instantaneously while changing their attacks. Additionally, eating the corpse
of this monster gives the eater the ability to move instantaneously. This is
something that goes against common sense, and the reports about this
monster are quite suspicious. However, there are rare adventurers who
possess the ability to move instantaneously. Although they do not reveal how
they acquired this ability, it is clear that all of them gained it after
descending into the dungeon. It cannot be entirely dismissed as mere rumor.
Regardless, the exact truth is that we know nothing for certain. The only way
to know their true abilities is for the adventurer to try fighting them.

j

Jelly-like Creature

blue jelly, spotted jelly, ochre jelly

Creatures born from the magic of Yendor. While sizes vary, there are often
individuals larger than humans. They have shimmering bodies that sway, but
their shapes are not fixed. They lack eyes, noses, and mouths. It is unclear
whether they possess any senses at all. The origin is unknown, but it is said
that they were created by melting several adventurers' corpses into a goo
and fusing them together. It is unlikely they were created for cleaning the
dungeon.
Their attacks are purely retaliatory against the opponent's attacks. Adventurers' sword

Mysterious / Jellies

233

block-level machine translation prototype · page 235

When they are stabbed with spears, they detach parts of their bodies, which fly through the
air like knives. These detached body parts contain acid and cold, and can inflict significant
damage on adventurers if they pierce their bodies.

Individuals of some kinds may use their bodies for attacks. Reports say that
they engulf adventurers like a brown jelly-like body. Since monsters have
powerful digestive juices, even experienced adventurers may suffer fatal
injuries if they struggle.
While it may appear to adventurers that its movements are negligible, once
attacked, it proves quite formidable. The training manuals at the adventurer's
school also caution at the end of their descriptions to remain vigilant, as its true
nature remains unknown.

Goblin

kobold, large~, ~lord, ~shaman

Dwarf wearing shabby clothing. Light on their feet and can be found
anywhere in the dungeon. Cobolds are not inherently aggressive, but if you
insult them, they will show their anger and attack. The cobolds in the
dungeon are under the control of a wizard and seem to challenge humans to
battle as soon as they spot them.
Kobolds are weak enough to be defeated by those entering the dungeon for
the first time. However, there are slightly tougher individuals like kobold
lords and kobold shamans, as well as those who use magic.
According to adventurers' words, the magic of the kobold shaman seems to
be a tiny magic that hardly inflicts any wounds....

Leprechaun

leprechaun

Imps, who are mischievous and thought to be gnomes, are very fond of
money. Many adventurers have had all their money stolen by them.
The leprechaun moves quickly and also has the ability to teleport, so it is
difficult to catch no matter how much an adventurer chases it. If you want to
defeat one, the best approach is to attack with missiles or to become
invisible before attacking.
Replikorns are often asleep, so it might be better to leave them alone and
not wake them if possible.

234

MONSTERS—monsters that wander the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 236

Mimic

small mimic, large~, giant~

Monster that can transform into various items and doors, and their true form
is still not accurately known. Some say they are amorphous creatures, while
others claim they are scaled quadrupeds. They use their special abilities to
transform into inanimate objects and wait for foolish victims.
If you enter combat with a mimic, those who have gained much experience in
the dungeon can easily deal with mimics. However, if you are still new to the
dungeon, it might be wiser to flee. Even small mimics can be troublesome,
but facing large mimics (large mimic) or giant mimics (giant mimic) and
trying to escape from combat would be difficult.

Explorers who have just begun should prioritize distinguishing between items
and mimics at the shop.

Nymph

wood nymph, water~, mountain~

Elf maiden
Dungeons contain several types of nymphs, including mountain nymphs
(mountain nymph), water nymphs (water nymph), and wood nymphs (wood
nymph) who live in forests.
Nymphs use their extraordinary beauty as a weapon, and adventurers who
are tempted lose the will to fight and become at the mercy of the nymphs.
Although adventurers may lose the ability to fight, nymphs do not intend to
harm them. Instead, out of mischief or pity, nymphs sometimes steal a large
number of items from the enchanted adventurers.
Moreover, nymphs have teleportation abilities, so even if an adventurer
searches the dungeon after having their items stolen, they will move around
instantaneously in various places, laughing at them.
Some adventurers find a nymph more terrifying than fierce monsters. This is
because, while ordinary monsters can be dealt with using items, if deceived
by a nymph, one would have to explore the dungeon naked and weaponless.

cobold. leprechaun. mimic. nymph

235

block-level machine translation prototype · page 237

leprechaun

nymph

orc

piercer

mimic

block-level machine translation prototype · page 238

0

Goblins,
Oaks [OCR?
Oaks]

goblin, hob goblin, Uruk-hai, orc, hill~,
Mordor~, ~shaman, ~-captain

Goblins (goblin) and Hobgoblins (hobgoblin) are indeed of the same kind of
creature as their names suggest. They are evil-minded goblins, much smaller
than adult humans in stature. They prefer dark places and their dark red
eyes, accustomed to the darkness, excel brilliantly in the dungeons where
sunlight never falls.
When comparing goblins to hobgoblins, hobgoblins are slightly stronger and
tend to appear in groups. When you see a hobgoblin, it's wise to be cautious
and assume there may be other companions nearby.
Orcs are among the species-rich types of monsters inhabiting the dungeon.
Orcs' build is almost the same as humans', except that their faces are said to
be pig-like and extremely ugly. Orcs prefer darkness, and their glowing red
eyes, like those of goblins, can spot enemies in any darkness.

For adventurers, a single orc is not a formidable opponent, but they often
have to struggle when attacked in groups. Among them, the Uruk-hai, called
uruk-hai, are particularly dangerous, as they shoot arrows soaked with poison
at adventurers in groups.

pickup

rock piercer, iron piercer

Dungeons have ceiling-dwelling monsters known as piercers. They are
divided into two types: rock piercer (with a rocky exterior) and iron piercer
(with an iron-like exterior).
Pickers have degenerated over time in the dark dungeons, lacking eyes and
limbs, and consist of a body with a mouth lined with sharp fangs and an
appearance similar to layered armor. They use their powerful jaws and fangs
to drill holes in the ceiling and then wait with their heads downward for prey
to pass by.
Suddenly attacking from above are pisahs, which can be troublesome
opponents. They hide on the ceiling, have hard outer skins, and their attacks
with fangs are powerful. However, such pisahs also have weaknesses. Their
body structure is unsuitable for movement on the ground, making them slow
and cumbersome.
Goblins, Oakts, Pisahs

237

block-level machine translation prototype · page 239

If you engage them in combat, using hit-and-run tactics or projectile attacks
would be effective.

Giant Beast

rothe, mumakil, leocrotta, wumpus,
titanothere, baluchitherium

Ancient beasts that perished long ago and creatures from ancient legends
are revived in Yendor's dungeon by evil magic.
Their attacks come in the form of biting with sharp fangs or tearing with sharp
claws and horns, using their overwhelming strength to assault adventurers.
Despite their large size, they are not slow movers and can be formidable
monsters for adventurers.

Among the largest land mammals in history, Baluchitherium (baruk chiri), a
member of the ceratopsian family, is a huge creature with a body length of
over 5.5 meters. When faced with resurrected ancient creature
Baluchitherium, even the bravest adventurer would be at a loss as to how to
attack it.
They have weaknesses, however; their resistance to magic is low. For
example, you could put them to sleep with a sleep spell before attacking.

r

Rat Rock
Mole

sewer rat, giant rat, rabid rat, rat were,
rock mole

Races that have lived longer than humans—rats' adaptability to their natural
environment is exceptionally great. In a NetHack dungeon where a human
might die within half a day, rats demonstrate their remarkable reproductive
power and vitality, living there as if it were natural for them.
Most of the rats are not particularly dangerous and can be killed immediately
if they attack. However, it's wise to be cautious of rabid rats (rabid rat). They
carry a fearsome pathogen, and there is a risk of being poisoned if you
receive a wound from them.
There is another werewolf-type creature where humans transform into other
creatures, known as a ratwere, not a werewolf. They can transform from
human to rat, and if they feel danger, they can summon their fellow small
rats. However, the most terrifying aspect is that if a person is bitten by a
ratwere and develops a fever, the adventurer who was attacked will tragically
turn into a ratwere themselves.
Rock Mole (appearance) is literally that of a strange shape
238

MONSTERS—monsters that wander the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 240

It appears as if they are rocks. There are few objects that can hinder the movement of rock
moles that can pierce even hard rock, so they can move freely throughout the dungeon.

It is not easy to defeat a rock goblin with tough skin, but experienced adventurers
can take advantage of their sluggish movements and easily defeat them.

Spiders, Scorpions

cave spider, giant spider, scorpion

Representative spiders encountered in the dungeon are two types: one is the
Cave Spider (cave spider), and the other is the vicious Giant Spider (giant
spider).
Cave spider is a type of spider that hunts without making a nest and attacks
by simply biting the opponent with its fangs.
Giant spiders are larger than cave spiders, and their fangs contain terrible
venom. When injected with this venom, strength diminishes, and you
become an easy meal for them.
Giant spiders usually lie in wait at their nest for prey, with various items
placed in the center of the nest to lure humans. When adventurers try to
take these items, the giant spiders approach the foolish victims while
dripping poison.

Scorpion (scorpion) stingers contain venom, as is well known. The type of
venom varies, but like the poison of the giant spider, it has the effect of
lowering an enemy's strength. It is crucial to end combat with a scorpion as
quickly as possible to avoid attacks from its venom.

Diver

lurker above, trapper

Lurker Above is a creature that hangs from the ceiling of the dungeon,
waiting for adventurers to pass by. When an adventurer approaches, the
Lurker Above spreads its wing-like bat wings and silently drops vertically,
widening its huge and flexible mouth to swallow the adventurer whole.
Adventurers swallowed by the lurker must find a way to escape immediately.
Otherwise, they will be slowly dissolved by the lurker's powerful stomach
acid.

Giant beasts, rats, rock moles, spiders, scorpions, lurkers

239

block-level machine translation prototype · page 241

baluchitherium

trapper

block-level machine translation prototype · page 242

Monster called Trapper also tries to swallow and digest its enemy whole.
However, in the case of Trapper, the body hides underground and attacks by
sensing vibrations as prey approaches.
Luaker and Tracker are said to have been the same kind of creature originally, but they are
considered to be creatures that diverged due to environmental adaptation while living in the
dungeon.

Unicorn

white unicorn, gray~, black~

Unicorn
Unicorns, who despise dirty humans, rarely reveal themselves before
humans and never approach to contact them. However, unicorns trust pure
virgins and sometimes allow them to ride on their backs.
Their movement is as swift as a gust of wind, and ordinary humans cannot
catch them. Additionally, their rear hoof attacks can inflict near-fatal injuries
on inexperienced adventurers with a single strike.
Unicorns' heads have a horn (unicorn horn) that spirals and is sharp,
containing magic said to bring various good fortune to the bearer. One
adventurer reported that his blind eyes were cured, while another reported
that poison water turned into plain water.
In the dungeon, besides the well-known white unicorn, gray unicorns and
black unicorns also live. They are sacred beasts of Law, Neutrality, and
Chaos respectively. According to legend, it is a calamitous act for an
adventurer to kill a unicorn of the same alignment.

v

Cloud
Burst
Whirlwind

fog cloud, dust vortex, energy~, ice~,
steam~, fire~

Fog Cloud (fog cloud) is a monster that resembles fog and attempts to suffocate
enemies by surrounding them with fog. However, Fog Cloud's movement is slow,
and it can be easily defeated if the enemy is not severely injured.

Strange whirlwind monsters called Vortexes have several types due to their
nature.
First is the Dust Vortex, but it contains countless particles of dust, and if an
adventurer is drawn into his whirlwind, they will temporarily become blind.

Unicorn, mist cloud, whirlwind

241

block-level machine translation prototype · page 243

Energy vortices are whirlwinds that flash with lightning, and anyone caught in
the wind is attacked by lightning. Ice vortices have chunks of ice whirling in
the wind and attack with extremely low-temperature cold. Those enveloped
by steam vortices or fire vortices are attacked by superheated steam or
crimson flame respectively.

A adventurer wrapped in a vortex suffers deep wounds just from that, and
there is also the risk of valuable items being destroyed. Energy vortices often
destroy staves and rings with their electricity, ice vortices freeze potions with
their cold air, and steam and fire vortices ignite scrolls and magic books,
boiling potions and causing explosions.

If you encounter them, it's better to avoid combat if possible, or if you must fight,
hide any items of the type that their vortex can destroy before engaging in battle.

W

Worm

baby long worm, baby purple~, long~, purple~

Worms are giant ringed animals. They move through the dungeon by writhing
their massive bodies and attack any living creatures they encounter immediately.

In the dungeon, two types of worms have been identified.
A long worm (long worm) is one of them, with its copper-colored body
incredibly long. The nerve center of the long worm is in its head, so even if
adventurers cut its body into pieces, it will keep attacking as long as the
head is not crushed. Additionally, the teeth in the long worm's mouth are
sharp and sturdy, and they can be made into an excellent weapon if
processed.
The other one is called the Purple Worm, and its body is indeed an eerie
purple. While they are not as long as Long Worms, their attack power
surpasses that of Long Worms. When enemies are present, Purple Worms
attempt to swallow them whole and dissolve them inside their bodies using
their teeth.
Baby long worm and baby purple worm have also been confirmed, but it is
reported that they are not as dangerous as adult worms.

242

MONSTERS—monsters that wander the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 244

Bug

grid bug, xan

Grid Bug (grid bug) is an insect that feeds on electricity, taking little damage
but capable of attacking with electric shocks. It appears on lower levels and
is reported to be one of the easiest monsters for adventurers to defeat.

Zan (xan) is an insect with jaws that extend from its jaw, resembling large
scissors and sharp teeth. They can fly swiftly through the air by flapping their
wings, and their bodies are covered with a hard and thick exoskeleton, making it
difficult to inflict fatal injuries on them.

If you enter battle with Zan, it's better to use the power of items and float in
the air if possible. This is because they constantly try to slice human legs
with their fangs while flying low to show their weakness in the lower
abdomen to the enemy.
Yellow Light

yellow light

If you see a spherical object floating in the air in the dungeon's darkness and
emitting yellow light, it is definitely Yellow Light (yellow light).
Yellow Light is said to be a semi-living creature created by the magician
Yendor to torment adventurers. They wander through the dungeon day and
night, approaching adventurers who have come seeking treasures.
If you find a yellow light, attacking it with a sword is a very dangerous choice.
This is because the only way a yellow light attacks is by exploding and
damaging enemies. Moreover, when a yellow light explodes, it is
accompanied by intense flash. Therefore, adventurers will suffer severe
injuries and become blind. If other monsters discover them in this state, even
experienced adventurers may be easily defeated.

Handling yellow light, you can use thrown projectiles while transparent and at
a distance, or blind the monster and attack directly.
It is dangerous to become blind in a dangerous dungeon, so it is not
recommended to fight Yellow Light without any preparation.

Worm, Bug, Yellow Light

243

block-level machine translation prototype · page 245

iron golem

yellow light

worm

dust vortex

xan

block-level machine translation prototype · page 246

Beast

zruty

Troll in the plains at the foot of the Tatra Mountains.

Legends exist only of a savage humanoid resembling an ape, or a fearsome
beast that can fell a bull with a single blow and possesses terrifying fangs.

Based on those rumors and adventurers' testimonies, it seems certain that
the monster has sharp pointed claws and huge, razor-sharp fangs. Although
somewhat sluggish, it is said to move fairly quickly.

• Golem

straw golem, rope~, leather~, wood~,
flesh~, clay~, stone~, iron~

Golems are not beings born in nature, elf realms, or the demon world. They
are pseudo-lifeforms that lack souls and were spawned by some mysterious
magical force.
Magic-created golems are classified by their material. There are straw
golems made from straw, rope golems made from rope, leather golems
made from leather, wood golems made from wood, fresh golems made from
assembled corpses, clay golems formed from hardened clay, stone golems
constructed from stones, and iron golems made from iron.

Golems continue to obey the commands of the one who created them faithfully
and precisely. The effect of those commands lasts semi-permanently until the
golem's body is completely shattered or the caster changes the order.

Some golems also have special abilities. For example, rope golems entangle
enemies with the ropes of their bodies and prevent them from moving, while
stone golems blow poisonous mist from their mouths at opponents.
Golems' motionless attacks, which aim to protect themselves, are terrifyingly
powerful. If an adventurer thinks they cannot defeat them, fleeing can also
be a wise strategy.

Beast. Golem

245

block-level machine translation prototype · page 247

Tunic, Kraken

giant eel, electric eel, kraken

Deep in the dungeon dwells an eel. While ordinary eels used for food rarely
harm humans, eels that inhabit the dungeon can become extremely
dangerous and easily take the lives of adventurers.
A giant eel attacks adventurers with its dreadful fangs; an electric eel, in
addition to biting, sends an electric current through its enemies powerful
enough to kill a buffalo in a single blow. But the most terrifying aspect of the
eels' attacks is probably the way they wrap their thick, long bodies around
adventurers and drag them underwater.
Clayton (kraken) can be found in the coastal regions of Norway according to legend.

Claymore is far too enormous, and its full adult form is not well known.
According to legend, its back resembles a small island floating on waves, with
shells growing there, and sometimes even trees. It can extend tentacles or
something akin to horns longer than ship masts from its body, which can
reach out of the water.
Because the kraken is so huge, its movements are slow, so attacking with
quick movements is effective. However, if you are caught by a kraken's
tentacles, you may be dragged deep underwater, so extreme caution is
needed in battle. If an adventurer is pulled underwater, there is no way to
save them, because the water is the battlefield where krakens are strongest.

@

Human

humans, elves, were-creature, Medusa, The Wizard

Humanoid monsters reside in large numbers in the dungeon.

Various races such as werewolves and other shapeshifting creatures, elves,
and soldiers await adventurers at the bottom of the dungeon.
Among them, the terrifying monster Medusa also has a humanoid form. Each
strand of her hair is a poisonous snake, so even if you find an opening
against Medusa, the snakes will bite the adventurer.
Medusa's attack against enemies is simply to direct her glowing red eyes at
them. However, the effect is tremendous, and those who meet her evil gaze
turn into stone statues. Nevertheless, such a Medusa could be easily killed by
a certain shield from Greece.

246

MONSTERS—monsters in the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 248

Like when the mythic hero Perseus slew Medusa.
Another human-like individual should be mentioned. This is the great and evil
mad wizard who rules this dungeon, Wizard of Yendor.

It is unknown for what purpose he created the vast dungeon, and only the name
of magic-user Yendor is widely known in connection with the dreaded dungeon.

Regarding the attacks that magic-user Yendor uses, all are shrouded in
mystery. This is because no one has survived a battle with him....
&

Demon

water demon, incubus, succubus, and more ...

Most adventurers will not see most of the demons. While there are vicious
monsters like Water Demons and Incubi that appear in relatively shallow
levels, demons or evil beings that can be called demons live in the deeper
parts of the dungeon. It is just a rumor, but it is said that the demons live in a
deep chasm called the Inferno at the bottom of the dungeon.

Monster attacks are powerful, and they are known to use a variety of attacks
including magical ones.
If you encounter a powerful monster known as a demon, you will have to
choose one of the two paths, provided you do not possess great strength. In
other words, you must either flee using any means possible, or call upon the
name of the gods and ascend to heaven.

Ghost

ghost

People are said to become ghosts and wander the earth even after their bodies
turn to bones. The underground dungeons of Yendor also have ghosts, many of
which are the forms solidified from the resentment of adventurers who died
during their quests.

According to gathered information, ghosts often stay on their own corpses or
items. They move slowly but are hard to damage due to being ethereal, and
can also pass through walls and corridors, so inexperienced adventurers will
have trouble with them.
Just, the ghosts' attacks drain a little essence from adventurers when they touch
them, so it's unlikely to die suddenly in combat. If you want to avoid combat,

Eel, Kraken. Human-like. Demon. Ghost

247

block-level machine translation prototype · page 249

elves

succubus

kraken

block-level machine translation prototype · page 250

It might be best to use speed to escape from that level.
If you manage to defeat a ghost, what brings the most joy is the various
items collected by the adventurers who turned into ghosts. However, it's
worth considering before using these items due to their sheer happiness.
Most of the items in the ghost's belongings are cursed.
You must be careful so that the mummy does not turn into a mummy.

Eel, Kraken, Human-like, Demon, Ghost

249

block-level machine translation prototype · page 251

Preface by Author Sandras Fone

Informations

Few return alive from Yender's dungeon. Those who return with Yender's
amulet can be counted on one hand. It is said they became heroes, or even
more than heroes—though it is uncertain if they actually obtained Yender's
amulet.

However, while the Amulet of Yendor could not be obtained, searches deep
into the dungeon were conducted, and some returned. Dioles is one of them.
When he emerged from Yendor's dungeon, his hair, which had been light
chestnut, had turned white, and his previously robust body had shriveled like
that of an old man.
I asked him many questions about Yendor's dungeon. However, that dungeon
had infected both his body and his mind. Dioles' answers were fragmentary
and vague, sometimes seeming to make no sense at all. He would suddenly
answer questions from two hours ago out of the blue, or simply mutter to
himself.

I was able to compile this information, combining his story with rumors that
have been circulating, as a piece of information for those who venture into
Yendor's dungeon. However, I cannot determine whether the information
recorded here is correct or incorrect.

Travel to Yendor's dungeon and verify the truth of this information yourself.

Dedicated to the beloved mad warrior——.

250

INFORMATION—To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 252

Meeting, Diolès the man
He lived near the slums, beneath a bridge. His home was a shack that barely
deserved to be called a house. A warrior of his caliber should have been given
a luxurious mansion. However, perhaps his spirit desired to live in this place.

This is the dwelling of a man named Diolès that I am visiting from here.
He was a famous Barbarian several years ago. Proud of his bulk, he boasted
that he could cut everything with the greatsword he wielded.
And in order to show off his great strength to all, he challenged the dungeon of
Yendor recklessly.
Though he could not obtain Yendor's magic repellent, Diolès returned from the
dungeon alive.
With many treasures as well.

Originally, he should have been awarded hero status just for that fact.

However, Dioles is now at the farthest distance from such glory.

When he emerged from that dungeon, the muscles that had been filled to the
point of cutting steel were miserably withered.
The once-brown hair, which had been envied by many daughters, had become
tattered white hair.
Its eyes lost their glow, colored with fear. The words that fell from its lips made
no sense.
What terror struck him, I, who am not a god, have no means of knowing.

But, one thing was certain.
Dioles's spirit had shattered in the dungeon of Yendor. Since then, he had been
living in this hut, cutting off contact with people and surviving in the darkness.
Occasionally, he would venture into town, selling the treasures obtained from
the dungeon of Yendor for a pittance.
Meeting, Diolès the man

251

block-level machine translation prototype · page 253

It seemed to be used for expenses.

If one knew his former appearance, they would be astonished by his difference
now. Dioles is akin to a living dead person at present.
However, his descent into the depths of Yendor's dungeon is an undisputable
fact. The knowledge he gained will likely be of great significance to my
research.

And then, I stood in front of his house's door.
When I opened the door -- although I omitted the knock because it seemed like the door
was about to collapse from the knock alone -- a pungent odor wafted in, making my eyes
water.

As light seeped through the doorway, two eyes glinted deep in the darkness,
meeting my gaze.
Immediately, he moved into the corner of the room where a faint shadow
remained, avoiding the light.
And he threw objects at me indiscriminately, uttering inarticulate growls as he
desperately tried to flee into the darkness, which gave me a slight sense of
despair. Diolès might no longer be able to engage in social interactions as a
human.

Thus our first meeting ended half in despair. After I left, he continued to throw
things as if he hated light itself, scattering them around his dwelling.

First Meeting
However, I did not give up and visited his house several more times afterward.
Of course, I brought some gifts each time.
At first, Diolus was afraid of me, but he gradually opened up to me. When his
mental state was healthy, we would even exchange some conversations.
He particularly enjoyed alcohol. Drunkenness might be erasing the fear from
his heart that was shattered by terror.
That day he brought the usual Dwarf's Wine. He was unusually
252

INFORMATION—To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 254

Started talking eloquently about the dungeon of Yendor.
The dungeon of Yendor cannot be described as terrifying. No, it was an
amazing experience... but...
When he talked about the dungeon, he would either become completely silent
or extremely talkative. This time, because he was extremely talkative, I was
able to obtain some important information.
First, I inquired about weapons inscribed with the legend of sleeping in
Yendor's dungeon. Tales of legendary swords forged in ancient times still thrill
our hearts at any time.
Dioles diligently searches for words to answer my question, scratching his
white hair. He drinks more alcohol in an attempt to find the right words, but his
reply is somewhat unclear.
After a few profanities against the gods leaked out, he finally began speaking,
squeezing the words from the depths of his heart.

First Meeting

253

block-level machine translation prototype · page 255

"Long sword... If you think your skill is worthy of praise, try immersing the long
sword in the spring. If the lady in the spring recognizes your skill, she will lend
you great power."

"Spring...immerse the cursed item in the spring... Yes, the power of the spring
water can wash away the curse. However, there is considerable danger
involved. By immersing it in the spring, you can also turn scrolls into blank
paper. You can drink the spring water... if you are not afraid of nymphs or
water demons..."
After repeating several words that seemed meaningless, his eyes wandered
into the void, as if recalling something from the depths of his heart.

Suddenly light appeared in his eyes. It was as if something had taken
possession of him. No, this might be his true form.
The slack jaw tightened, and the eyes glowed with radiance, unlike the Diores
who had been there just moments ago. Had the expression of the former
warrior returned?
Occasionally, you might find them tucked away in weapon shops or general
stores. Probably sold by an adventurer in dire need of money. While they were
quite valuable, I didn't have enough gold to purchase them.
Here he took a break and drank the beer greedily, continuing to speak.
"Asked about weapons inscribed with names? If you truly desire a weapon
inscribed with a name, try continually offering sacrifices to the temple of the
god you hold in faith. There may be divine favor."
I was seized by the illusion that another Diolus was opening its mouth. Words
flowed like a muddy river without hesitation, each one filled with dignity and
grace. Perhaps the Diolus that was once revered as a warrior has returned. It
was hard to tell if the man before me was the same one I first met.

Next, I asked if there is a temple in Yendor's dungeon. While I had conjectured
from some information that a temple might exist, I had no idea why it would be
there. Now, Dioleres

254

INFORMATION——To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 256

The dungeon has a temple in it.
"Found. But it's more of a altar than a temple. There aren't any temples with
priests, but you'll need to go deeper. After all, obtaining Yendor's amulet is a
religious ceremony."
Getting an amulet is a religious ceremony? What does that mean? However,
Dioles continued talking without giving me a chance to ask a question.
Praying at the surface of the dungeon is the most important thing. By gaining the favor
of the gods, you can navigate through various difficulties that occur in the dungeon.

I asked about the method to make the gods my allies. If I could make the gods
my allies, even that dungeon would become somewhat easier to navigate.

First Meeting

255

block-level machine translation prototype · page 257

"Offer sacrifices at the altar of the god to whom you belong. Keep offering the
corpses of the monsters you defeat. The larger and more powerful the
sacrifice, the greater the reward. If four-leaf clovers begin to fall, it means the
offering has pleased the god. By doing this, you can gain divine favor. Yes, you
may even speak with the god, and it seems that a famous blade may be
bestowed by the god. I received no such favor, though."

It seems that in Yendor's dungeon, the power of the gods increases compared
to our world. Only a handful of priests can speak with the gods on the surface,
and even they cannot receive gifts from the gods.

When he was unable to find the altar of the god he worships, the question
arose as to what he did.
"Basically, it doesn't matter which god you choose. First, find the altar. Just
offer a sacrifice to it. Of course, to the god you worship. If your worshipped
god's power is strong, the altar will transform."

256

INFORMATION——To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 258

I want to show you the light when it happens. When that black light enveloped
the altar, it was truly breathtaking....
In the Yendorian dungeon, even struggles among the gods become intense. It
is said that the distortion of time and space occurs within that dungeon due to
Yendor's magical power. Such things might be related to his words.

...and then, while I was letting my thoughts drift, Diolès let out some
unbelievable words.
"Sometimes it might be interesting to offer a human to the temple."
whispered as if uttering his last words, and madness gleamed in his eyes. It
was clear that he had offered human corpses to the temple. It was a trance-like
expression.
and began spouting out words that seemed to have no meaning.
"Swinging bridge... moat... floating there. If the gods don't teach us the
melody, that's all we've got..."
"become xorn... just become xorn..."
I realized that further questions would be futile. Thus, the first day of our
conversation ended with Dioles.

The next day his mental state was also good——or it appeared to be good.

Sometimes he would shout meaningless words, but he could be considered to
have maintained a state of relative stability. During their conversation about
the blessings and curses of the gods, he let slip these words.
"Among cursed items, there were times when placing something on the altar
would result in a black flash or an amber flash... Do you know what these
flashes mean? A black flash indicates a curse, while an amber flash indicates a
blessing. With this knowledge, you can navigate Yendor's dungeon safely."

Determine if the item obtained is cursed or not
First Meeting

257

block-level machine translation prototype · page 259

This can be considered the key point in advancing through the demon's
dungeon. This was made clear through my previous research. There have
likely been many adventurers who lost their lives by improperly equipping
items without knowing the strong curse that is upon them.

"Simply put, putting water on the altar is meaningless. Water will be blessed or
cursed by the power of the altar's god. If you place water on Morlognon's altar,
it will become cursed water. Conversely, the altars of the gods of discipline will
have the opposite effect."

Furthermore, he also taught me how to make holy water. There is a rumor that
you can remove a curse by soaking an item in holy water.
These two stories can be considered quite significant achievements. This day
was sufficient with just two answers. And the following day, when I visited his
house——.
"Recalled by a cursed item..."
He suddenly started answering my question from the previous day.

When you go to the dungeon, pets you bring along are not only friends but also
useful tools. Can you understand that? Dogs and cats can judge whether
something is cursed or not. It's probably because they can detect a smell
emitted by cursed items. They won't walk on top of cursed items. Priests might
just be sniffing for that smell instead of using holy power.

Dioles wore a vulgar smile. My neck prickled unpleasantly, and a chill ran down
my spine.
Here he drank the liquor down in one gulp. The intoxication loosened his
tongue and seemed, temporarily at least, to re-form his shattered mind.
Next I asked him about scrolls. We discussed several things, but what was
interesting was the following testimony.
"In the dungeon, I read a scroll of punishment three times and was punished
with manacles and a ball and chain. I either read a scroll of remove curse or
cast the remove curse spell to escape..."

258

INFORMATION——To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 260

I thought he was unlikely to be versed in magic, but I refrained from saying so.
Interjecting any objections here seemed like it could spoil his mood—and
perhaps even his memory.
Dioles took a breath and tried to pour wine into the cup. However, only a few
drops dripped from the bottle. He frowned and, in an attempt to catch the last
bit of wine at the bottom, turned the cup upside down.
"But once, when I didn't have a scroll of remove curse, I transformed into a
monster that ate iron... I noticed that monsters like the last monster and iron
golem really loved iron. I thought that if I turned into an iron golem, I could eat
iron."

First Meeting

259

block-level machine translation prototype · page 261

Dioles continued to speak, licking his lips. Perhaps he was remembering the
taste of the iron ball he ate when he turned into an Iron Golem.
There are also methods of calling such monsters with magic or scrolls and
offering them iron balls. In that dungeon, there should always be multiple ways
to escape even when pushed to a desperate situation. Use your head. Those
who are not smart cannot survive in that dungeon...

Dioles suddenly slumped his shoulders. He must have hit upon an unwelcome
memory. He let out a long, deep sigh.
"Cannot survive..."
Dioles repeated those words several times. I sensed the limit of his mental
power and ended our conversation for the day.

Consideration of Cursed Items
A few days later, he continued his story as if pouring water on a flat board. It
seems Dioles has changed completely from the man he was at their first
meeting.
However, understanding which question those words answered required
considerable effort. For example, as in the following words---.
If you pick up the cursed scroll of genocide, enter the small room and close all
the doors. Then, sheathe your weapon and remove all your armor. Read the
scroll of genocide to kill the nurse (nurse). However, the cursed scroll of
genocide will likely summon many nurses. Simply wait for time to pass; they
will heal you...

Dioles scratched his hair while holding his head. His eyes had lost all light.

"Remember to remove your weapons and armor. If the armor is cursed, read
the scroll to destroy it. The armor will be reduced to dust along with the curse."

He was actually talking about effective ways to use items that don't seem useful.

260

INFORMATION——To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 262

It seems. He could read, surprisingly, according to my notes. He had cited an
example of how using it in an unconventional way would be beneficial.

No, he probably just said it because he recalled his experience. That's why
there didn't seem to be any will in his tone...
At times like this, a small trigger can cause Dioles to slip back into being a
half-crazed one. However, even in such circumstances, he cannot forget about
the dungeon.
What he saw in the dungeon, I cannot know. But whatever he beheld with his
eyes, it must have been something that traps one in such a terrible memory.
As a researcher of the dungeon, it may be an object of interest, but I do not
think I would want to experience such things myself.

Dungeon Food Situation

After about a month had passed since I first visited his home, Dioles' mental
condition was still relatively good. By this time, he would even greet me upon
my arrival. It seems that his mind is being reconstructed through our
conversations.
This day, I asked Diores about the food situation in the dungeon. While some
do bring in food, carrying all the food required for the days needed to traverse
a dungeon extending to dozens of floors in Yendor is not feasible.

Dungeon Food Situation

261

block-level machine translation prototype · page 263
