Almost impossible.
However, this doubt was quickly resolved by Diolès's answer.
Many monsters in the dungeon don't seem to know they can be eaten. There
are also grocery stores, and food drops all over the place. You can get plenty
of nutrition and even become full sometimes. Sure, you're often hungry, but
it's not that bad. Just don't expect great taste. Have you ever eaten goblin or
oakum meat? You probably wouldn't want to, but that's the gist of it.

If the food doesn't bother me, the food situation in Yendor's dungeon isn't that
bad. However, I still don't want to eat goblin or orc meat.

Furthermore, Diolus provided some valuable information about the taste of
monster meat.
Special abilities of monsters can be obtained by eating their flesh... After
eating ants with fire resistance, I no longer felt heat from fire. Such things do
happen. However, there are many poisonous ones, and some will make you
lose your senses immediately upon consumption. Once eaten, you won't
forget, but being attacked by other monsters right after eating such things was
the worst case scenario...

This story was also something I had heard about. It's about creatures like
leprechauns or nymphs who possess dimensional transcendence powers, and
those who eat their meat gain such powers. However, it was difficult to
determine whether this story was just a legend because it often ended with the
person being unable to control the power and dying by getting stuck inside a
wall.

According to his words, obtaining such abilities is never guaranteed to happen
and is greatly influenced by luck. Additionally, the stronger the monster you
defeat, the more likely you are to gain the ability.
This section covers Dioles's discussion of many monsters' characteristics. For
details on these features, please refer to my book, The Book of Dungeon
Exploration, Volume on Biology. Much of his information was also referenced
here.
262

INFORMATION——To conquer the dungeon

block-level machine translation prototype · page 264

.
According to the story, Diolus defeated even dragons. There was nothing to
prove his claim, but it is probably true. He might have fought a young dragon.

Final Hour·His Death
A considerable amount of time had passed since I first visited his house.

That day, I had almost finished writing this book and went to Diolès to have it
checked. However, what I saw at his house was his corpse.
He was sorting through some treasures—likely acquired in Yendor's dungeon—and
had died of exhaustion on the bed. Beside him was a letter for me.

According to the letter, Diolus was aware of the abnormality in his mind.
Furthermore, he knew that his life was running out, so it seems he tried to
convey as much information as possible to me while his mind was still clear.

I hope that by recording various information I experienced in the dungeon
through writing, the number of those who fall into madness like him will be
reduced somewhat.
Therefore, this book, which was supposed to circulate among a few people as
part of my research, ended up being published.
To reiterate, Diolus was once a strong warrior. Such madness exists in the
dungeon that can turn even a mighty man into this form. Those who cannot
fight against this madness should not enter the treasure-laden dungeons of
Yendor, regardless of their motives.

This trial may require a greater sacrifice than your life. I hope you understand
the will of Diores.

Final Hour·His Death

263

block-level machine translation prototype · page 265

[OCR? Islamic religion]

block-level machine translation prototype · page 266

Command List

Action Commands
items with a '^' require pressing the specified letter key while holding down
the CTRL key.
Commands marked with "#" require you to first enter the "#" key followed by
the specified letter.

Commands Related to Movement

Go up stairs to upper level (when on stairs)
Go down stairs to lower level (when on stairs)
Rest
Close door
Open door
Teleport (if teleportation ability is available)
Jump to other location

• command to check your inventory
Table
iShow all items
lShow items of specified type
xShow list of known spells ('+' works same)
)Show equipped weapon
[Show equipped armor
=Show worn rings
"Show worn amulets
"Show used tools
$Count gold coins held
¥Show what kinds of objects have been found
^Inspect types of shops

Action Commands

265

block-level machine translation prototype · page 267

• Commands to Operate Inventory

Remove all armor worn
d Put items down
D Put some items down
P Wear amulet, ring
r Read scroll or spell book
R Remove amulet or ring
T Remove any piece of armor equipped
w Pick up weapon. Use 'w-' to wield nothing and be unarmed
W Equip armor
, Pickup item from floor
# Name held item

• Command to use an item
use an item
eat food (or a monster's corpse)
drink a potion of water
throw an object. or shoot an arrow with a bow
zap a wand. if it's a magic wand, its effect will occur
#d immerse something in something else

Other Actions
Kick something (usually a door)
Stamp a message on the floor
Pay for items bought at the shop
Search for secret doors or traps (usually requires several searches)
Cast a spell
Show what is at your feet
Talk to something next to you
Pick lock

266

List of Commands——Command List

block-level machine translation prototype · page 268

Table
#l rob what is in the box below or in the backpack
#m use a monster's special ability
#o sacrifice an offering on an altar to the god
#p pray to the god for help
#r rub a lamp to summon an entity
#s sit down at the spot
#t (if available) drive away an undying monster
#u disarm a trap
#w wipe one's face

■ Player Commands
Open Help Menu
/ Display the description of symbols shown
& Display the description of command actions
^a Repeat the last command executed
C Name individual monsters
D Set options
^p Redisplay the previous message (continuing will display messages in order)
Q Quit the game
^r Redraw the screen if it is messed up
S Save and quit the game. Next time, you can continue from where you saved
V Show version number of NETHACK
V Show history of NETHACK
#- Show compilation options when NETHACK was compiled
X Enter discovery mode (invincibility mode). Once entered, cannot return to normal
state.
@ Toggle pickup option. Changes by pressing
! Exit to DOS shell. To return to the game, type 'exit'

Player Commands

267

block-level machine translation prototype · page 269

To my surprise, such a book has been created (laughs).
Thinking back, the start of this was when I began searching for interesting free
software games to write about for the Game Collection 30 that came out at the
beginning of last year. All the games covered in this book were excellent, but
NetHack particularly matched my feelings, to the point where the first time I
played it, an entire day had passed before I realized it.

Due to file size limitations, we had to give up including NetHack in Game
Collection 30, but upon seeing the returned survey postcards, there were
relatively more voices saying they wanted to play NetHack.
Therefore, simply introducing NetHack in book size and putting it on a disk
would be boring and lacking in creativity. Is it not possible to make it more
interesting by including various experimental elements... After thinking
through this, this book was created (laughs).

A somewhat unusual and challenging writing experience occurred where one
co-author stopped communicating for a while, and when we commissioned
illustrations, the results were far from what we expected. It took up to a year
from project initiation to completion due to these issues, but it was quite
interesting. Perhaps it would be enjoyable to continue such work.

If you could send your thoughts and feedback as usual, it would be greatly
appreciated. Your voices as readers truly become a great strength.
How will I appear before you this time? Enjoy (surely part 3 of collection 30? I'm
not that obedient a person.)

268

Afterword

block-level machine translation prototype · page 270

Thought otherwise, that's a big mistake). For now, I'll conclude here.

April 1, 1993 -- Ryojiro Hasukawa

Postscript
This book's attached disk contains the version of NetHack 3.0.
However, at the very last moment of writing this book, it was announced that
NetHack 3.1 had been released on the other side of the sea! Moreover, the
porting to PC-9801 was relatively easy, and it has already been released.
Distribution through various BBS systems is going smoothly.
Changes seem to include——an increase in items——and other things.

But the authors have not yet grasped the full scope of NetHack 3.1. And the
gameplay (well, sort of).

And, that's the way it is.

For this recording, please forgive me using NetHack 3.0 (I kept saying "it's
coming, it's coming" but it never did. Sigh).

Afterword

269

block-level machine translation prototype · page 271

block-level machine translation prototype · page 272

● Please send your comments and opinions about this book and the game to our
service center in writing. However, please note that we may not be able to answer
questions regarding the content due to the nature of the game.
● If you believe there is a malfunction, please write in detail including your name,
address, the model of your computer, how you operated the game, and what happened
before and after the issue occurred. Please also include a return envelope and send it
to our service center in writing. We may not be able to respond to inquiries via
telephone.
● The disk attached to this book does not contain the source code of NetHack due to
capacity limitations. If you would like to receive the source code, please fill out the
necessary information on the reader card provided and return it.

block-level machine translation prototype · page 274
